Title: Data Analyst
Description:
SteerBridge Strategies is a modern technology company delivering innovative, mission‑focused solutions to the U.S. Government and private sector. Leveraging deep expertise in federal acquisition, digital transformation, and emerging technologies, we deliver agile, commercial‑grade capabilities that accelerate operational effectiveness and drive measurable mission success.

At the core of SteerBridge is our people—especially the veterans whose leadership, problem‑solving mindset, and commitment to excellence elevate every project we support. We don’t simply hire exceptional talent; we cultivate it, creating meaningful career pathways for veterans, military spouses, and professionals who share our passion for advancing technology and strengthening the missions we serve.

Position Overview

SteerBridge Strategies is seeking a highly skilled Data Analyst to support mission-critical operations in a secure, controlled data environment. This role focuses on advanced data analysis, automation, and reporting to drive informed decision-making at leadership levels. The ideal candidate will have strong SQL expertise, experience working with large datasets, and the ability to translate complex data into actionable insights.

Key Responsibilities


Data Analysis & Insights
Analyze large, relational datasets to identify trends, inefficiencies, and performance gaps
Translate raw data into actionable insights to support leadership decision-making
Identify degraders, shortages, and operational inefficiencies
SQL Development
Write clean, efficient, and production-ready SQL (Amazon Redshift, Oracle SQL Developer)
Utilize advanced SQL techniques (CTEs, window functions, complex joins, aggregations)
Optimize queries for performance and scalability
Troubleshoot and resolve data discrepancies across multiple data sources
Data Quality & Validation
Implement automated validation checks (row counts, duplicates, completeness, integrity)
Ensure accuracy and consistency of data before reporting
Identify and resolve root causes of data inconsistencies
Automation & ETL Processes
Support and maintain ETL pipelines and data workflows
Build automated processes for data ingestion, transformation, and validation
Implement scheduling/orchestration tools (cron, AWS services, Airflow, or equivalent)
Ensure processes run without manual intervention
Scripting & Process Automation
Develop scripts using Python, Bash, or PowerShell
Automate data pulls, file processing, and pipeline validation
Integrate scripts into end-to-end workflows
Reporting & Dashboards
Develop and maintain automated reports and dashboards
Support daily, weekly, and monthly reporting requirements
Ensure reports are accurate, consistent, and delivered on schedule



Required Qualifications

Education & Experience


U.S. Citizenship required with the ability to obtain a Secret Clearance
Bachelor’s degree in a technical field (Data Analytics, Information Technology, Computer Science, or related)
Minimum 3+ years of professional experience in data analysis or a related field
Strong experience working in sensitive or controlled data environments
Advanced SQL proficiency (Amazon Redshift, Oracle preferred)
Experience with large, relational datasets and data structures
Strong understanding of joins, aggregations, and query optimization
Experience troubleshooting data discrepancies and ensuring data integrity
Familiarity with ETL processes and data pipelines
Experience building recurring reports and dashboards
Strong attention to detail and data validation skills



Interpersonal & Professional Skills


Strong communication skills with the ability to translate technical concepts for non-technical and operational stakeholders
Ability to develop relationships with collaborators, program providers, community partners, and military personnel
Able to successfully prioritize and manage multiple critical projects simultaneously with a high degree of accuracy
Experience working on applied data projects involving diverse organizations to collect, analyze, and interpret data



Preferred Qualifications


3-6 years of post-college professional experience
Experience with predictive analytics or modeling
Proficiency in Python or R for data analysis
Experience with automation tools and scheduling frameworks
Experience supporting executive-level decision-making



Core Competencies

Analytical Thinking

Collaboration & Teamwork

Attention to Data Quality

Problem Solving

Ownership & Accountability

Continuous Learning

Benefits


Health insurance
Dental insurance
Vision insurance
Life Insurance
401(k) Retirement Plan with matching
Paid Time Off
Paid Federal Holidays



SteerBridge Strategies is proud to be an Equal Opportunity Employer. We are committed to creating a diverse and inclusive workplace where all qualified applicants and employees are treated with respect and dignity—regardless of race, color, gender, age, religion, national origin, ancestry, disability, veteran status, genetic information, sexual orientation, or any other characteristic protected by law.

We also provide reasonable accommodations for individuals with disabilities in accordance with applicable laws. If you require assistance during the application process, we encourage you to reach out so we can support your needs.

If you would like information about how your application is processed, please contact us.

Title: Housing Systems, Data, and Automation Specialist, UM Housing
Company: The University of Montana - College of Business
Description:
Description

UM Housing invites applications for a Housing Systems, Data, and Automation Specialist to serve as the primary technical and analytical authority for Housing Integrated Systems, with a central focus on StarRez and related enterprise technologies.

The complexity of work ranges from routine system maintenance to highly complex system design and integration efforts. The position is expected to anticipate needs, resolve issues independently, and continuously improve systems and processes while aligning with departmental and institutional goals.

Examples of Duties and Responsibilities


Design and manage data architecture, integrations, imports, mapping strategies, student-facing portal experiences, and configuration consistency across housing systems and functional areas
Collaborate with campus partners, communications teams, and StarRez consultants to support system-driven communications, custom tools, advanced modules, and an optimized student housing experience
Develop, maintain, and optimize reporting, analytics, and executive dashboards across StarRez, Banner, SQL/StarQL, Tableau, and Microsoft tools to support strategic planning, operational decision-making, performance measurement, and housing metrics
Establish reporting standards, train and support staff in data utilization, and ensure accurate completion of institutional and industry reporting requirements while helping leadership effectively interpret and apply data insights
Lead and support integrations between housing systems, Banner, and campus platforms by collaborating with IT and stakeholders to ensure reliable data exchanges and effective manual processes where integrations are unavailable
Leverage AI, APIs, scripting, and Microsoft technologies to drive automation, improve operational efficiency, and enhance the student residential experience through thoughtful system design
Provide advanced administration, troubleshooting, training, and optimization of StarRez and related housing systems while driving system, data, and process improvements through automation, analytics, and industry best practices


Minimum Qualifications


Bachelor’s degree or equivalent combination of education and relevant experience
Two (2) or more years of experience supporting enterprise systems, data management, reporting, or business process and technology operations
Proficiency with relational databases and query development (e.g., SQL), including data analysis and reporting
Experience configuring, maintaining, and supporting enterprise platforms such as CRM or student information systems
Ability to translate business requirements into technical solutions, workflows, system configurations, and actionable reporting dashboards
Strong troubleshooting, analytical, and problem-solving skills, with the ability to support users across varying technical skill levels
Excellent project management, communication, and collaboration skills, with the ability to manage multiple priorities while working independently and across cross-functional teams


Preferred Qualifications


Bachelor’s degree in Information Systems, Data Analytics, Computer Science, Business, or a related field, with experience in higher education, student housing, or similar operational environments
Expertise administering enterprise systems (e.g., StarRez, CRM, student information systems), including system implementation, optimization, and process improvement initiatives
Strong technical skills in relational databases, SQL/StarQL querying, system integrations, APIs, data exchanges, automation tools, and the Microsoft Power Platform

Title: Data Analyst I
Description:
SteerBridge Strategies is a modern technology company delivering innovative, mission‑focused solutions to the U.S. Government and private sector. Leveraging deep expertise in federal acquisition, digital transformation, and emerging technologies, we deliver agile, commercial‑grade capabilities that accelerate operational effectiveness and drive measurable mission success.

At the core of SteerBridge is our people—especially the veterans whose leadership, problem‑solving mindset, and commitment to excellence elevate every project we support. We don’t simply hire exceptional talent; we cultivate it, creating meaningful career pathways for veterans, military spouses, and professionals who share our passion for advancing technology and strengthening the missions we serve.




Position Overview

SteerBridge Strategies is seeking a highly skilled Data Analyst to support mission-critical operations in a secure, controlled data environment. This role focuses on advanced data analysis, automation, and reporting to drive informed decision-making at leadership levels. The ideal candidate will have strong SQL expertise, experience working with large datasets, and the ability to translate complex data into actionable insights.

Key Responsibilities

Data Analysis & Insights
Analyze large, relational datasets to identify trends, inefficiencies, and performance gaps
Translate raw data into actionable insights to support leadership decision-making
Identify degraders, shortages, and operational inefficiencies




SQL Development
Write clean, efficient, and production-ready SQL (Amazon Redshift, Oracle SQL Developer)
Utilize advanced SQL techniques (CTEs, window functions, complex joins, aggregations)
Optimize queries for performance and scalability
Troubleshoot and resolve data discrepancies across multiple data sources




Data Quality & Validation
Implement automated validation checks (row counts, duplicates, completeness, integrity)
Ensure accuracy and consistency of data before reporting
Identify and resolve root causes of data inconsistencies




Automation & ETL Processes
Support and maintain ETL pipelines and data workflows
Build automated processes for data ingestion, transformation, and validation
Implement scheduling/orchestration tools (cron, AWS services, Airflow, or equivalent)
Ensure processes run without manual intervention




Scripting & Process Automation
Develop scripts using Python, Bash, or PowerShell
Automate data pulls, file processing, and pipeline validation
Integrate scripts into end-to-end workflows




Reporting & Dashboards
Develop and maintain automated reports and dashboards
Support daily, weekly, and monthly reporting requirements
Ensure reports are accurate, consistent, and delivered on schedule




Required Qualifications

Education & Experience

U.S. Citizenship required with the ability to obtain a Secret Clearance
Bachelor’s degree in a technical field (Data Analytics, Information Technology, Computer Science, or related)
Minimum 3+ years of professional experience in data analysis or a related field
Strong experience working in sensitive or controlled data environments
Advanced SQL proficiency (Amazon Redshift, Oracle preferred)
Experience with large, relational datasets and data structures
Strong understanding of joins, aggregations, and query optimization
Experience troubleshooting data discrepancies and ensuring data integrity
Familiarity with ETL processes and data pipelines
Experience building recurring reports and dashboards
Strong attention to detail and data validation skills




Interpersonal & Professional Skills

Strong communication skills with the ability to translate technical concepts for non-technical and operational stakeholders.
Ability to develop relationships with collaborators, program providers, community partners, and military personnel.
Able to successfully prioritize and manage multiple critical projects simultaneously with a high degree of accuracy.
Experience working on applied data projects involving diverse organizations to collect, analyze, and interpret data.




Preferred Qualifications

3–6 years of post-college professional experience
Experience with predictive analytics or modeling
Proficiency in Python or R for data analysis
Experience with automation tools and scheduling frameworks
Experience supporting executive-level decision-making




Core Competencies

Analytical Thinking

Collaboration & Teamwork

Attention to Data Quality

Problem Solving

Ownership & Accountability

Continuous Learning

Benefits

Health insurance
Dental insurance
Vision insurance
Life Insurance
401(k) Retirement Plan with matching
Paid Time Off
Paid Federal Holidays




$81,000 - $95,000 a year




SteerBridge Strategies is proud to be an Equal Opportunity Employer. We are committed to creating a diverse and inclusive workplace where all qualified applicants and employees are treated with respect and dignity—regardless of race, color, gender, age, religion, national origin, ancestry, disability, veteran status, genetic information, sexual orientation, or any other characteristic protected by law.




We also provide reasonable accommodations for individuals with disabilities in accordance with applicable laws. If you require assistance during the application process, we encourage you to reach out so we can support your needs.




If you would like information about how your application is processed, please contact us.

Title: Product Information Analyst
Description:
Your Opportunity at ARC’TERYX:




As a Product Information Analyst, you are responsible for ensuring product data is accurate, complete, and operationally ready across the Merchandising Lifecyle. You enable scalable data management and decision making by maintaining high-quality product information and translating business inputs into structured, system-ready data. This role is a critical connector across Design, Product Development, Merchandising and Supply Chain, ensuring product data flows seamlessly from concept to launch. You are responsible for inputting the data and, as you evolve the role, leveraging the data to generate reporting and insights that inform the assortment and enable better business decisions.     




This role is based out of our Portland office and is expected to be performed on location.




 




Meet Your Future Team:




The Process Operations organization sits at the heart of Arc’teryx’s end-to-end value chain, leading enterprise-wide alignment and advancing a culture of radical collaboration. The Product Data & Analytics team, a new function within Process Operations, is the central authority and gatekeeper for seasonal product data. We are responsible for transforming data management from a necessary task to an enterprise capability, resulting in a portfolio of data products that enable better decisions from concept to guest. We partner with Technology, Product Creation, Merchandising and Master Data to evolve our governance model, modernize tooling, and embed KPI-led performance management into our end-to-end value chain. You will be at the forefront of these efforts, helping to shape the vision, operating model and execution of our initiatives.   







If you were in the Product Information Analyst role now, here are some of the core activities you would be doing:



Participate in key go-to-market milestones between Design, Product Development, and Merchandising.


Create, maintain, and validate Model and SKU-level data in Merchandising and PLM systems.


Ensure completeness and accuracy of product attributes across the full lifecycle - from raw materials to finished goods.


Execute and audit seasonal data loads and updates in alignment with key Design, Development, Merchandising, and Planning milestones.


Create and enforce data standards, naming conventions, and attribute definitions.


Identify, communicate, and resolve data inconsistencies, gaps, and errors.


Partner with cross-functional teams to improve data integrity and reduce rework.


Collaborate with Product Line Managers, Regional Merchants, Product Developers and Business Planners to capture and maintain accurate product information.


Surface and resolve anomalies and edge cases related to attribution, hierarchy, and key measures.


Translate your category’s business processes, seasonal strategy, and product inputs into structured system data.


Act as a subject matter expert on product data and business processes by championing issues and opportunities between Product Creation, Operations, and Technology teams.


Ensure market readiness by ensuring product data is complete and system ready.


Monitor deadlines and ensure timely delivery of all data milestones.


Identify opportunities to streamline workflows and improve efficiency.


Contribute to documentation, training, and continuous improvement initiatives.


Support implementation of data governance frameworks and tooling enhancements. 








Here are some of the things you could be working on in the future:



Act as a data steward for your assigned category, owning the quality, structure, and usability of product data.


Brief in requirements for enhancing our analytics data platform to serve use cases for reporting, self-service tools, pre-configured dashboards, and modern query interfaces.


Research, identify, and translate offline product attribution to systems-ready definitions and partner with Technology teams to enable and scale these where appropriate.


Contribute to the redesign of Merchandising hierarchies, attributes, and taxonomies to improve data quality, searchability and analytics usefulness across ERP systems.


Use tools such as SQL, Python, Tableau, Power BI (DAX) and Excel to develop and test analytics solutions in a sandbox environment.


Partner with Technology to push the most useful solutions to production and champion tool adoption amongst your user base through business-friendly documentation and ongoing training and education.  


Develop and present Merchandising analytics education modules in partnership with your Process Operations functional leaders.    








Are you our next Product Information Analyst?



Bachelor’s degree or equivalent experience in Business, Information Systems, or a related field.


2-5+ years of experience in product data management, Merchandising Operations, or a related function.


Familiarity or direct experience with managing business operations in Direct-to-Consumer Retail, Wholesale or E-com.


General knowledge of Merchandising, Buying or Open to Buy processes.


Proven ability to synthesize and interpret data, spot trends, draw conclusions, develop recommendations, and influence action.


Comfort with ambiguous, fast-growth, entrepreneurial environments.


Strong intellectual curiously and a continues improvement mindset.


Proactive identification of inefficiencies and a demonstrated ability to design scalable, sustainable solutions. 


Effective communication across technical and non-technical stakeholders.


Thrives in ambiguous environments and brings structure to complexity.


Exceptional problem-solving skills with a strong attention to detail.


Deep expertise in Excel required.


Experience working with a PLM or a home-grown Merchandising system preferred.


Experience working with ERP platforms (e.g., SAP) and adjacent systems such as supply planning (e.g., o9), and product information management systems (e.g., Akeneo) preferred.


Experience using SQL, Python, Tableau, Power BI (DAX), and other analytics query tools preferred.


Experience using Miro, Confluence, Smartsheet, and other project management tools preferred.








Compensation: The salary range for this role is USD $71,000 to USD $88,000.



















Total Rewards




Most new team members join between the start and midpoint of the range, where you can contribute right away while continuing to grow your craft, your collaborations, and your impact at Arc’teryx. This range reflects market alignment and the scope of the role. Individual pay is determined by your skills, experience, and level of responsibility. 










We’re committed to fair, equitable, and competitive pay, reviewed regularly to ensure internal alignment and market relevance. Transparency is part of how we follow through on our commitments and how we honour the value you bring to our team. 










In addition to base pay, Arc’teryx supports people through benefits designed to sustain both work and life: 












Health & wellbeing - Extended health, dental, and vision coverage, including mental health support, fertility benefits, and a 24/7 Employee Assistance Program (EAP).


Financial wellbeing - Immediately vested 401(k) retirement savings plan matching, eligibility for Arc'teryx Annual Incentive Plan and access to Employee Stock Purchase program (ESPP) where applicable.


Time & Flexibility - Paid time off and No Wasted Day program (dedicated paid days to get outside & explore).


Family support - Parental leave for birthing and on-birthing parents, including an option to ease the transition back to work.


Growth, community & gear - Professional development opportunities, Arc'teryx Academies (outdoor skill-building events), Employee Belonging Councils, and access to employee discounts and Pro Deals (exclusive discounted pricing on gear). 











 *Eligibility for certain programs may vary by role. 













Equal Opportunity




 




Arc’teryx is committed to actively creating and fostering a culture of inclusivity where voices are heard, people are seen, and values are respected. We care about the uniqueness of our applicants, employees, and guests, and we do so in a safe space fueled by curiosity and acceptance. 




 




Creating an inclusive workplace is connected to our core value Do Right while also having the added benefit of helping make our business better. We believe inclusion helps us to create a healthier, happier workplace, drive creativity and innovation, and reflect the communities where we operate.  




 




All applicants, employees, and guests can expect equality of opportunity and fair treatment in alignment with our values.




 




Leave it Better




 




We believe that the mountains transform us, that how we adventure matters, and that there’s always a better way. 




Join us in creating positive change in ourselves, our communities, and the world. 




 




Live it. Get out there - the mountains make us better 




Disruptive evolution. In pursuit of better. Always. 




Commit. We set bold objectives and see them through.

  • Time & Flexibility - Paid time off and No Wasted Day program (dedicated paid days to get outside & explore).

  • Family support - Parental leave for birthing and on-birthing parents, including an option to ease the transition back to work.

  • Growth, community & gear - Professional development opportunities, Arc'teryx Academies (outdoor skill-building events), Employee Belonging Councils, and access to employee discounts and Pro Deals (exclusive discounted pricing on gear). 




 *Eligibility for certain programs may vary by role. 





Equal Opportunity


 


Arc’teryx is committed to actively creating and fostering a culture of inclusivity where voices are heard, people are seen, and values are respected. We care about the uniqueness of our applicants, employees, and guests, and we do so in a safe space fueled by curiosity and acceptance. 


 


Creating an inclusive workplace is connected to our core value Do Right while also having the added benefit of helping make our business better. We believe inclusion helps us to create a healthier, happier workplace, drive creativity and innovation, and reflect the communities where we operate.  


 


All applicants, employees, and guests can expect equality of opportunity and fair treatment in alignment with our values.


 


Leave it Better


 


We believe that the mountains transform us, that how we adventure matters, and that there’s always a better way. 


Join us in creating positive change in ourselves, our communities, and the world. 


 


Live it. Get out there - the mountains make us better 


Disruptive evolution. In pursuit of better. Always. 


Commit. We set bold objectives and see them through.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
We’re searching for a Data Analyst. Are you searching for new possibilities?

Westland Insurance Group Ltd is growing and has an exciting opportunity for an Data Analyst to join our growing team. This hybrid role is based out of our Head Office in Surrey, BC.

Expected Compensation: The expected hiring range for this role is $62,400- $78,000 with a 37.5-hour work week. Your rate of pay will be determined based on your individual qualifications and experience, while considering internal equity. 

Westland also offers a competitive annual short-term incentive program that enables employees to share in the rewards of the business and emphasizes the measures that are key to our success.

Westland is committed to continuous learning where employees can grow their potential and write the stories of their careers. Financial assistance is available for approved work-related training/development courses leading to certification, and programs that offer professional development, including seminars and conferences.

Discover What’s Possible, With Westland.

The Westland story is all about growth, and that means plenty of possibility for everyone on our team. Every day, Westland proves that building a great business means taking care of clients, communities, and each other with equal commitment. As we continue to open new doors, we're inviting amazing people like you to join us.

As a Data Analyst, you’ll have the unique opportunity to lay the foundation for how Westland can leverage data to make important decisions.

Interested in learning more?

Why You'll Love Westland


An open, flexible and welcoming workplace
Plenty of opportunities to grow and learn
Autonomy to drive your own success
Be part of our inclusive culture, alongside an extraordinarily talented community of people with a wide variety of backgrounds
A total rewards program that takes care of your financial, physical, and mental health
Flexible paid Values Days to celebrate days important to you
Paid Volunteer Days for employees as part of Amplifying Communities, a program to support causes that matter most to our employees and clients


Why We’ll Love You


You strive to understand the data and love seeking ways to improve the quality of the data and reporting
You enjoy collaborating with others to identify and prioritize use cases for analysis and build out models to answer business questions
You love numbers and have a strong background in math, statistics or equivalent such as a bachelor’s degree in those areas or related fields
You can translate complex information and findings for business leaders
At least two years’ experience in a data analytics role
Previous experience with data analysis and modeling using SQL, R, and Python
Bonus: Experience in P&C insurance
Bonus: Experience using data platforms such as Databricks, Snowflake or Microsoft Fabric Bonus: Experience with Git based version control on platforms such as GitHub or Azure DevOps


Once Here, You'll


Be a contributing member of our inclusive culture, alongside an extraordinarily talented community of people with a wide variety of backgrounds.
Working with IT and others to design and improve the quality of our data and reporting by understanding current and future data analysis needs, business opportunities and risks.
Continuously seek out and identify areas to improve data reporting practices, data quality, and processes
Prepare and present findings to diverse audiences, including partners


Want to get to know each other better? Send your resume our way.

You belong here

We strive to be much more than a great place to work. Here, you join a collective of incredibly vibrant, purpose driven, and open-minded people. Every day, Westland proves that building a great business means taking care of communities, clients, and each other with equal commitment. You will be supported and respected for who you are and for the voice you add to the conversation.

At Westland, you will feel the power of community. Westland Insurance Ltd. is an inclusive organization that values diversity in its workforce. We listen, champion equity and diversity, and create safe welcoming spaces where everyone has a voice and is free to be themselves. Westland Insurance Ltd. encourages applications from all qualified individuals and will accommodate applicants' disability-related needs, up to the point of undue hardship, throughout all stages of the recruitment and selection process.

If you require a disability-related accommodation in order to participate in the recruitment process, please contact the recruitment team by email at careers@westlandinsurance.ca

Title: Product Data Analyst
Description:
Job Description

First Hawaiian Bank is currently seeking a Product Data Analyst to join the Deposit & Lending Services Department.

Work Schedule

Monday to Friday 8:00 AM to 5:00 PM (hours may vary)

Compensation

The pay range for this role is $46,000 - $100,000/yr; commensurate with experience.

The successful candidate will be hired for the level of the position that aligns with their skill level and experience.

Responsibilities

Serve as portfolio analyst for the Deposit & Lending Services Department. Performs detailed analytics to determine trend, formulate recommendations and identify opportunities to enhance profitability, reduce costs, increase market share or improve operational efficiency. Develops reports and dashboards to assist with tracking key performance metrics. This position will assist the department with the development, marketing and promotion of deposit and loan products and services.

Qualifications

The ideal candidate will have:

Minimum Qualifications (Product Data Analyst II Level)


3 years experience in banking, marketing, financial sales, data analytics and/or related field required


Minimum Qualifications (Product Data Analyst III Level)


5 years experience in banking, marketing, financial sales, data analytics and/or related field required


Requirements For All Candidates


Bachelor’s Degree in Business, Finance, Economics or Marketing or related field required
1 years’ experience in banking, marketing, financial sales, data analytics and/or related field required
Experience with advanced Excel tools including pivot tables, macros and complex charting required
Previous experience with databases (Oracle, MS SQLServer, MS Access), business intelligence software, extracting, interpreting and analyzing data
Exposure to the R modeling environment, scripting and automation experience a plus
Knowledge or experience in finance or banking a plus
Excellent oral, written and interpersonal communication skills are essential for interacting with all levels of staff and management and external vendors
Demonstrated analytical, planning and problem-solving skills essential.
Ability to write and execute SQL queries for data retrieval.
Possesses high attention to detail with excellent follow-through
Must be able to work independently with minimal supervision
Able to prioritize responsibilities and work under pressure with multiple deadlines
Must be able to handle confidential matters judiciously
Good time management skills
Proficient in Microsoft Office suite of products

Title: Business Data Analyst
Description:
Turner Supply Company is hiring for a Business Data Analyst in Mobile, AL!

About Turner Supply Company

Founded in 1905, Turner Supply Company is a family-owned industrial distributor serving manufacturers nationwide. Customers rely on Turner for a comprehensive product assortment, competitive pricing, expert problem-solving, and cost-reduction programs that improve operational performance.

Our leadership team actively champions our core values—Empowerment, Development, Execution, Teamwork, Innovation, and Leadership—values that helped Affiliated Distributors recognize Turner Supply as a Top Workplace in 2022 ⭐.

📍 Headquartered in Mobile, Alabama, Turner operates 8 locations across the United States.

Total Rewards

Turner offers a competitive pay and benefits package, including:

·        Medical, Dental, and Vision coverage

·        Wellness Program

·        Company-paid Life Insurance

·        Company-funded Health Reimbursement Arrangement (HRA)

401(k) with a generous company match




Job Title:                        Business Data Analyst

Department:                  Information Technology

FLSA Status:                  Salaried Exempt            

Direct Report:               VP, Technology & Procurement

Supervisory

Responsibility:             None

Location:                      Mobile, AL

                                      Onsite M-F (7:30am – 4:30am CT)




Job Summary:

The Business Data Analyst will be responsible for collecting, processing, and analyzing data to provide valuable insights and support strategic decision-making within the organization. They will work with large datasets, employ statistical methods, and utilize data visualization tools to communicate findings effectively. They will analyze and interpret business data including sales, e-commerce, procurement and operations information to increase sales/profitability. The role requires a keen analytical mindset, attention to detail, and the ability to collaborate with cross-functional teams.

Responsibilities:

Data Collection and Processing:
Gather and collect data from various sources within the wholesale distribution system
Usage of APIs to extract, transform, and load data from external sources
Apply API integration tools and platforms to streamline data retrieval and updates
Clean, preprocess, and organize data to ensure accuracy and completeness
Data Analysis:
Perform statistical analysis on sales, e-commerce, inventory, and operational data to identify trends and patterns
Generate reports and dashboards to communicate key insights to stakeholders
Utilize data visualization tools to present findings in a clear and understandable manner.
System Administration:
Perform standard system administration duties for several key systems (ERP, PIM applications, eCommerce applications & websites)
Create and maintain product data exports as needed for web site and other uses.




 

Support Forecasting and Predictive Modeling:
Review & develop models to forecast sales, demand, and inventory levels throughout Turner Supply Company
Support implementing predictive analytics to optimize inventory management and procurement processes
Performance Metrics:
Report and monitor key performance indicators (KPIs) that evaluate business performance
Collaborate with relevant teams to identify areas for improvement and efficiency
Business Strategy Support:
Work closely with management to understand business objectives and provide data-driven recommendations
Contribute to strategic planning by offering insights based on data analysis
Data Quality Assurance:
Implement measures to ensure data integrity and accuracy
Conduct regular audits of data sources and processes
Collaboration:
Collaborate with cross-functional teams, including sales, operations, and finance, to gather relevant data and insights
Communicate effectively with non-technical stakeholders to convey findings and recommendations




Required Qualifications:

Bachelor's degree in a related field (e.g., Data Science, Business Analytics) preferred
3+ years relevant data analysis experience
3+ years relevant experience using ERP applications and data
Proficiency in data analysis tools such as Excel, Python, R, SQL, and data visualization tools (e.g., Power BI)
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills
Ability to work independently and collaborate effectively in a team

·        Ability to learn and apply various software tools/systems used at Turner Supply Company, such as AD EnterWorks PIM, Optimizely B2B Web Platform, INFOR SX.e/CSD, Google Merchant, Amazon Seller Central, Acenda Marketplace Manager, epaCUBE

·        Self-starter with motivation to work with minimal direction and supervision

·        Excellent written, communication and presentation skills

·        Fluency in English (reading, writing and speaking)




Preferred Skills:

Prior experience with Distributor and/or Manufacturing organizations
Experience with advanced statistical analysis and machine learning
Knowledge of supply chain and logistics
Familiarity with e-commerce applications & ERP (Enterprise Resource Planning) systems such as: Infor SX.e, Infor CloudSuite Distribution, Optimizely, Acenda, AD EnterWorks
Experience with SOAP & RESTful APIs and their application in extracting relevant business data

Supervisory Responsibility: This position does not have direct report responsibility.




Work Environment: This job operates in a professional office environment as well as warehouse and outside sales. This role routinely uses standard office equipment such as computers, phones, photocopiers, filing cabinets, and fax machines. 

 

Physical Demands: The physical demands described here are representative of those that must be met by an employee to successfully perform the essential functions of this job. The job has a low amount of physical demands – sitting at a desk, filing, and using office equipment. This job could require being able to lift 20 lbs.




Travel: This position requires less than 10% travel.




Other Duties: Please note this job description is not designed to cover or contain a comprehensive listing of activities, duties, or responsibilities that are required of the employee for this job. Duties, responsibilities, and activities may change at any time with or without notice.







Turner Supply Company is an Equal Opportunity Employer. Turner Supply Company does not discriminate based on race, religion, color, sex, gender identity, sexual orientation, age, non-disqualifying physical or mental disability, national origin, veteran status or any other basis covered by appropriate law. All employment is decided based on qualifications, merit, and business need.

Title: Business Intelligence Analyst
Description:
Department: IT Group

Location: Clackamas

Description

At Pacific Seafood, we do more than just provide the world with the healthiest proteins on the planet. We are a family-owned, excellence-driven organization committed to being the brand of choice in the marketplace and the employer of choice in the community. We believe in servant leadership, investing in our team members’ personal and professional growth, and rewarding performance. We live by the core values of our Diamond Philosophy: Teamwork, Productivity, Quality, and Excellence—which means consistently doing your best and always striving to do better.

Summary

The Business Intelligence (BI) Analyst at Pacific Seafood plays a key role on our IT/Data team, helping transform raw data into actionable insights that drive operational and strategic decisions. This position focuses on building and maintaining reporting solutions, dashboards, and data models that provide visibility into business performance. It is ideal for someone who is technically skilled, detail-oriented, and passionate about using data to improve processes and outcomes.

Key Responsibilities2. Data Warehousing & System Support:3. Data Management & Governance:4. Business Collaboration & Analytics Support:5. Duties:Additional responsibilities may be assigned as deemed necessary to support the overall goals and objectives of the position.


Application Support & Development:
Design, develop, and maintain BI reports, dashboards, and data visualizations using Power BI.
Translate business requirements into technical data models and reporting solutions.
Ensure accuracy, consistency, and performance of reporting solutions.
Support testing, validation, and deployment of BI solutions in coordination with development and business teams.
Collaborate with developers and analysts on large-scale data and reporting initiatives
Support and maintain data warehouse environments and data pipelines to ensure data availability and reliability.
Assist in data integration efforts from multiple sources into centralized BI platforms.
Perform data validation, cleansing, and transformation to ensure data quality and integrity.
Support data migrations, data refresh processes, and backup procedures as required.
Document data models, data definitions, ETL processes, and reporting standards.
Ensure compliance with data governance, security, and privacy standards.
Work closely with business stakeholders to understand reporting and analytics requirements.
Provide insights and recommendations based on data analysis to support decision-making.
Deliver ad hoc analysis and reports for various business units.
Participate in design reviews and contribute to continuous improvement of BI processes and tools.
Provide technical guidance and support to team members on BI tools and best practices.
Be a role model in projecting and applying The Pacific Group Diamond Philosophy (Productivity, Quality, Exigence, Teamwork) and promote teamwork at all items
Responsible for analysis, design, development, testing, implementation, and documentation of BI and analytics solutions.
Develop integrations between ERP systems and BI/reporting platforms.
Perform analysis needed to support the multiple areas of the business in their day-to-day use of the application
Manage multiple project priorities to successfully deliver solutions to our business owners within defined timelines
Troubleshoot, identify and fix application bugs
Provide information by answering questions and requests
Maintain quality service by establishing and enforcing organization standards
Maintain professional and technical knowledge
Contribute to team efforts by accomplishing related results as needed
Support the systems afterhours as part of the on-call rotation
Follow IT change management process
Ability to collaborate with the Solutions Architect on projects that span multiple technologies and systems


Required

What you bring to Pacific Seafood


Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Information Technology, or a related field.
1–3 years of experience with Power BI.


Preferred:


Understand basic data modeling concepts (e.g., star schema)
Experience with scripting languages such as Python or R.
Experience creating digital forms (e.g., GoFormz, Microsoft Forms, etc.)


Physical Requirements

The physical demands described here are representative of those that must be met by a team member to successfully perform the essential functions of this job. While performing the duties of this job, the team member:


Sedentary work, walking and standing are required only occasionally.
Reaching. Extending hand(s) and arm(s) in any direction.
Fingering. Picking, pinching, typing or otherwise working, primarily with fingers rather than with the whole hand as in handling.
Talking. Expressing or exchanging ideas by means of the spoken word. Those activities in which they must convey detailed or important spoken instructions to other workers accurately, loudly, or quickly.
Hearing. Perceiving the nature of sounds at normal speaking levels with or without correction. Ability to receive detailed information through oral communication, and to make the discriminations in sound.
Close visual acuity to perform an activity such as: preparing and analyzing data and figures; transcribing; viewing a computer terminal; extensive reading.
Repetitive motion. Substantial movements (motions) of the wrists, hands, and/or fingers.
Occasionally lift and/or move up to 25 pounds.


Working Conditions:

Duties

The working environment characteristics described here are representative of those a team member encounters while performing the essential functions of this job. While performing the duties of this job, the team member is exposed to:


Office, as well as warehouse environment.
Moderate noise level in the work environment.
Plant exposure to wet, cold, and/or humid temperatures.
Standard office equipment and heavy machinery.


Benefits

Total compensation:

At Pacific Seafood Your Base Wage Is Only a Portion Of Your Overall Compensation Package. We Invest In Our Team Members Through a Comprehensive And Attractive Total Rewards Package, Including But Not Limited To


Health insurance benefits options, including medical, prescription, vision, dental, basic group life and short term disability.
Flexible spending accounts for health flex and dependent care expenses
401(k) Retirement Plan options with generous annual company profit sharing match
Paid time off for all regular FT team members, to include sick days, holiday pay, vacation, and personal time
Employee Assistance Program- Confidential professional counseling, financial, and legal assistance provided at no charge to Team Members and immediate family members
Product purchase program


Pacific Seafood is an equal opportunity employer and all qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, national origin, disability status, protected veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by law.

Key Responsibilities2. Data Warehousing & System Support:3. Data Management & Governance:4. Business Collaboration & Analytics Support:5. Duties:Additional responsibilities may be assigned as deemed necessary to support the overall goals and objectives of the position.

  • Application Support & Development:
  • Design, develop, and maintain BI reports, dashboards, and data visualizations using Power BI.
  • Translate business requirements into technical data models and reporting solutions.
  • Ensure accuracy, consistency, and performance of reporting solutions.
  • Support testing, validation, and deployment of BI solutions in coordination with development and business teams.
  • Collaborate with developers and analysts on large-scale data and reporting initiatives
  • Support and maintain data warehouse environments and data pipelines to ensure data availability and reliability.
  • Assist in data integration efforts from multiple sources into centralized BI platforms.
  • Perform data validation, cleansing, and transformation to ensure data quality and integrity.
  • Support data migrations, data refresh processes, and backup procedures as required.
  • Document data models, data definitions, ETL processes, and reporting standards.
  • Ensure compliance with data governance, security, and privacy standards.
  • Work closely with business stakeholders to understand reporting and analytics requirements.
  • Provide insights and recommendations based on data analysis to support decision-making.
  • Deliver ad hoc analysis and reports for various business units.
  • Participate in design reviews and contribute to continuous improvement of BI processes and tools.
  • Provide technical guidance and support to team members on BI tools and best practices.
  • Be a role model in projecting and applying The Pacific Group Diamond Philosophy (Productivity, Quality, Exigence, Teamwork) and promote teamwork at all items
  • Responsible for analysis, design, development, testing, implementation, and documentation of BI and analytics solutions.
  • Develop integrations between ERP systems and BI/reporting platforms.
  • Perform analysis needed to support the multiple areas of the business in their day-to-day use of the application
  • Manage multiple project priorities to successfully deliver solutions to our business owners within defined timelines
  • Troubleshoot, identify and fix application bugs
  • Provide information by answering questions and requests
  • Maintain quality service by establishing and enforcing organization standards
  • Maintain professional and technical knowledge
  • Contribute to team efforts by accomplishing related results as needed
  • Support the systems afterhours as part of the on-call rotation
  • Follow IT change management process
  • Ability to collaborate with the Solutions Architect on projects that span multiple technologies and systems

Required

What you bring to Pacific Seafood

  • Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Information Technology, or a related field.
  • 1–3 years of experience with Power BI.

Preferred:

  • Understand basic data modeling concepts (e.g., star schema)
  • Experience with scripting languages such as Python or R.
  • Experience creating digital forms (e.g., GoFormz, Microsoft Forms, etc.)

Physical Requirements

The physical demands described here are representative of those that must be met by a team member to successfully perform the essential functions of this job. While performing the duties of this job, the team member:

  • Sedentary work, walking and standing are required only occasionally.
  • Reaching. Extending hand(s) and arm(s) in any direction.
  • Fingering. Picking, pinching, typing or otherwise working, primarily with fingers rather than with the whole hand as in handling.
  • Talking. Expressing or exchanging ideas by means of the spoken word. Those activities in which they must convey detailed or important spoken instructions to other workers accurately, loudly, or quickly.
  • Hearing. Perceiving the nature of sounds at normal speaking levels with or without correction. Ability to receive detailed information through oral communication, and to make the discriminations in sound.
  • Close visual acuity to perform an activity such as: preparing and analyzing data and figures; transcribing; viewing a computer terminal; extensive reading.
  • Repetitive motion. Substantial movements (motions) of the wrists, hands, and/or fingers.
  • Occasionally lift and/or move up to 25 pounds.

Working Conditions:

Duties

The working environment characteristics described here are representative of those a team member encounters while performing the essential functions of this job. While performing the duties of this job, the team member is exposed to:

  • Office, as well as warehouse environment.
  • Moderate noise level in the work environment.
  • Plant exposure to wet, cold, and/or humid temperatures.
  • Standard office equipment and heavy machinery.

Benefits

Total compensation:

At Pacific Seafood Your Base Wage Is Only a Portion Of Your Overall Compensation Package. We Invest In Our Team Members Through a Comprehensive And Attractive Total Rewards Package, Including But Not Limited To

  • Health insurance benefits options, including medical, prescription, vision, dental, basic group life and short term disability.
  • Flexible spending accounts for health flex and dependent care expenses
  • 401(k) Retirement Plan options with generous annual company profit sharing match
  • Paid time off for all regular FT team members, to include sick days, holiday pay, vacation, and personal time
  • Employee Assistance Program- Confidential professional counseling, financial, and legal assistance provided at no charge to Team Members and immediate family members
  • Product purchase program

Pacific Seafood is an equal opportunity employer and all qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, national origin, disability status, protected veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by law.

Title: Product Analyst / Data Analyst
Description:
Your Job

Flint Hills Resources is seeking a Product Analyst/Data Analyst to join our team in Wichita, KS. As an IT Product Analyst/Data Analyst, you will serve as a highly capable contributor who helps ensure strong delivery execution and continuity within the product team. You will play a critical role in translating product direction into high-quality delivery outcomes and in extending the team's impact across the broader technology ecosystem. This role is well-suited for someone ready to take accountability for delivery outcomes, influence how work gets done, and contribute meaningfully to business and technology objectives.

What You Will Do


Work closely with the business to understand problems at their root and drive solutions through technology — whether through your own development work or by collaborating across the business, software engineering, data architecture and solutions architecture.
Develop and maintain scripts, agents, and lightweight applications using tools like Python to automate processes, move data, and solve business problems.
Understand data models, system integrations, and application behavior to effectively translate business needs into sound technical approaches.
Analyze data, build reports and dashboards, and surface insights that inform product and business decisions — including understanding how application data and APIs translate into graph and analytical data models.
Own projects end-to-end — from problem statement through delivery and adoption — maintaining accountability for outcomes throughout.
Contribute to roadmap development, collaborating closely with product and business leadership to identify new features, enhancements, and incremental improvements aligned with product direction.
Continuously innovate and push the team forward — challenging the status quo, identifying opportunities to transform how work gets done, and raising the bar for those around you.



Who You Are (Basic Qualifications)


Experience in IT, data, software engineering, or a business role with demonstrated technical aptitude
A passion for or experience writing scripts, queries, or code (e.g., Python, JavaScript, SQL, AI agents, or equivalent) to solve problems, automate processes, or move and validate data
Experience working directly with business stakeholders to understand problems and translate them into technical solutions
Experience communicating technical concepts clearly to both technical and nontechnical audiences
This role is not eligible for visa sponsorship



What Will Put You Ahead


Experience with Cypher, graph databases, or data modeling
Experience with data engineering or API integrations — building pipelines, moving data, or connecting systems
Experience with cloud technologies, specifically AWS (e.g., SQS, CloudWatch, Lambda)
Experience with AI agents, LLMs, or prompt engineering
Experience with Power BI, Microsoft Fabric, data modeling, or analytics — including building reports, dashboards, or data products that inform business decisions
Experience in procurement, accounts payable, source-to-pay, or operations



At Koch companies, we are entrepreneurs. This means we openly challenge the status quo, find new ways to create value and get rewarded for our individual contributions. Any compensation range provided for a role is an estimate determined by available market data. The actual amount may be higher or lower than the range provided considering each candidate's knowledge, skills, abilities, and geographic location. If you have questions, please speak to your recruiter about the flexibility and detail of our compensation philosophy.

Hiring Philosophy

All Koch companies value diversity of thought, perspectives, aptitudes, experiences, and backgrounds. We are Military Ready and Second Chance employers. Learn more about our hiring philosophy here.

Who We Are

As a Koch company, Flint Hills Resources is an industry-leading refining company making the oil and transportation products that keep the world moving. We make a large supply of fuel for gas stations and airports in addition to transporting crude oil, natural gas liquids and refined products through 4,000+ miles of pipeline – delivering energy to farmers, manufacturers and families. Our team champions innovation and excellence to help power modern life.

At Koch, employees are empowered to do what they do best to make life better. Learn how our business philosophy helps employees unleash their potential while creating value for themselves and the company.

Our Benefits

Our goal is for each employee, and their families, to live fulfilling and healthy lives. We provide essential resources and support to build and maintain physical, financial, and emotional strength - focusing on overall wellbeing so you can focus on what matters most. Our benefits plan includes - medical, dental, vision, flexible spending and health savings accounts, life insurance, ADD, disability, retirement, paid vacation/time off, educational assistance, and may also include infertility assistance, paid parental leave and adoption assistance. Specific eligibility criteria is set by the applicable Summary Plan Description, policy or guideline and benefits may vary by geographic region. If you have questions on what benefits apply to you, please speak to your recruiter.

Additionally, everyone has individual work and personal needs. We seek to enable the best work environment that helps you and the business work together to produce superior results.

Equal Opportunities

Equal Opportunity Employer, including disability and protected veteran status. Except where prohibited by state law, some offers of employment are conditioned upon successfully passing a drug test. This employer uses E-Verify. Please click here for additional information. (For Illinois E-Verify information click here, aquí, or tu).

Title: BI Suite Analyst
Description:
MANTECH seeks a mission-driven and detail-oriented BI Suite Analyst to join our team in Oahu, Hawaii.

Responsibilities Include But Are Not Limited To


Develops and maintains interactive dashboards and reports that track key performance indicators and resource allocation.
Draft technical documentation for data processing workflows and standard operating procedures for BI tool usage.
Maintain data repositories and ensure the accuracy of information updated by various project teams.
Drafts updates for the Monthly Status Report (MSR) utilizing automated data feeds from the management portal.
Develop workflows for the review and approval of purchase requests and travel authorizations within the portal.
Assist in converting mission-partner data into desired formats for dissemination across the theater.
Takes direction, guidance, and mentoring from more senior staff.


Minimum Qualifications


BA/BS in Data Science, Information Technology, or a related field and 3+ years of position-relevant experience.
Demonstrate the ability to draft technical reports and follow established SOPs for data management.
Proficient in utilizing SharePoint and standard BI visualization tools.


Preferred Qualifications


7+ years of position-relevant experience.
Foundational training in knowledge management or project management.
Familiarity with USSOCOM data policies and the USINDOPACOM area of responsibility is preferred.


Clearance Requirements


Must hold an active TS/SCI clearance.


Physical Requirements


The person in this position must be able to remain in a stationary position 50% of the time.
Occasionally move about inside the office to access file cabinets, office machinery, or to communicate with co-workers, management, and customers, via email, phone, and or virtual communication, which may involve delivering presentations.

Title: Pricing & Reporting Analyst
Description:
Adventure is our Culture. Join a team that celebrates a lifestyle as bold as the terrain we love. At Backcountry, we are rooted in adventure, recognition, and wellbeing on and off the mountain. We spotlight employee stories, celebrate milestones, and offer exclusive outdoor perks. Whether you are at HQ, in a retail store, or remote, you will be part of a team that thrives on energy, exploration, and connection.

Reports to: Sr. Pricing Analyst

Location: Cottonwood Heights, Utah (Hybrid - 3x/week in-office)

About The Role

The Merchandise Planning team needs a data-driven analyst to own the day-to-day mechanics of pricing across Backcountry's four banners—competitive pricing audits, markdown execution, MAP/MSRP compliance, and the reporting that powers pricing decisions.

As Pricing & Reporting Analyst, you will work directly with the Sr. Pricing Analyst to maximize profitability through smart pricing execution. By 6–12 months, you will be running pricing operations independently, regularly making recommendations that move sell-through, margin, or working capital, and serving as the team's go-to for AI-augmented workflows.

This is a lean team. You will own a lot, move fast, and make decisions with full end-to-end responsibility.

About The Role


Pull pricing, margin, and sales data from the data warehouse for ad hoc analysis
Use AI tools (Claude, Claude Code, Copilot, or similar) daily to write formulas, draft SQL, automate repetitive tasks, summarize results, and accelerate your own learning
Execute daily, weekly, and seasonal price changes across four banners, ensuring accuracy and on-time delivery
Monitor competitive pricing across key categories and partner brands; surface risks and opportunities to the Sr. Pricing Analyst and Buying teams
Track MAP and MSRP compliance across the assortment, flagging and correcting violations in a timely manner
Bring analytical judgment to pricing and strategy conversations—surface trends, audit outcomes, flag risks, and recommend pricing actions that improve sell-through, margin, or working capital
Build and maintain pricing reports and dashboards that Planning, Buying, and Marketing teams rely on
Document and improve pricing processes—turn one-off analyses into repeatable AI-powered workflows
Partner with Marketing on promotional pricing setup and post-promo recaps
Maintain a strong feedback loop with the Sr. Pricing Analyst on what is working, what is not, and where to push next


What You'll Do


Pull pricing, margin, and sales data from the data warehouse for ad hoc analysis
Use AI tools (Claude, Claude Code, Copilot, or similar) daily to write formulas, draft SQL, automate repetitive tasks, summarize results, and accelerate your own learning
Execute daily, weekly, and seasonal price changes across four banners, ensuring accuracy and on-time delivery
Monitor competitive pricing across key categories and partner brands; surface risks and opportunities to the Sr. Pricing Analyst and Buying teams
Track MAP and MSRP compliance across the assortment, flagging and correcting violations in a timely manner
Bring analytical judgment to pricing and strategy conversations—surface trends, audit outcomes, flag risks, and recommend pricing actions that improve sell-through, margin, or working capital
Build and maintain pricing reports and dashboards that Planning, Buying, and Marketing teams rely on
Document and improve pricing processes—turn one-off analyses into repeatable AI-powered workflows
Partner with Marketing on promotional pricing setup and post-promo recaps
Maintain a strong feedback loop with the Sr. Pricing Analyst on what is working, what is not, and where to push next


Required Qualifications


3–5 years of analyst experience, preferably in retail, ecommerce, or a related data-driven role
Demonstrated daily use of AI tools (Claude, ChatGPT, Copilot, Gemini, or similar) for real work—you iterate on prompts, evaluate outputs critically, and know when AI is the right tool versus when it is not
High proficiency with Microsoft Excel (pivots, lookups, formulas) and a strong aptitude to learn technical applications quickly
Solid understanding of retail math (margin, markdown, sell-through, IMU) and financial concepts (GMROI, NPV, CM%)
Awareness of customer pricing behavior—anchoring, discount perception, and how merchandising and pricing decisions land on a product detail page
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills with the ability to work with messy datasets
Excellent attention to detail—pricing errors are expensive, and you treat that responsibility seriously
Excellent written and verbal communication; able to translate analysis into a clear, concise recommendation
Able to work both independently and collaboratively across Planning, Buying, Marketing, and Finance
Bachelor's degree in Business, Economics, Finance, Statistics, or a related field preferred; equivalent demonstrated experience considered
Passion for the outdoor industry and knowledge of outdoor gear is a plus.


Why Join

The people who do best here are builders. They take ownership, move fast, and want to see the direct impact of their work.


Cross-Functional Impact: Your pricing work carries weight beyond the Pricing team—recommendations you make will shape decisions across Planning, Buying, Marketing, and Finance.
AI-First Skill Building: AI is a first-class tool here, not a buzzword. You will use Claude, Copilot, and similar tools daily and actively shape how the team adopts them.
Real Ownership: This role pairs tactical pricing execution with strategic contributions. You will report to an experienced Sr. Pricing Analyst with growth-focused mentorship and high visibility from day one.
Competitive Benefits: Paid time off policies, 401(k) match, medical/dental/vision and a variety of supplemental policies, and employee discounts across our portfolio of brands.


Interview Process


Recruiter Screen - A 30-minute conversation with our recruiting team to align on the role, your background, and what you are looking for.
Hiring Manager Interview - Conversation with the Sr. Pricing Analyst focused on your analytical approach, pricing experience, and AI tool proficiency.
Take-Home Assignment - A pre-built test requiring high-level analysis, the use and documentation of AI as a working partner, and advanced communication skills to convey complex business recommendations.
In-Person Interview - We'd like to meet you at our Cottonwood Heights office. Details and logistics will be arranged with your recruiter.
Reference Checks - Conducted in parallel with the final stages where possible.
Offer - We move quickly for the right candidate.


Interview process is subject to change. Any updates will be communicated promptly and clearly.

CSC Generation is an equal opportunity employer. We do not discriminate on the basis of race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, age, disability, veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by law.

The CSC Generation family of brands is committed to providing reasonable accommodations for qualified individuals with disabilities in our job application procedures. If you need assistance or accommodation due to a disability, please contact [email protected] .

We may use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to support parts of the hiring process, such as reviewing applications, analyzing resumes, or assessing responses and identifying potential inconsistencies or verification signals in application materials based on available information. These tools assist our recruitment team but do not replace human judgment. Final hiring decisions are ultimately made by humans. If you would like more information about how your data is processed, please contact us.

Understanding and aligning enterprise and business unit goals, strategies, objectives, and operational needs of business unit(s) and facilitating the identification and creation of supportive, high-value, and actionable information and analytics.
Developing comprehensive sets of data and analytic (D&A) requirements.
Gathering and synthesizing information across multiple sources to derive insights that support the achievement of strategic and operational objectives.
Facilitating seamless communication between internal and external teams to ensure shared understanding and high-quality data and analytic work products.
Preparing and presenting relevant and actionable information.



This position is responsible for translating business needs into D&A solutions. It requires the ability to think strategically and cultivate relationships and collaborations across HMSA departments and with business and clinical partners.

Minimum Qualifications


Bachelor's degree and four years of work experience; or equivalent combination of education and work experience
Experience working in a collaborative team environment
Experience with analyzing, writing reports, and documenting business requirements
Technical requirements writing experience
Statistical analysis knowledge
Demonstrated knowledge in identifying issues, collecting data, and analyzing and interpreting information
Effective verbal, written, and presentation communication skills
Basic working knowledge of Microsoft Office applications including Word and Outlook/Email
Intermediate working knowledge of Microsoft Office PowerPoint
Advanced working knowledge of Microsoft Office Excel
Experience with SQL



Duties And Responsibilities


a. Research and Data analysis
Provides quality, objective, and professional analysis of relevant topics.
Gathers and analyzes information to identify trends, issues, innovations, and solutions for priority business needs.
Develops design analysis frameworks
Prepares reports
Presents results with recommendations
Implements solutions

Excellent communication skills using methods such as: extemporaneous verbal discussions; status reports; written analysis, assessments, and recommendations; oral and video presentations; email messages; and program documentation repositories.
Identifies and appropriately escalates issues, risks, and concerns.
Represents the Pharmacy Management department in interactions with both internal and external parties.
On a regular, sustained basis, cooperates with other staff members both within and outside the department in accomplishing one's own job duties as well as assisting others in accomplishing theirs.
Performs all other miscellaneous responsibilities and duties as assigned or directed.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
TTM Technologies, Inc. – Publicly Traded US Company, NASDAQ (TTMI) – Top-5 Global Printed Circuit Board Manufacturer

About TTM

TTM Technologies, Inc. is a leading global manufacturer of technology products, including mission systems, radio frequency (“RF”) components, RF microwave/microelectronic assemblies, and technologically advanced printed circuit boards (“PCB”s). TTM stands for time-to-market, representing how TTM's time-critical, one-stop design, engineering and manufacturing services enable customers to reduce the time required to develop new products and bring them to market.

Additional information can be found at www.ttm.com

TTM Technologies is seeking a Data Analyst with and understanding of Quality Production Engineering. This role will design, develop, and maintain dashboards and reports that provide actionable insights for forecasting and managing demand across our product lines.

Job Duties:


Diagnose problems in the production line and provide recommendations and training.
Assess and analyze current production methods
Develop and write program improvements for production and other areas to improve efficiency
Develop and draft an improved production process
Design and implement cost-reductive changes
Maximize productivity of machinery and workers
Lead Capacity studies for assigned processes
Collaborate with the production engineering staff in preparing & maintaining capacity models and plans
Determine the correct data sets and variables
Collect large sets of structured and unstructured data from disparate sources
Clean and validate the data to ensure accuracy, completeness, and uniformity
Devise and apply models and algorithms to mine the stores of big data
Analyzing the data to identify patterns and trends
Interpret the data to discover solutions and opportunities
Communicate findings to stakeholders using visualization and other means
Identify the data-analytics problems that offer the greatest opportunities to the organization


Qualifications:


Bachelor’s degree in Programming, Production Engineering or Industrial Engineering with 2-5 years of experience, or Bachelor’s degree in Engineering, or an equivalent combination of 5+ years’ practical experience and coursework is preferred.
Superb analytical, problem-solving, and critical thinking skills.
Proficiency with Microsoft Office is preferred
Demonstrated proficiency with a programming language such as Python, Matlab or Visual Basic is preferred.
Experience or familiarity with CAD is preferred
Familiarity with standardized work combination tables, Takt time, cycle time, etc.
Fundamental concepts in data science and analytics
Understanding of SQL and relational databases
Experience in SQL Join and conditional flow statements is preferred
RStudio for programming experience is preferred


#ISBU

Compensation and Benefits:

TTM offers a variety of health and well-being benefit programs. Benefit options include medical, dental, vision, 401K, Flexible Spending Account, Health Savings Account, accident benefits, life insurance, disability benefits, paid vacation & holidays. Benefits are available 1st of the month following date of hire.

Compensation for roles at TTM Technologies varies depending on a wide array of factors including but not limited to the specific office location, role, skill set and level of experience. As required by local law, TTM provides a reasonable range of compensation for roles that may be hired in New York, California and Colorado. For California-based roles, compensation ranges are based upon specific physical locations.

Export Statement:

Must comply with TTM Export Control Policies and Procedures and all applicable laws including ITAR, EAR and OFAC including but not limited to: a) being able to identify ITAR product on the manufacturing floor and understand that access to these products and related technical data is restricted to only US Citizens and US Permanent Residents; b) recognition of Foreign Person visitors by badge differentiation; c) understand and follow authorization procedures for bringing foreign visitors into facilities (VAL); d) understand the Export and ITAR requirements for shipments leaving the US; e) manage vendor approvals for ITAR manufacturing and services.

All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, citizenship, disability or protected veteran status.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
We are seeking a Data Analyst to join our growing Data & Analytics team at Funko. In this role, you will leverage data to deliver actionable insights that support business growth, operational efficiency, and smarter decision-making. You’ll work closely with cross-functional partners to build scalable reporting, analyze trends, and help drive a more data-informed culture.This is a hands-on individual contributor role ideal for someone who enjoys translating data into meaningful business impact.What You’ll Do
Partner with Operations, Sales, Marketing, Finance, and other teams to support data-driven decisions
Build and maintain dashboards, reports, and KPI tracking in BI tools (Power BI, Tableau Looker, etc.)
Develop AI-enabled and automated reporting solutions to enhance insight generation and reduce manual work
Perform exploratory data analysis (EDA) to identify trends, risks, and opportunities
Translate complex datasets into clear insights and business recommendations
Write and optimize SQL queries to work with large, complex datasets
Identify predictive modeling opportunities and build models
Collaborate with Data Engineering to improve data models, data quality, and reporting scalability
Support reporting and analytics needs tied to ERP and operational systems
What You’ll Bring
Bachelor’s degree in Data Science, Computer Science, Statistics, Mathematics, or related field (or equivalent experience)
3–5+ years of hands-on experience in data analytics
Strong SQL skills and experience working with large datasets
Proficiency in BI and data visualization tools (Tableau, Power BI, Looker, etc.)
Data science skill set: knowledgeable in statistical modeling (e.g. linear and logistical regression, classification, clustering) and tooling (python, R, etc.) with broad domain applicability
Experience with AI-driven analytics, automated reporting, or leveraging machine learning outputs in reporting workflows
Familiarity with cloud data platforms (Snowflake, AWS, GCP, Azure, etc.)
Experience working with ERP systems (Microsoft D365, Oracle, or similar) strongly preferred
Solid understanding of data warehousing and data modeling concepts
Strong analytical thinking, problem-solving skills, and ability to manage multiple priorities
Clear communication skills with the ability to present insights to non-technical stakeholders 
Salary InformationThe base salary range for this position in the selected city is $90,000 - $110,000 annually.​Compensation may vary outside of this range depending on a number of factors, including a candidate’s qualifications, skills, competencies and experience, and location. Base pay is one part of the Total Package that is provided to compensate and recognize employees for their work, and this role may be eligible for additional discretionary bonuses/incentives, and restricted stock units.

What Funko Offers
Funko offers a competitive compensation package with full benefits and a 401(K) plan with matching contributions from the company. Most importantly, we offer a creative work environment with people who love pop culture just as much as you do. Can’t wait to gush about your latest binge? Neither can we! Looking for a place where your favorite pop culture t-shirt will receive the compliments it deserves? We know how you feel!


This role will be based in our Everett, WA HQ office. We will prioritize local candidates first and candidates located near a local office. This is NOT a remote opportunity at this moment.
