Requirements of the role:
  • Bachelor’s degree, 5+ years in technology platforms, project management, or content development and distribution.
  • Experience with CMS and/or DAM platforms, content lifecycle management, and content migrations, including API-based integrations.
  • Familiarity with AI-assisted content analysis and UX or digital marketing concepts.
  • Strong skills in workflow/process mapping, with the ability to manage multiple priorities, meet deadlines, and operate effectively in ambiguous environments.
  • Advanced technical proficiency, including SQL and Excel (advanced formulas, pivot tables; VBA a plus), along with strong desktop tool capabilities.
  • Strong analytical and statistical reasoning skills, with ability to organize, analyze, and communicate large data sets with accuracy and attention to detail.
  • Knowledge of content categorization (campaign, program, asset class, product), with preferred experience in investment or asset management content.
  • Client-focused, execution-oriented professional with strong communication, organizational, relationship-building, and influencing skills, plus a data-driven approach to measuring content performance and impact.

The salary range for this position is $55,000 to $61,000 per year. The total compensation offered for this position includes salary and incentive pay and will vary based on skills, experience and location.

Full Time / Part Time

Full time

Worker Type

Employee

Job Exempt (Yes / No)

Yes

Workplace Model

Pursuant to Invesco’s Workplace Policy, employees are expected to comply with the firm’s most current workplace model, which as of October 1, 2025, includes spending at least four full days each week working in an Invesco office. This reflects our belief that spending time together in the office helps us build stronger relationships, collaborate more easily, and support each other’s growth and development.

What’s in it for you?

Our people are at the very core of our success and we strive to provide employees with a competitive total rewards package which includes:
  • Hybrid working environment
  • RRSP Contributions
  • Health & Wellness Benefits
  • Work flexibility Programs
  • Parental Leave Benefits
  • Study Support

The above information on this description has been designed to indicate the general nature and level of work performed by employees within this role. It is not designed to contain or be interpreted as a comprehensive inventory of all duties, responsibilities and qualifications required of employees assigned to this job. The job holder may be required to perform other duties as deemed appropriate by their manager from time to time.

Invesco is committed to fair and accessible employment practices. If selected for an interview, we will work with you to ensure that your interview is accessible and accommodation is provided. Please contact us at accessibility@invesco.ca or 1.800.874.6275 to let us know if you require accommodation for an interview due to a disability.

Title: Analyste de données
Description:
Entreprise: CAGC

Service: Sinistres

Catégories d’emplois: Poste permanent à temps plein

Modèle de travail: Hybride (2 jours au bureau)

Langue: Le bilinguisme français et anglais est un atout.

L’opportunité :

Nous sommes une coopérative canadienne de services financiers de premier plan engagée à promouvoir activement une société durable et résiliente, et notre équipe est essentielle au succès de cette stratégie. C’est pourquoi nos gens sont notre priorité. Nous offrons une culture d’entreprise vivante et des occasions de perfectionnement qui permettent à notre équipe d’exploiter tout son potentiel et de concrétiser notre raison d’être. Et le meilleur : vous travaillerez avec des personnes qui se soucient vraiment de vous, de notre clientèle et de nos collectivités.

Nous souhaitons apporter la tranquillité d’esprit chez nos clients et dans nos collectivités. Notre équipe nationale des sinistres, caractérisée par son savoir-faire et ses professionnels de confiance, sert les clients avec compassion. Nous agissons avec la plus grande intégrité envers nos clients, animés par le désir d’intervenir de la bonne façon et de constamment nous améliorer.

En qualité d’analyste de données, vous contribuez activement à diverses initiatives en analysant des données pour résoudre les problèmes de l’entreprise et en offrant des conseils judicieux Vous comprenez le domaine de l’assurance et vous faites en sorte que vos tâches contribuent à l’atteinte des objectifs de l’entreprise en appliquant les méthodes appropriées, en faisant preuve de pensée critique et en effectuant des analyses.

Comment vous créez un impact :


Effectuer des analyses descriptives et diagnostiques de manière autonome en utilisant les méthodes appropriées (p. ex. : analyse de régression, analyse de cohortes, analyse typologique, test A/B, analyse de facteurs).
Concevoir et développer des mesures visant à permettre l’analyse descriptive et diagnostique.
Concevoir une preuve de concept et de livrer une solution analytique prototype de manière guidée pour permettre une communication narrative efficace et une analyse descriptive ou diagnostique.
Travailler sur les requêtes de données (T-SQL, SQL, MySQL, etc.)
Extraire, nettoyer, rapprocher et préparer les données aux fins d’analyses.
Transmettre vos résultats à vos collègues et à votre gestionnaire de manière efficace et autonome.


Pour joindre notre équipe :


Vous possédez de trois à cinq années d’expérience en intelligence d’affaires ou dans un domaine connexe.
Vous détenez un diplôme universitaire en intelligence d’affaires, en informatique, en actuariat, en mathématiques ou en statistique, ou une expérience technique équivalente.
Les certifications ACAS ou FCAS constituent un atout.
Vous avez de l’expérience en résolution de problèmes standard en analysant les solutions possibles au moyen de votre expérience, de votre jugement et du statu quo précédent et complexe.
Vous avez une bonne connaissance et une compréhension de l’analyse des données, de la programmation, des techniques de modélisation, des entrepôts de données, de la gestion des données, de la recherche, de l’architecture et de la conception.
Posséder de l’expérience avec les technologies suivantes est requis : Sql, Python, R, Microsoft fabric
Posséder de l’expérience avec les technologies suivantes est un atout : Service now, Jira, Databricks
La personne qui occupe ce poste travaille principalement avec des groupes non francophones et avec des équipes à l’extérieur du Québec. La maîtrise de l’anglais est donc obligatoire. Les tâches essentielles qui nécessitent la connaissance d’une autre langue que le français ne peuvent être réattribuées à d’autres membres de l’équipe. 


Comment vous brillez :


Vous stimulez le changement et l’amélioration continue en vue de dépasser les attentes de la clientèle.
Vous faites appel à votre esprit critique afin de cerner les problèmes et de proposer des solutions de façon proactive.
Vos solides compétences en communication vous permettent de transmettre clairement les messages.
Vous faites preuve d’un bon esprit d’équipe et vous partagez vos connaissances afin de soutenir vos pairs.


Ce que vous devez savoir :


Si votre candidature est retenue, nous procéderons à la vérification de votre solvabilité et de vos antécédents judiciaires comme condition d’embauche.


Comment vous en bénéficiez :


Occasions de formation et de perfectionnement vous permettant de bâtir votre carrière.
Horaire de travail flexible et congés rémunérés pour subvenir à vos besoins et à ceux de votre famille.
Approche globale à l’égard de votre bien-être, programmes de santé physique et mentale et culture solidaire en milieu de travail.
Journées de bénévolat rémunérées pour redonner à votre collectivité.
En plus de nos salaires concurrentiels et de nos programmes incitatifs, les employées et employés admissibles bénéficient également d’un programme de rémunération globale complet, y compris des régimes d’épargne-retraite collectifs et une assurance collective (p. ex., assurance soins médicaux, mieux-être, soins dentaires, invalidité et vie), du soutien en santé mentale et un programme d’aide aux employés et employées.


Renseignements sur les salaires

Échelle salariale prévue de : CAD $60,574.00 to $100,956.00 CAD

Le salaire du candidat retenu sera déterminé à la discrétion de Co-operators et variera selon plusieurs critères, notamment les conditions du marché local, la situation géographique et des facteurs liés à l'emploi tels que les connaissances, les compétences, les qualifications, l'expérience et la formation.

Les employés pourront également participer à des programmes de primes et obtenir une rémunération supplémentaire liée à leur rendement individuel et/ou à celui de l'entreprise, ou à d'autres indicateurs de performance.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
We’re Hiring!

Data Analyst

Toronto, ON | Montreal, QC

www.socan.com

POSITION TYPE: Permanent Full-Time

HIRING RANGE: $71,920 to $89,900

REASON FOR HIRING: Replacement

WORK MODEL: Hybrid

LANGUAGE: English, French is an asset

Who We Are

SOCAN is a not-for-profit copyright collective dedicated to ensuring music creators and publishers receive what they have rightfully earned for the use of their work. SOCAN supports and represents nearly 200,000 songwriters, composers, and music publishers. Through licensing, global royalty collection and advocacy, we help creators to keep making the music that entertains, moves, and inspires us

OUR COMMITMENT TO DIVERSITY, EQUITY, INCLUSION, AND ANTI-RACISM   

SOCAN thrives with a variety of viewpoints, identities, and backgrounds, and we are committed to anti-racism. Everyone is welcome to apply for our wide range of roles, regardless of gender identity, gender expression, ethnicity, race, age, culture, sexual orientation, religious belief, or physical ability. Learn more about SOCAN’s commitment to Inclusion & Anti-Racism.  

WHY WORK WITH US?


Make a difference by supporting and collaborating with a vibrant and creative community
Enjoy many options for workplace flexibility and work-life balance
Get involved in the rapidly changing creative space
Advocate for and empower the creative community
Together, we’ll help music creators earn more income and make a living


What We Offer


35-hour workweek schedule (possible flexible work options i.e., 4-day work week (position based)
Twelve paid sick days annually (including five personal days)
Access to SOCAN fitness facility
Annual Performance Incentive bonus (dependent on a personal and company performance)
Defined contribution Pension Plan
Comprehensive, health and dental benefits program
Inclusive and collaborative working environment 


About The Role

SOCAN is seeking a Data Analyst to join our Analytics department. In this role, you’ll be accountable for turning raw data into actionable business insights through various reports and dashboards, driving improvement in our key organizational initiatives. You must be comfortable working closely with data engineers, BI analysts, and business leaders. The ideal candidate has a solid grasp of data fundamentals and is eager, curious, and possesses a drive for continuous learning and self-improvement, especially with modern cloud data stacks.

The knowledge of English is required for this position as the position requires to interact on a constant and regular basis with English-speaking stakeholders outside of Quebec

What You’ll Do / Key Responsibilities


Work directly with data engineers, BI analysts, other data analysts, and business leaders to document reporting requirements and understand business processes and how they affect data.
Perform exploratory data analysis to clean, transform, and analyze large datasets within Databricks to identify trends, anomalies, and opportunities to improve our data.
Support data quality assurance by creating data quality tests and plans for various reporting and Lakehouse projects that can assist in troubleshooting data discrepancies and pipeline issues.
Work with each department leader to define and document KPIs.
Perform root-cause analysis on KPI fluctuations to provide actionable insights to department leaders.
Collaborate on the continuous development of interactive Databricks AI/BI dashboards and reports, designing and maintaining clear dashboards to communicate findings to various stakeholders.
Curate and document semantic layers to power natural language querying (Genie) for business users.
Participate in, and contribute towards daily standup meetings, project planning sessions, and retrospectives.
Manage, prioritize, and respond to ad hoc requests for data analysis.


Required

WHAT YOU WILL BRING / QUALIFICATIONS & EXPERIENCE


Strong analytical and quantitative skills with the ability to work on large data sets, while keeping the big picture in mind.
Strong SQL skills and intermediate Python. An understanding of Spark and distributed compute environments.
Experience and proficiency performing data analysis using Databricks or similar technologies (2+ years).
Strong communicator, with the ability to tackle loosely defined problems with a solution oriented mindset.
Business writing skills as well as the ability to create technical documentation, communicating clearly for both technical and non-technical audiences.
Degree in a quantitative field (e.g., Computer Science, Stats, Economics, Business Analytics) or equivalent experience.


Nice To Have


Passion & love for music.
Fluency in French


COMPETENCIES


Critical Thinking & Problem Solving
Emotionally intelligent
Commitment to Continuous Learning
Process Oriented


ACCESSIBILITY & ACCOMMODATION

SOCAN is committed to providing an inclusive workplace environment that meets the accessibility needs of employees with disabilities. Should you require accommodations please contact us directly at hr@socan.com and we will make the necessary accommodations. Applications submitted to this email address will not be accepted.

EQUAL EMPLOYMENT OPPORTUNITY

SOCAN is an Equal Opportunity Employer. Hiring and other employment decisions at SOCAN are made without regard to race, colour, religion, sex, ancestry, national origin, ethnic origin, age, disability, citizenship, veteran status, sexual orientation, record of offences, marital status, family status, or any other characteristic protected by federal, provincial, or local law, regulation, or ordinance.

If you don’t see yourself fully reflected in every job requirement listed on the posting above, we still encourage you to reach out and apply. Research has shown underrepresented groups often only apply when they feel 100% qualified. We are committed to creating a more equitable, inclusive, and diverse company and we strongly encourage applicants of all genders, ages, ethnicities, cultures, abilities, sexual orientations, and life experiences to apply

How To Apply

If you’re interested in joining us, we’d love to hear from you: http://www.socan.com/careers/

Thank you for your interest in our company; we will be in touch with those candidates we wish to interview.

To learn more about SOCAN, go to www.socan.com. You can also follow us on LinkedIn, Twitter, Instagram, and Facebook (@SOCANmusic/@SOCANmusique).

Title: Data & Systems Analyst
Description:
SKYSERVICE BUSINESS AVIATION

Skyservice is the North American market leader in business aviation. Built on the key principles of safety and service excellence, we offer full-service support and expertise in aircraft management, charter, maintenance, and sales from our fixed-based operator services across Canada and the United States. People and values are inseparable assets that fuel our commitment to Quality, Safety, Respect, Commitment, and Efficiency. These key values guide us every day and contribute to our reputation as industry leaders in aviation services. Skyservice is a team that is committed to supporting and developing our people. If you wish to advance your career and be part of team committed to reaching further and aiming higher in Business Aviation, the Skyservice family of companies is the team for you.

Get to know us: https://www.skyservice.com/

YOUR FUTURE AT SKYSERVICE:

Here at Skyservice, we are continuing to grow and provide high-level service to our clients, expanding our footprint and name throughout the Americas. You will bring strong attention to detail and a fast-paced way of working with dynamic schedules to a team that works together to exceed the expectations of our clients. We focus on excellence and are committed to support and develop our teams!

JOB PURPOSE: The Data & Systems Analyst supports business operations by leading data analytics initiatives, managing critical data pipelines, and maintaining core business applications. This role partners with business stakeholders and technology teams to bridge the gap between complex software development, cloud data engineering, and business intelligence, transforming raw data into secure, actionable insights.

SUMMARY OF RESPONSIBILITIES:


Application & Systems Management: Maintain, optimize, and develop business systems and web interfaces using Visual Studio environments, C# (.NET Framework and Core 9.0+).
Data Engineering & Pipelines: Extract, synthesize, and transform large datasets from multiple sources using Azure Databricks (PySpark and Databricks SQL) and automate workflows using Power Automate.
Database Administration & Querying: Write, optimize, and troubleshoot complex queries across relational databases, specifically Azure SQL and Oracle SQL, using SSMS and Oracle SQL Developer.
Reporting & Business Intelligence: Design, build, and maintain enterprise-level reports and dashboards using both modern tools (Power BI) and legacy systems (Crystal Reports).
AI & Document Automation: Implement intelligent data extraction and automation workflows using Azure Document Intelligence and Python scripting.
Stakeholder Collaboration: Collaborate with cyber and business stakeholders to understand needs, map data requirements, and deliver secure data solutions.
Data Quality & Documentation: Monitor data quality, troubleshoot database issues to ensure absolute integrity, and document data processes, definitions, and application workflows.


QUALIFICATIONS AND SKILLS REQUIRED:

Education & Experience:


Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Data Engineering, Information Systems, or a related quantitative field (or equivalent experience);
4–7+ years of blended experience spanning data analytics, software development, and data engineering (Senior – P03 level).


Technical Skills (Required):


Software Development: Strong proficiency in MS Visual Studio, C# with experience in .NET Framework and .NET Core (9.0+).
Data Engineering & Automation: Hands-on experience with Azure Databricks (PySpark / Databricks SQL) and Python for data manipulation and scripting.
Database Management: Advanced SQL skills with deep operational experience in both Azure SQL (SSMS) and Oracle SQL (Oracle SQL Developer).
Reporting & BI: Double-threat reporting capabilities with experience in Power BI (modern dashboards) and Crystal Reports (legacy enterprise reporting).
Cloud & AI Tools: Familiarity with Azure Document Intelligence for document/data extraction and Power Automate for workflow automation.


Core Competencies:


Solid understanding of data modeling, data structures, and database performance tuning.
Strong analytical, problem-solving, and critical-thinking skills with a high attention to detail.
Ability to manage multiple priorities, work independently, and troubleshoot complex system-level bugs.
Excellent communication skills with the ability to translate technical developer concepts into clear business insights.
Practical, solution-oriented mindset with a focus on delivering business value;
Strong interpersonal and stakeholder management skills, with the ability to translate business needs into data solutions;
Excellent communication skills (both written and verbal).


Benefits:

JOB PURPOSE: The Data & Systems Analyst supports business operations by leading data analytics initiatives, managing critical data pipelines, and maintaining core business applications. This role partners with business stakeholders and technology teams to bridge the gap between complex software development, cloud data engineering, and business intelligence, transforming raw data into secure, actionable insights.

SUMMARY OF RESPONSIBILITIES:

  • Application & Systems Management: Maintain, optimize, and develop business systems and web interfaces using Visual Studio environments, C# (.NET Framework and Core 9.0+).
  • Data Engineering & Pipelines: Extract, synthesize, and transform large datasets from multiple sources using Azure Databricks (PySpark and Databricks SQL) and automate workflows using Power Automate.
  • Database Administration & Querying: Write, optimize, and troubleshoot complex queries across relational databases, specifically Azure SQL and Oracle SQL, using SSMS and Oracle SQL Developer.
  • Reporting & Business Intelligence: Design, build, and maintain enterprise-level reports and dashboards using both modern tools (Power BI) and legacy systems (Crystal Reports).
  • AI & Document Automation: Implement intelligent data extraction and automation workflows using Azure Document Intelligence and Python scripting.
  • Stakeholder Collaboration: Collaborate with cyber and business stakeholders to understand needs, map data requirements, and deliver secure data solutions.
  • Data Quality & Documentation: Monitor data quality, troubleshoot database issues to ensure absolute integrity, and document data processes, definitions, and application workflows.

QUALIFICATIONS AND SKILLS REQUIRED:

Education & Experience:

  • Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Data Engineering, Information Systems, or a related quantitative field (or equivalent experience);
  • 4–7+ years of blended experience spanning data analytics, software development, and data engineering (Senior – P03 level).

Technical Skills (Required):

  • Software Development: Strong proficiency in MS Visual Studio, C# with experience in .NET Framework and .NET Core (9.0+).
  • Data Engineering & Automation: Hands-on experience with Azure Databricks (PySpark / Databricks SQL) and Python for data manipulation and scripting.
  • Database Management: Advanced SQL skills with deep operational experience in both Azure SQL (SSMS) and Oracle SQL (Oracle SQL Developer).
  • Reporting & BI: Double-threat reporting capabilities with experience in Power BI (modern dashboards) and Crystal Reports (legacy enterprise reporting).
  • Cloud & AI Tools: Familiarity with Azure Document Intelligence for document/data extraction and Power Automate for workflow automation.

Core Competencies:

  • Solid understanding of data modeling, data structures, and database performance tuning.
  • Strong analytical, problem-solving, and critical-thinking skills with a high attention to detail.
  • Ability to manage multiple priorities, work independently, and troubleshoot complex system-level bugs.
  • Excellent communication skills with the ability to translate technical developer concepts into clear business insights.
  • Practical, solution-oriented mindset with a focus on delivering business value;
  • Strong interpersonal and stakeholder management skills, with the ability to translate business needs into data solutions;
  • Excellent communication skills (both written and verbal).

Title: Systems Analyst II - Data Analysis and Migration
Description:
Description

Join our team

Do you thrive in a fast-paced, team centric environment? Are you a detailed-oriented individual who loves solving problems by leveraging creative solutions? Do you want to be in a space where your knowledge and experience are used and fostered? Read on as this is the job for you!

As a key member of the TELUS Health team, you will be joining a growing team of Analysts in transforming and converting health data for Canadian physicians on a TELUS EMR (Electronic Medical Record) platform. This exciting opportunity will allow you to leverage your experience and skillset within an innovative and collaborative environment while performing meaningful work that is crucial to the success of our TELUS Health EMR platforms.

Here’s How

As part of the team, you’ll be responsible for:



Extracting data from various source systems and data stores by performing light business coding (ex. Java, Python, C#, etc.)
Connecting with our customers to conduct requirement gathering and performing data validations
Mapping and transforming data using various techniques (ex. XSLT, MapForce, scripting, etc.) based on customer input and requirements
Performing ad-hoc queries (ex. SQL, LINQ, etc.) and reports to analyse customer data before and after migration
Planning and performing go-live transformation and validation to ensure no data is lost or corrupted
Executing production data migrations (requires occasional evening/weekend work)
Collaboration with various teams across the business to identify roadblocks and resolve issues, to ensure timely completion for each project
Contributing to on-going development and refinement of data migration tools and processes
Tracking issues, communicating project status, and managing project risk



What You Bring

Required Experience



Graduation from a post-secondary institution with a Diploma specializing in related technologies
1 year minimum experience working on data transformation, development, or school related projects



Technical Knowledge



SQL/TSQL
Data Transformation Scripting and Mapping (ex. XML, XSL/XPath)
Familiarity with databases such as MSSQL, Oracle, MySQL, etc.
Scripting (ex. Python/Groovy) or development (ex. Java/C#)
Linux/Unix and Groovy an asset
MapForce an asset



Functional Skills



Detail oriented
Avid problem solver
Ability to communicate both verbally and written in English, French is an asset
Ability to multi-task and manage numerous concurrent projects
Strong analysis and problem solving skills with the ability to identify root cause
Ability to communicate in an effective and respectful manner with customers and fellow team members
Self-directed in completing project-based work



Great to Have



Agile & Project Management skills
Understanding of Network Infrastructure
Electronic Medical Records (EMR), Medical or clinical workflow experience
Experience with HL7, CoPD and CDS/CMS XML standards
Experience with the Atlassian tool suite (ex. Jira, Bitbucket, Confluence)

Title: AI and Analytics Analyst
Description:
The future you want is within reach. Let's build it together.

At PCL Constructors Inc., part of the PCL Family of Companies (PCL), we don't just build projects -- we build opportunities, careers and communities. We are 100% employee-owned, every employee has a stake in our success, and that shared commitment drives how we work, grow and lead in the construction industry.

We're a team of builders who care deeply about what we create and who we build with. That includes you. We are not only shaping the future of construction, we are also committed to shaping the future of your career.

As an AI and Analytics Analyst for PCL Constructors Inc. in Edmonton, Alberta (Corporate), you'll have the opportunity to lead our HR storytelling, by leveraging HR and employee information to determine actionable insights that drive strategic decision-making. This role focuses on HR analytics and reporting, identifying key metrics and trends to proactively inform and prepare the organization. The individual will strategically explore AI capabilities and trends and drive transformative AI initiatives. Focusing on discovering and prioritizing high-impact AI use cases that deliver measurable value.

Why Choose PCL?

Choose a Career With Rewards That Matter. PCL's Total Rewards Are Designed To Support Your Growth, Well-being And Future Success -- Because When You Succeed, We All Do. Our Offerings Could Include


Employee ownership opportunities that build long-term value
Annual discretionary performance bonuses
RRSP, TFSA, Pension Contribution Options
Flexible medical, dental and vision benefits
Prescription drug coverage and virtual care services
Life, AD&D and disability insurance
Paid parental leave and family care support
Health and lifestyle spending account options
Mental health and wellness support, including Employee Assistance Programs
Career growth pathways, leadership development and mentorship programs
Access to world-class training through PCL's College of Construction and professional development courses
Ongoing opportunities to learn new skills, explore different roles and grow your career across sectors and regions


Here's how an AI and Analytics Analyst for PCL Constructors Inc. within Edmonton NAHQ contributes to our team:

Responsibilities


Lead HR analytics and reporting initiatives to provide insights that inform HR strategies and business decisions.
Identify patterns and trends in HR, employee and business metrics to support strategic decision-making for process improvements based on data insights and analytics.Use data to tell a compelling story, translating complex information into clear, actionable narratives for stakeholders.
Research AI capabilities, AI trends, and automation to optimize HR processes strategically.
Identify, evaluate, and prioritize AI use cases that deliver measurable business value and operational efficiency.
Collaborate with other corporate departments and the HR Leadership team to ensure alignment with priorities and initiatives.
Ensure data integrity and compliance with privacy and security standards.


Qualifications


Post-secondary degree or diploma in Business Analytics, Data Science, or a related discipline.
Minimum 5 years of relevant experience, strong exposure to analytics and reporting.
Advanced proficiency in data visualization tools (e.g., Power BI, SAP SuccessFactors).
Strong understanding of AI capabilities, trends, and automation.
Strong organizational skills, high level of detail orientation, and ability to multitask and prioritize conflicting deadlines.
Possess business acumen and an understanding of how to incorporate AI solutions that positively contribute to business operations.
Demonstrated ability translate data into actionable insights.
Effective verbal and written communication skills with the ability to negotiate and present ideas and content that influence a favorable outcome.
Results oriented and has proven project management experience.


Your Work Has Purpose Here

PCL projects are where life happens, where communities connect, careers begin, and progress is made. Regardless of the type of project, we are building something bigger: a future that's inclusive, resilient and full of opportunity.

At PCL Constructors Inc., we are committed to creating a workplace where everyone belongs. We value the diverse experiences, identities and perspectives our employees bring. Employment decisions are based on merit, potential and the drive to make a difference, regardless of race, gender, age, ability or background.

We know everyone's needs are different, if you require accommodation during the application process, please contact careers@pcl.com and include the position and location of interest.

This recruitment process does not involve the use of artificial intelligence (AI) to screen, assess, or select applicants. All hiring decisions are made by human reviewers.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Role: Data Governance Analyst

Location: Calgary, AB, Canada

Type: Contract

Duration: 12 Months




Job Responsibilities:

Seeking a Data Governance Analyst to support a large-scale SAP implementation project. This role will be responsible for capturing requirements and establishing data governance standards to ensure high-quality, consistent, and compliant data. The analyst will work closely with business stakeholders, data stewards, and technical teams to define data ownership, improve data quality, and support project readiness and long-term sustainability.

Top 3 Outcomes:

Establish and maintain data governance standards
Support data migration activities for master data governance tool implementation
Develop and track data quality KPIs and metrics




Responsibilities:

Define and document data governance policies, and standards for SAP data objects (e.g., customer, vendor, material, finance).
Establish and support data ownership and data stewardship models across business domains
Collaborate with business and IT teams to define data definitions, business rules, and data standards.
Support data migration activities, including data cleansing, validation, and reconciliation for master data governance tool implementation
Develop and track data quality KPIs and metrics.
Ensure compliance with internal policies and regulatory requirements related to data management.
Facilitate data governance workshops with stakeholders to align on definitions, rules and ownership.
Review SOX Compliance documents and work closely with control / audit teams to gather SOX requirements.
Maintain data structures, metadata, and documentation in approved data catalogues.
Support the implementation of data governance tools and workflows.
Collaborate with functional teams (Finance, Procurement, Supply Chain, etc.) to ensure alignment on data requirements.
Assist with project closure activities, ensuring data-related deliverables are complete and signed off.




Qualifications & Skills Required

Required Qualifications

Bachelor’s degree in Information Systems, Business, Computer Science, or a related field.
3–7 years of experience in data governance, data management, or data quality roles.
Experience supporting or working on SAP implementations (e.g., S/4HANA preferred).
Strong understanding of master data domains (Customer, Vendor, Material, Finance).
Experience with data migration, data cleansing, validation processes, and data management methods.




Technical Skills

Knowledge of SAP tables and data structures.
Strong understanding of databases and data structures, independent of specific platforms or technologies.
Experience with SAP transactions (e.g., SE16N) and data extraction tools.
Familiarity with data profiling and data quality tools.
Strong Excel and data analysis skills.
Experience with data governance or metadata tools (e.g., Prospecta MDO, SAP MDG, Purview, DataSphere, or Information Steward) is an asset.




Preferred Experience

Prior experience in large-scale ERP transformation projects.
Experience in regulated industries (e.g., energy, finance, utilities).
Understanding of data privacy and compliance requirements. Knowledge of regulatory or compliance-driven data environments.




Soft Skills

Strong stakeholder management and communication skills.
Ability to translate business requirements into data rules and standards.
Detail-oriented with strong analytical and problem-solving skills.
Ability to facilitate workshops and drive alignment across teams.
Strong organizational skills and ability to manage multiple priorities.

Title: Revenue Operations Analyst
Description:
Revenue Operations Analyst

About Coolit Systems Inc.

Founded in Calgary, Alberta in 2001, CoolIT Systems is the leading Direct Liquid Cooling technology provider for data centers, supercomputers, and desktop computers. We design and manufacture solutions used by the largest tech companies globally.

Recognized as one of The Americas' Fastest Growing Companies 2023 by the Financial Times and honored with the Deloitte Fast 50 Clean Technology award in 2022, as well as Deloitte Fast 500 distinctions in both 2021 and 2022, CoolIT's rapid growth is capturing global attention.

As demand for generative AI, high performance computing, and cloud solutions rapidly expands, there has never been a more exciting time to join our team.

Role

WHAT YOU'LL DO:

Reporting to the Director, Strategic Operations, the Revenue Operations Analyst helps build a disciplined, data-driven commercial engine. This role supports forecast accuracy, pipeline visibility, commercial reporting, and executive decision-making across Sales, Customer Operations, Finance, Technical Sales, and other stakeholders. It is well suited to a candidate who enjoys turning data into clear business insight inside a fast-growing manufacturing environment.


Own recurring pipeline, forecast, bookings, and commercial performance reporting for leadership reviews.
Analyze stage conversion, sales-cycle velocity, opportunity aging, slippage, and current-quarter revenue risk.
Build and maintain executive-ready scorecards, dashboards, and monthly business review materials.
Partner with Sales leadership to improve forecast discipline, deal inspection rigor, and data quality.
Support strategic account reviews by highlighting performance trends, execution risks, and commercial exposure.
Work closely with Finance to reconcile bookings, shipment assumptions, and revenue timing risks.
Translate large datasets into concise, practical recommendations for action.


Responsibilities

Forecasting & Revenue Insight


Prepare weekly forecast packs, including commit, best case, upside, and risk commentary.
Track forecast accuracy over time by region, rep, segment, and product line.
Identify and quantify current-quarter risks tied to delayed deals, operational blockers, or customer dependencies.
Support bookings-to-revenue bridge analysis and quarter-close readiness.


Pipeline Analysis & Commercial Reporting


Monitor pipeline coverage, stage movement, stale opportunities, and push-out reasons.
Produce weekly and monthly pipeline health reports for leadership.
Build reports on funnel conversion, average deal size, cycle time, win rates, and productivity.
Support MBRs, QBRs, annual planning, and ad hoc commercial analysis.


Cross-Functional Partnership


Partner with Sales, Customer Operations, Finance, Marketing, and Technical Sales to align reporting logic and assumptions.
Incorporate customer-risk, escalation, and post-sales data into broader account and revenue visibility.
Help standardize metric definitions and reporting discipline across the organization.


Continuous Improvement


Surface data quality issues, process gaps, and reporting inefficiencies.
Recommend opportunities to automate recurring analysis and reduce manual work.
Support improvements to opportunity stage governance, dashboard usability, and KPI consistency.


Who You Are

Basic Qualifications


Bachelor's degree in Business, Finance, Operations, Analytics, Economics, or a related field, or equivalent practical experience.
3-5 years of experience in Revenue Operations, Sales Operations, business analytics, or a similar commercial reporting role.
Strong Excel capability, including advanced formulas, pivot tables, and structured analysis.
2-3 years of experience working with CRM or ERP systems such as NetSuite, Salesforce, HubSpot, or similar.


Preferred Qualifications


Experience in a manufacturing, industrial technology, data center, hardware, or other complex B2B environment.
Experience building recurring KPI packs, forecast reporting, or leadership dashboards.
Exposure to BI/reporting tools such as Power BI, Tableau, Looker, or equivalent.
Familiarity with bookings, backlog, shipments, or quote-to-order metrics is a strong asset.


Skills


Strong analytical judgment and the ability to connect numbers to business decisions.
Clear written communication and strong executive-summary skills.
High attention to detail, accuracy, and follow-through.
Ability to work under deadline pressure in a fast-paced growth environment.
Comfort partnering with leaders and asking probing questions when data does not make sense.


WHY COOLIT?

We strive to be an employer of choice and, as such, we believe in rewarding our employees with career and development opportunities that maintain and strengthen our culture while aligning to our vision and values.

Benefits


Vacation and flex days, and paid company-wide shutdown during the holiday season.
Comprehensive benefits including Dental, Drugs, Optical, Paramedical, and Health Spending. These costs are covered by CoolIT and are enacted from your first day.
Dress for your day - in a casual yet professional environment.
Retention rewards like The Big Coffee Break that reward tenure with additional vacation time and a monetary bonus.
Company-funded RRSP contributions to support your long-term financial goals.


We thank every candidate in advance; however, only those selected for an interview will be contacted.

Successful candidates for this position will be required to undergo background checks including criminal records check and education verification.

  • Monitor pipeline coverage, stage movement, stale opportunities, and push-out reasons.
  • Produce weekly and monthly pipeline health reports for leadership.
  • Build reports on funnel conversion, average deal size, cycle time, win rates, and productivity.
  • Support MBRs, QBRs, annual planning, and ad hoc commercial analysis.

Cross-Functional Partnership

  • Partner with Sales, Customer Operations, Finance, Marketing, and Technical Sales to align reporting logic and assumptions.
  • Incorporate customer-risk, escalation, and post-sales data into broader account and revenue visibility.
  • Help standardize metric definitions and reporting discipline across the organization.

Continuous Improvement

  • Surface data quality issues, process gaps, and reporting inefficiencies.
  • Recommend opportunities to automate recurring analysis and reduce manual work.
  • Support improvements to opportunity stage governance, dashboard usability, and KPI consistency.

Who You Are

Basic Qualifications

  • Bachelor's degree in Business, Finance, Operations, Analytics, Economics, or a related field, or equivalent practical experience.
  • 3-5 years of experience in Revenue Operations, Sales Operations, business analytics, or a similar commercial reporting role.
  • Strong Excel capability, including advanced formulas, pivot tables, and structured analysis.
  • 2-3 years of experience working with CRM or ERP systems such as NetSuite, Salesforce, HubSpot, or similar.

Preferred Qualifications

  • Experience in a manufacturing, industrial technology, data center, hardware, or other complex B2B environment.
  • Experience building recurring KPI packs, forecast reporting, or leadership dashboards.
  • Exposure to BI/reporting tools such as Power BI, Tableau, Looker, or equivalent.
  • Familiarity with bookings, backlog, shipments, or quote-to-order metrics is a strong asset.

Skills

  • Strong analytical judgment and the ability to connect numbers to business decisions.
  • Clear written communication and strong executive-summary skills.
  • High attention to detail, accuracy, and follow-through.
  • Ability to work under deadline pressure in a fast-paced growth environment.
  • Comfort partnering with leaders and asking probing questions when data does not make sense.

WHY COOLIT?

We strive to be an employer of choice and, as such, we believe in rewarding our employees with career and development opportunities that maintain and strengthen our culture while aligning to our vision and values.

Benefits

  • Vacation and flex days, and paid company-wide shutdown during the holiday season.
  • Comprehensive benefits including Dental, Drugs, Optical, Paramedical, and Health Spending. These costs are covered by CoolIT and are enacted from your first day.
  • Dress for your day - in a casual yet professional environment.
  • Retention rewards like The Big Coffee Break that reward tenure with additional vacation time and a monetary bonus.
  • Company-funded RRSP contributions to support your long-term financial goals.

We thank every candidate in advance; however, only those selected for an interview will be contacted.

Successful candidates for this position will be required to undergo background checks including criminal records check and education verification.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Descripción oferta

Desde la Dirección de NEGOCIO, precisamos incorporar un/a DATA ANALYST en ANÁLISIS DE NEGOCIO, ubicado en BARCELONA.

Su misión será facilitar a los equipos de Negocio información de valor sobre la evolución del negocio y los clientes de VidaCaixa, facilitando la obtención de insights y conclusiones a partir de la explotación analítica de datos. La persona que se incorpore combinará el conocimiento de técnicas y herramientas de explotación de datos con fuertes capacidades analíticas y conocimiento de negocio.

¿Cuál será tu propósito dentro de VidaCaixa?


Desarrollo y divulgación de análisis e informes que permitan comprender la evolución del negocio y faciliten la obtención de insights.
Análisis en profundidad que faciliten la toma de decisiones y acompañen la estrategia de negocio: evolución de las ventas y las cancelaciones por producto, impacto de campañas, lanzamiento de nuevos productos, simuladores y creación de escenarios, valoración de oportunidades y potencial de negocio, …
Incorporación de la visión cliente que permita entender las necesidades y comportamientos de los clientes a partir la analítica avanzada de datos.
Desarrollo de cuadros de mando (QlikSense) y procesos automatizados que permitan innovar y optimizar la explotación y acceso transversal a los datos.
Innovación y mejora continua en las formas de explotar y visualizar la información, explorando el potencial de nuevas herramientas y la IA.
Trabajar juntamente con los equipos de negocio para la identificación de nuevas oportunidades a partir de los datos.
Conocimiento de las bases de datos y el sistema informacional a partir del que se explotan los datos de clientes.



Requisitos:

Eres el talento que estamos buscando si cuentas con…


Formación universitaria superior (Matemáticas, Estadística, Ingeniería, Informática, Física…) o cualquier formación con foco en especialidad analítica.
Nivel avanzado en programación SQL para el análisis y transformación de bases de datos.
Experiencia previa como Data Analyst o analista de Business Intelligence: análisis de perfil de clientes, seguimientos de negocio, definición de indicadores, etc.
Eres una persona con iniciativa, organizada y orientada a resultados.
Cuentas con capacidad analítica para la extracción, comunicación y presentación de conclusiones a los responsables de negocio a partir de los datos.
Facilidad para trabajar en equipo y en proyectos con equipos transversales.
Curiosidad, predisposición a la innovación, aprendizaje y mejora continua.
Se valorará de forma positiva experiencia en herramientas de diseño de cuadros de mando y visualización de datos (QlikSense, PowerBI, Tableau).
Se valorará de forma positiva experiencia previa en el sector bancario o asegurador.



Beneficios

Sabemos que harás un gran trabajo, por eso hemos pensado en estos beneficios para ti…


Contrato indefinido
Flexibilidad horaria de entrada (7:30 a 9:30) y en la comida (puedes emplear entre un mínimo de 45 minutos y un máximo de 2 horas) para que puedas autogestionar tu tiempo
Plan de acogida y seguimiento de tu desarrollo profesional, con evaluaciones 360 y planes de desarrollo individuales.
Acceso a una plataforma online de formación, con un amplio catálogo autoformativo, para que tu aprendizaje sea continuo.
Todos los viernes con jornada intensiva para que puedas disfrutar más del fin de semana.
Del 15 de junio al 15 de septiembre jornada intensiva TODOS los días, porque sabemos que es verano y quieres disfrutar más tiempo de las horas de sol
Modelo de trabajo híbrido. Porque creemos que pasar tiempo en la oficina es un factor clave para desarrollar la creatividad, y disfrutar en equipo.
Fruta, porque es importante cuidarse. ¡Mens sana, in corpore sano!
29 días hábiles de vacaciones. ¡Desconectar, para volver a conectar!
Plan de Beneficios: plan de pensiones, seguro de salud, seguro de vida, ticket restaurante
Retribución flexible aplicado a guardería y/o transporte.



Proceso

¿Cómo será el proceso de selección?


Recibimos y validamos tu solicitud
Evaluación telefónica con especialista de selección
Realización de informe
Entrevista con el/la responsable de departamento
Entrevista personal con HRBP
¡Y no lo olvides, te daremos feedback!

Title: Data Analyst (BigQuery, Looker, SQL)
Description:
En Grupo Aubay buscamos un/a Analista de Datos con buen nivel de SQL para participar en todo el ciclo analítico: obtención de datos desde SAP y otras fuentes, transformación en BigQuery, modelado para reporting y creación de dashboards en Looker.




La persona seleccionada tendrá un rol muy orientado a negocio dentro del área de Supply Chain y con exposición a tecnologías modernas como Google Cloud Platform.




¿Cuáles serán tus principales funciones?

Ingesta, análisis y comprensión de datos procedentes de SAP, sistemas legacy y GCP
Transformación de datos bajo arquitectura Medallion (Bronze, Silver, Gold)
Diseño y mantenimiento de modelos de datos dimensionales (Kimball)
Desarrollo de pipelines en Dataform
Creación de dashboards reutilizables en Looker
Control y validación de calidad de datos
Traducción de necesidades de negocio a soluciones analíticas
Documentación técnica y funcional de modelos y datasets




Requisitos

1–2 años de experiencia como Data Analyst o perfil similar
SQL avanzado
Experiencia en modelado dimensional (Kimball)
Conocimientos de Python
Experiencia o conocimiento en GCP (especialmente BigQuery)
Nivel alto de inglés (imprescindible)
Tecnologías del entorno: GCP (BigQuery), Dataform, Looker, GitHub, Modelado Kimball




Se ofrece

Participación en proyectos de analítica avanzada en Supply Chain
Horario de 9h a 18h
Modelo híbrido de trabajo
Trabajo con equipos multidisciplinares internacionales
Retribución flexible

Title: Data Analyst - Alternance
Description:
Référence 26000EOM

Vos missions au quotidien

Basé dans nos locaux à Boulogne-Billancourt ( 92 ) au sein de la Direction de la direction Marketing .Vous aurez pour mission de développer de nouveaux outils (dashboards Tableau et modèles de machine learning) afin d’apporter de la connaissance, et faciliter le pilotage de l’activité Marketing Epargne.

Dans le cadre de vos missions, vous serez amené à :


Concevoir et assurer la maintenance de dashboards de pilotage de l’activité commerciale et marketing (via Tableau Software)
Réalisation d’études statistiques et des analyses de connaissance clie
Construction de modèle de machine learning (segmentation, scoring, prévision)
Participer activement aux réflexions sur l’optimisation des processus, des architectures et des pratiques de développement


Et si c’était vous ?

Vous recherchez une alternance d’une durée d’un an à partir de août 2026 dans le cadre d’un Bac+5 en statistique, data ou économétrie , science de la donnée, exploration et modélisation statistique, datamining, big data. Vous avez déjà eu une première expérience dans la Data à travers un stage, un projet scolaire ou un mémoire.

Vous maîtrisez SQL, SAS, Python ainsi que les outils de datavisualisation (Power BI, Tableau, Looker…) et PowerPoint. Vous avez également des notions en bourse et épargne..

Vous êtes reconnu(e) pour votre proactivité, votre écoute active, votre capacité d’analyse et votre enthousiasme

Alors rejoignez-nous ! Si votre candidature est sélectionnée, vous serez invité(e) à réaliser une vidéo de présentation. Nous vous contacterons ensuite pour un entretien.

Durée de l'alternance : 1 an

Date de début : Août 2026

Lieu : Boulogne-Billancourt (92)

Pourquoi nous choisir ?

BoursoBank, filiale du groupe Société Générale, est pionnière et leader sur ses deux activités : la banque en ligne et l’information économique et financière sur Internet avec son Portail Boursorama.com.

Avec son positionnement unique de banque-média et un développement centré sur les nouvelles attentes des clients (autonomie, simplicité, accès à l’information, sécurité et frais réduits), BoursoBank compte aujourd’hui plus 8 millions de clients et conforte, d’année en année, sa position d’acteur bancaire majeur en France.

Engagée à simplifier la banque, à donner du pouvoir d’achat à ses clients et à offrir à chacun la faculté d’agir sur son argent, BoursoBank a aussi l’ambition de participer de façon active et responsable à la transition écologique de ses clients en plus de celle qu’elle opère pour son propre fonctionnement. C’est grâce et avec l’ensemble de ses équipes, sensibilisées et actives à l’optimisation de l’impact environnemental et social quel que soit leur métier, que BoursoBank est certifiée B Corp depuis février 2023 et labélisée Numérique Responsable de niveau 2 depuis mars 2025.

BoursoBank a également à cœur d’assurer le bien-être de ses équipes en proposant divers avantages à travers notamment un accord d’intéressement motivant, un plan d’Epargne Entreprise, un plan d’épargne Retraite et une prise en charge à plus de 60 % de la mutuelle et des tickets restaurant.

Et parce que la diversité est une force, BoursoBank s’engage à offrir des opportunités inclusives et accueillantes pour tous.

Envie de prendre part à cette aventure professionnelle enrichissante et construire un projet professionnel solide ?

Rejoignez vos 1 000 futurs collègues dans nos locaux de Boulogne-Billancourt (92) ou, pour certains de nos métiers, de Nancy (54), pour faire partie d’une équipe où les talents de chacun sont développés !

Title: Data Analyst Italy
Description:
Reports to: Digital CRM & Media Director Italy

Contract Type: Permanent

Location: Milan, Italy

About The Role

As digitalization accelerates across all divisions, our business model increasingly relies on seamless digital and physical client journeys. To support this transformation, we are seeking a Data Analyst to join our Digital Team in Italy.

Your mission: leverage data for actionable client insights that empower all our divisions and markets.

Key Responsibilities



Collect and analyze data from multiple sources
Create clear dashboards and reports to present analytical findings
Interpret results to identify key trends, opportunities, and challenges
Collaborate closely with Divisions to understand data needs and develop tailored analyses and solutions
Maintain and enhance data systems and analytical tools to ensure accuracy and relevance
Conduct complex analyses and synthesize findings into impactful, decision-ready reporting



Required Qualifications



Bachelor’s or master’s degree in business, Economics, Data Analytics, Marketing, or related field
2–4 years of experience in a data/analytics role, ideally in retail, luxury, or CRM
Strong proficiency in Excel and Power BI
Familiarity with Salesforce and Imagino
Fluent in English; Italian is a strong plus



Desired Skills & Attributes



Analytical mindset with strong attention to detail and data accuracy
Ability to synthesize complex data into clear, actionable insights for diverse stakeholders
Strong communication skills and ability to train non-technical users
Comfortable managing multiple deliverables across three Divisions simultaneously
Proactive, autonomous, and passionate about the luxury industry

Title: Data Analyst Intern
Description:
Glovo is part of the Delivery Hero Group, the world’s pioneering local delivery platform, our mission is to deliver an amazing experience—fast, easy, and to your door. We operate in around 65 countries worldwide. Headquartered in Berlin, Germany. Delivery Hero has been listed on the Frankfurt Stock Exchange since 2017 and is part of the MDAX stock market index.

Job Description

YOUR MISSION

You will be at the heart of Glovo's Global Logistics and Operations team, owning the data work behind how we design, test, and optimize the order compensation structures that reward our riders across 23 countries. This is one of the highest impact areas in the company, rider payments are our largest operational cost, and every model you build or experiment you run translates directly into business outcomes at scale.

The environment is fast moving. Priorities shift and ad hoc requests come in regularly. This internship is built for someone curious, hands-on, and eager to learn how real operational decisions get made with data. You will be paired with experienced analysts who will mentor you along the way.

THE JOURNEY


Support the team in modelling and reviewing order payment structures that improve engagement and operational efficiency.
You will be using SQL to query large datasets to surface trends, answer business questions quickly, and build the analytical foundation for strategic decisions.
Translate findings into action. You will help present insights to non-technical stakeholders in a way that is concise and convincing.
Support the design and running of experiments that validate setting changes before global rollout. You will be exposed to the full cycle: design, execution, and analysis.
Leverage AI in your workflow. You will actively incorporate AI tools (agents, copilots, and LLM-assisted analysis) into your day to day work to move faster and work smarter.
Monitor and iterate. Track KPIs, spot anomalies, and make data driven adjustments on an ongoing basis.
Understand the rider perspective to ensure models are fair, locally relevant, and operationally sound.


Qualifications

WHAT YOU WILL BRING TO THE RIDE


Recently completed a degree or master's in Engineering, Mathematics, Statistics, Economics, or another quantitative field.
SQL is essential. You can write queries to pull and filter data, join tables, and aggregate results on your own.
Python familiarity. You can use pandas (or similar) to manipulate data and automate simple tasks.
Excel / spreadsheet familiarity. You know your way around formulas, pivot tables, and structuring data cleanly.
Strong problem-solving instincts. You can take an ambiguous question, break it down, and work toward a clear answer without waiting to be told exactly how.
Fluent English is a must. You communicate clearly to both technical and non-technical audiences.


Nice-to-haves


Hands-on experience leveraging AI in your workflow: copilots, agents, or LLM-assisted analysis used to move faster and work smarter.
Exposure to data visualization tools (Looker, Tableau, Power BI).
Any exposure to experimentation (A/B testing, switchback tests, or similar).


We Believe Driven Talent Deserves


🍔 Monthly Glovo credit to satisfy your cravings!
💳 Discounts on transportation, food, and even kindergarten expenses.

HTML:
Glovo is part of the Delivery Hero Group, the world’s pioneering local delivery platform, our mission is to deliver an amazing experience—fast, easy, and to your door. We operate in around 65 countries worldwide. Headquartered in Berlin, Germany. Delivery Hero has been listed on the Frankfurt Stock Exchange since 2017 and is part of the MDAX stock market index.

Job Description

YOUR MISSION

You will be at the heart of Glovo's Global Logistics and Operations team, owning the data work behind how we design, test, and optimize the order compensation structures that reward our riders across 23 countries. This is one of the highest impact areas in the company, rider payments are our largest operational cost, and every model you build or experiment you run translates directly into business outcomes at scale.

The environment is fast moving. Priorities shift and ad hoc requests come in regularly. This internship is built for someone curious, hands-on, and eager to learn how real operational decisions get made with data. You will be paired with experienced analysts who will mentor you along the way.

THE JOURNEY

  • Support the team in modelling and reviewing order payment structures that improve engagement and operational efficiency.
  • You will be using SQL to query large datasets to surface trends, answer business questions quickly, and build the analytical foundation for strategic decisions.
  • Translate findings into action. You will help present insights to non-technical stakeholders in a way that is concise and convincing.
  • Support the design and running of experiments that validate setting changes before global rollout. You will be exposed to the full cycle: design, execution, and analysis.
  • Leverage AI in your workflow. You will actively incorporate AI tools (agents, copilots, and LLM-assisted analysis) into your day to day work to move faster and work smarter.
  • Monitor and iterate. Track KPIs, spot anomalies, and make data driven adjustments on an ongoing basis.
  • Understand the rider perspective to ensure models are fair, locally relevant, and operationally sound.

Qualifications

WHAT YOU WILL BRING TO THE RIDE

  • Recently completed a degree or master's in Engineering, Mathematics, Statistics, Economics, or another quantitative field.
  • SQL is essential. You can write queries to pull and filter data, join tables, and aggregate results on your own.
  • Python familiarity. You can use pandas (or similar) to manipulate data and automate simple tasks.
  • Excel / spreadsheet familiarity. You know your way around formulas, pivot tables, and structuring data cleanly.
  • Strong problem-solving instincts. You can take an ambiguous question, break it down, and work toward a clear answer without waiting to be told exactly how.
  • Fluent English is a must. You communicate clearly to both technical and non-technical audiences.

Nice-to-haves

  • Hands-on experience leveraging AI in your workflow: copilots, agents, or LLM-assisted analysis used to move faster and work smarter.
  • Exposure to data visualization tools (Looker, Tableau, Power BI).
  • Any exposure to experimentation (A/B testing, switchback tests, or similar).

We Believe Driven Talent Deserves

  • 🍔 Monthly Glovo credit to satisfy your cravings!
  • 💳 Discounts on transportation, food, and even kindergarten expenses.
  • 🏊 Discounted gym memberships to keep you energized.

  • 👪 Enhanced parental leave, and office-based nursery.

Title: Data Analyst (H/F)
Description:
BBDO Paris recherche un ou une Data Analyst pour rejoindre l'équipe Marketing Science




Vos missions :

Rattaché(e) à la Head of Data, vous intervenez sur l'ensemble de la chaîne de valeur data, de la collecte à l'analyse et à la restitution des résultats.

Vous serez amené(e) à :

Analyser des données marketing et digitales afin d'identifier des opportunités d'optimisation et de croissance.
Concevoir et maintenir des tableaux de bord et dispositifs de reporting pour nos clients.
Accompagner les projets de tracking, de mesure et d'analytics (plan de marquage, qualité des données, web analytics).
Réaliser des analyses ad hoc, études d'audience et évaluations de performance.
Présenter vos analyses et recommandations auprès des équipes internes et des clients.
Contribuer au développement de nouvelles méthodologies et à l'intégration des outils d'IA dans nos pratiques data.




Votre profil :

Diplômé(e) d’une école d’ingénieur généraliste ou de commerce, vous justifiez d'une première expérience de 2 à 4 ans dans l’analyse de données ou de mesure de la performance digitale, acquise en agence, cabinet de conseil ou chez l'annonceur.




Les compétences recherchées :

Solide esprit analytique, rigueur et capacité de synthèse.
Excellentes capacités de communication écrite et orale.
Capacité à transformer des données complexes en recommandations claires, actionnables et adaptées aux enjeux business des clients.
Expérience de mise en œuvre ou d'exploitation d'outils d'analytics et de mesure digitale (web/app analytics, UX analytics, TMS, A/B testing, personnalisation).
Expérience de création de tableaux de bord et de restitution d'insights à destination d'équipes métier ou de clients.
Une expérience des langages SQL et/ou Python est appréciée.
Culture digitale et marketing développée ; une sensibilité aux enjeux social media est un plus.
Curiosité pour les nouvelles technologies, l'IA et l'innovation appliquée à la donnée.

Vous évoluez avec aisance au sein d'équipes pluridisciplinaires et prenez plaisir à collaborer avec des experts data, conseil, stratégie et social media pour répondre à des problématiques marketing complexes.




Type de contrat : CDI à pourvoir au 1er Juillet 2026

Rémunération : 32k€-35k€ bruts annuels, selon niveau d’expérience (minimum 2 ans)

Avantages : Participation, Tickets Restaurant, Prise en charge 50% de l'abonnement de transport , Avantages CSE

Télétravail : jusqu'à deux jours par semaine




Le petit plus ? De beaux bureaux dogfriendly au coeur du 17ème arrondissement de Paris




Join Nice & Talented people to do big things.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Application Deadline: 7 July 2026

Department: Document Control

Location: Dundalk



Description

Team Overview:
The Document Control department ensures the efficient management, organisation, and distribution of all project-related documentation. This team maintains version control, tracks approvals, and ensures that the latest documents are readily accessible to all stakeholders across the project lifecycle. By upholding strict document management protocols, the department supports compliance, enhances collaboration, and helps maintain consistency and accuracy across all project communications.

Role Overview:
Reporting to the Senior Data Analyst, you will play a key role in the Data Analytics Team, supporting the preparation of accurate reporting and actionable insights. You will be joining a high-impact team experiencing significant growth and visibility across the business.
The ideal candidate will be responsible for managing and analysing data to support business development, operational efficiency, and strategic decision-making. This role requires a strong analytical mindset, proficiency in data tools, and the ability to communicate insights effectively to both technical and non-technical stakeholders.

Job Title| Data Analyst
Location| Dundalk
Hours| Monday-Thursday 8am-5pm and Friday 8am-3pm
Salary| Competitive Depending on Experience
Responsible to| Senior Data Analyst


What You'll be Doing

Experienced in collecting, cleaning, transforming, and preparing data using Excel, Power Query, Power BI, and other analytical tools to ensure accurate and reliable reporting. Skilled in integrating data from multiple sources, conducting detailed analysis to identify trends, anomalies, and actionable insights, and applying statistical techniques to support business decision-making. Adept at collaborating with senior leaders and stakeholders to gather reporting requirements and translate them into meaningful dashboards and visualisations. Experienced in designing, developing, and maintaining interactive Power BI reports, delivering training and “Show & Tell” sessions, facilitating discussions to resolve reporting challenges, and supporting continuous improvement initiatives through ad hoc analysis, data documentation, and the development of reporting standards.

Title: Data Analyst Junior
Description:
VINCLE, consultora tecnológica en expansión, busca incorporar para un importante proyecto con gran multinacional de gran consumo, un Data Analyst Junior.




Te incorporarás a uno de los proyectos más pioneros del mercado, basado en la automatización de la demanda y de los suministros. Centrado en elaborar la previsión de la demanda y asegurar que el nivel de stock es el que se establece desde la dirección del departamento. Todo centralizado en departamentos de Supply y Demand.




🚀 FUNCIONES PRINCIPALES:

- Responsable del seguimiento y análisis de los KPI's clave del proyecto: Forecast Accuracy, Cambios, OSA, Freshness, Ventas.

- Interlocución con los departamentos de sistemas y gestión de incidencias relacionadas con el Forecast como principal input de la generación de los pedidos.

- Mantenimiento de los parámetros necesarios para el correcto dimensionamiento de los pedidos.

- Animación y seguimiento del forecast así como tareas que garantizan el nivel de servicio.




🔎 ¿Qué buscamos?:

Formación Superior en ADE, Ingenieria Industrial, Ingenieria Quimica, Grado en Matemáticas o similar.
Deseable haber realizado Erasmus o estancias en el extranjero por estudios o trabajo.
Experiencia de 1 año en puesto similar sobre todo en empresa de gran consumo y en análisis de datos.
Se valorará experiencia previa en departamentos de logística, demand.
Dominio avanzado de excel (tablas dinámicas, macros, formulación, ...)
Muy valorable conocimientos de herramientas de análisis estadístico: Matlab, SAS, Revolution Analytics, BI, ...
Capacidad de análisis, gestión y resolución.
Actitud positiva ante situaciones complejas.
Alto nivel de aprendizaje y compromiso con el trabajo.
Persona con entusiasmo, ganas de trabajar y actitud positiva.




✨ ¿Qué ofrecemos?

Horario: 40 horas semanale de Lunes a Viernes: L – J 9:00h a 18:30h y V 8:30h a 15:00h
Contrato Indefinido.
Ubicación del puesto: centro Barcelona.
Incorporación inmediata.

Title: Data Analyst (m/f/d)
Description:
InPost Group is an innovative European out of home deliveries company, revolutionizing the way parcels are delivered to customers. With operations across several countries, our network of intelligent lockers provides customers with a fast, convenient, and secure delivery option. InPost Group is a publicly traded company, with a market capitalization of about $5 billion as of March 2023. With over 10,000 people worldwide, InPost Group is one of the largest out of home delivery providers in Europe, committed to providing sustainable and efficient delivery solutions to meet the evolving needs of customers in today's rapidly changing landscape.





Job Description



In this role, you will be responsible for delivering high‑quality data, insights, and analytics that empower managers to make accurate, data‑driven product, campaign, and operational decisions. You will help optimize processes—such as merchant onboarding and servicing within the Pay product—by identifying trends, anomalies, and risks to support more effective (cost‑ and time‑efficient, and occasionally PR‑sensitive) management of the area.



Key Responsibilities



Power BI Reporting & Dashboard Management

Prepare, develop, and maintain Power BI reports (transactional and operational dashboards) to support effective decision‑making in product development, deployments, and marketing activities.



End‑to‑End Analytical Support

Conduct comprehensive analyses, interpret complex datasets, and present clear, concise conclusions and recommendations to internal and external stakeholders.



Trend & Anomaly Detection

Identify trends, patterns, deviations, and anomalies (e.g., campaign performance, transaction result outliers) and communicate findings to relevant teams for managerial action.



Data & Dashboard Optimization Activities

Participate in projects aimed at refining the maintenance, aggregation, and structuring of dashboards to improve efficiency and workspace organization within the Pay team.



Analytical Contribution to Business Cases

Support the development of business cases—including those requiring large or multi‑source datasets—for budgeting, financial planning, and strategic initiatives.



Business Analysis for Non‑Reported Areas

Perform business analyses for sales, product, growth, payments, or operational topics where no ongoing reporting exists or where data retrieval requires advanced database querying.



Coordination Between Data & Business Teams

Facilitate collaboration between Data and Business teams to streamline planning processes, enhance Data team understanding of business needs, and accelerate implementation timelines.





Qualifications


Proven ability to work with large datasets, including advanced SQL skills, query optimization, and reporting process automation (including VBA).
Experience with Business Intelligence tools, especially Power BI (Power Query, DAX).
Basic programming knowledge in Python (including PySpark).
Strong working knowledge of Databricks or similar platforms.
Highly developed analytical thinking and problem‑solving mindset.
Ability to translate data into actionable insights and business‑oriented recommendations.
Skill in creating clear, logical, user‑friendly, and visually appealing data visualizations and reports.
Solid understanding of statistics and mathematics at a practical level.


Additional Information



​​​​​​Why Join InPost?



The option to work from the office or 100% remotely
Opportunity to work in a diverse, international and cross-functional environment, along with leading experts.
Fulfilling careers with a range of benefits and invests in providing training opportunities for their development.
Involvement in technology monitoring and choices
Your impact will be visible instantly and you will be making a difference in our users lives
We offer B2B type of contract

Title: Assistant Data Analyst (H/F) - Alternance
Description:
Envie de mettre la Data au service d’une expérience client toujours plus performante ? Rejoignez Allianz France pour accompagner les équipes et mesurer l’impact réel de leurs initiatives.

Vous intégrerez l’équipe de Stratégie Expérience Client en charge d’appuyer les initiatives de l’ensemble des équipes de la Direction de l’Expérience Client (parcours digitaux, marketing relationnel, satisfaction, etc) pour les aider à renforcer leur connaissance client et mesurer les impacts des initiatives prises sur la qualité de l’expérience client dans une vision data-driven.

Et pour en arriver là, on s’entoure uniquement de collaborateurs qui placent le sérieux au-dessus de tout.

Alors oui, c’est pénible les gens trop sérieux. Sauf quand ils vous assurent.

A Ce Titre, Vos Missions Seront Les Suivantes

Première tâche et non des moindre : Prendre l’humour très au sérieux.


Participer aux analyses stratégiques pour cadrer et prioriser les initiatives en cours et à venir d’amélioration de l’expérience client
Appuyer les équipes dans leur connaissance fine des clientèles et leurs pratiques et usages pour déterminer et prioriser les actions à mener
Identifier des logiques de segmentation de clientèle au regard de leurs pratiques et usages pour identifier des cibles prioritaires
Produire des indicateurs de suivi de la qualité de l’expérience client
Etre force de proposition pour enrichir l’expérience client
Participer à la construction des actions de communication sur les sujets expérience client


Atouts Du Poste


Être au cœur des enjeux digitaux et expérience client dans le secteur de l’assurance,
Vision très transverse de l’entreprise, l’expérience client interagit avec tous les métiers,
Etre en lien direct avec les équipes métier et suivre l’opérationnalisation concrète de plans d’actions définis grâce aux analyses data,
Travail en autonomie.


Votre Profil

Ecole et expérience :


Master 2 Ecole de Commerce ou en Ecole d’ingénieur avec une spécialisation Data et/ou Analyse de données


Compétences


Bonne connaissance de Python / SQL et Excel pour manipuler un grand nombre de données. Cependant ces outils ne doivent jamais être utilisés pour faire quoi que ce soit de drôle.
La maîtrise de Spark est un plus
Goût pour la programmation et l’automatisation
Très bonnes qualités rédactionnelles, d’analyse et de synthèse
Connaissance des bonnes pratiques liées à l’UX design
Rigueur et organisation
Volonté de monter en compétences sur la gestion de projet
Capacité d’écoute et de synthèse
Bon relationnel qui permettrait d’interagir avec les différentes équipes
Autonomie et proactivité
Une compréhension de base de l'intelligence artificielle (IA) et de ses applications potentielles dans votre métier est précieuse.

Et pour en arriver là, on s’entoure uniquement de collaborateurs qui placent le sérieux au-dessus de tout.

Alors oui, c’est pénible les gens trop sérieux. Sauf quand ils vous assurent.

A Ce Titre, Vos Missions Seront Les Suivantes

Première tâche et non des moindre : Prendre l’humour très au sérieux.

  • Appuyer les équipes dans leur connaissance fine des clientèles et leurs pratiques et usages pour déterminer et prioriser les actions à mener
  • Identifier des logiques de segmentation de clientèle au regard de leurs pratiques et usages pour identifier des cibles prioritaires
  • Produire des indicateurs de suivi de la qualité de l’expérience client
  • Etre force de proposition pour enrichir l’expérience client

  • Travail en autonomie.

Votre Profil

Ecole et expérience :

  • Master 2 Ecole de Commerce ou en Ecole d’ingénieur avec une spécialisation Data et/ou Analyse de données

Compétences

  • Bonne connaissance de Python / SQL et Excel pour manipuler un grand nombre de données. Cependant ces outils ne doivent jamais être utilisés pour faire quoi que ce soit de drôle.
