Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Data Analyst




CHI SIAMO:




SCS Consulting è una società di consulenza di direzione presente sul mercato da più di 40 anni che opera nel campo dei servizi professionali di consulenza, sviluppo delle competenze e valorizzazione delle risorse umane e che punta all’innovazione e al perseguimento della vision strategica dei propri clienti.

SCS Consulting opera su tutto il territorio nazionale, con un team costituito oggi da circa 200 professionisti, cui si affianca un consolidato network di partner per diversi settori specifici, nel supportare imprese e territori con servizi e soluzioni per la creazione di valore e la realizzazione di una crescita sostenibile, attraverso la visione integrata delle proprie aree di competenza: consulenza strategica e organizzativa, change management e sostenibilità.







IL PROFILO




In ottica di potenziamento della Divisione Finance, siamo alla ricerca di una/un Data Analyst con solida conoscenza di Excel e Power BI per supportare il nostro team di analisi dati. La risorsa lavorerà su progetti di business intelligence, reportistica e analisi dati, contribuendo allo sviluppo di dashboard e strumenti a supporto delle decisioni aziendali.




TASK PRINCIPALI:




• Analizzare e preparare dati provenienti da diverse fonti aziendali, garantendone qualità e coerenza

• Creare e aggiornare dashboard interattive in Power BI per il monitoraggio delle performance aziendali

• Utilizzare Power Query per la raccolta, trasformazione e integrazione dei dati da fonti multiple

• Sviluppare metriche e KPI in DAX per supportare analisi e decisioni operative

• Strutturare modelli dati funzionali alle analisi, garantendo affidabilità e semplicità di utilizzo

• Ottimizzare la user experience delle dashboard, rendendole intuitive e facilmente fruibili dagli stakeholder

• Pubblicare e gestire report tramite Power BI Service, assicurandone aggiornamento e condivisione

• Sviluppare e ottimizzare report su Excel, utilizzando formule avanzate, tabelle pivot e automazioni

• Collaborare con il team e le funzioni di business per identificare insight utili a supporto delle decisioni

• Documentare processi e logiche di calcolo per garantire la replicabilità delle analisi




Requisiti:




• 2–3 anni di esperienza in ambito data analysis/business intelligence.

• Ottima conoscenza di Excel, tabelle pivot, formule avanzate e Power Query.

• Buona conoscenza di Power BI (creazione di report, power query, modelli dati, DAX, Power BI Service).

• Capacità di trasformare dati in insight chiari e utilizzabili.

• Capacità di analisi e problem-solving, con attenzione ai dettagli.

• Propensione a lavorare in team e a interagire con stakeholder non tecnici.

• Interesse per il mondo della business intelligence e della data analytics.




Completano il profilo:




ottime doti di comunicazione con standing adeguato al ruolo
forte orientamento al team working, alla collaborazione e al supporto dei colleghi, mantenendo sempre un approccio positivo alle situazioni
essere costantemente focalizzato sul risultato, sulla responsabilità individuale e sulla qualità del lavoro
attenzione continua all’eccellenza tecnica ed al dettaglio




COSA OFFRIAMO




Le nostre persone sono il vero motore dell’impresa. SCS Consulting sviluppa la propria crescita attorno alla valorizzazione delle sue persone, investendo in modo continuo sulla loro crescita e percorso professionale, offrendo quindi un inquadramento e retribuzione in linea con esperienze e capacità del candidato, formazione continua e welfare aziendale.




Il candidato, quindi, avrà la concreta opportunità di una crescita professionale e di carriera all’interno di un ambiente dinamico e stimolante che promuove il confronto continuo e la proattività.




Avrà la possibilità di sviluppare un sistema di relazioni professionali attraverso l’interlocuzione con le prime linee di importanti aziende italiane ed internazionali, associazioni di riferimento di SCS, business school.




Inoltre, avrà la possibilità di lavorare in una realtà del mondo della consulenza strategica che basa da sempre il suo approccio di creazione di valore sulla contaminazione di esperienze e competenze di business, people management e sustainability.




COME CANDIDARSI




Inviare CV dettagliato accompagnato da mail di presentazione alla mail:

selezionifinance@scsconsulting.it

riportando nell’oggetto della mail: DataAnalyst_26




La modalità di lavoro prevista è ibrida: 60% di presenza (cliente e/o sede) e 40% di lavoro agile, su base settimanale, in coerenza con le effettive esigenze di progetto. È richiesta disponibilità a trasferte nazionali.




Sede di lavoro: Torino




In SCS crediamo fermamente in un ambiente rispettoso delle differenze, valorizziamo attivamente le unicità di ciascuno, secondo il merito e le competenze con particolare attenzione alla valorizzazione delle diversità di genere : SCS è certificata dal 2023 secondo gli standard stabiliti dalla prassi UNI/PdR 125:2022




https://9009262.fs1.hubspotusercontent-na1.net/hubfs/9009262/UNI_PdR_125-2022-C602570-1-it-IT-20231211-20231211154358.pdf?hsCtaTracking=a2f9cb99-c9ef-4f67-936c-80e51b507b09%7C48df7cc9-a31e-440a-b18d-cab05989be50

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
In 1966, in Marseille, Pierre Bellon laid the foundations of what would become Sodexo, guided by a simple yet powerful idea: improving everyday life through services that truly matter.

Today, Sodexo is a global leader in Food and Services, supporting people at every moment of life, at work, in schools, hospitals and communities. With 426,000 colleagues across 43 countries, we serve 80 million consumers every day, generating €24.1 billion in revenues. Our growth goes hand in hand with social and environmental responsibility, driven by our purpose: to create a better everyday for everyone and build a better life for all.




About Sodexo Business Services (SBS)




Portugal is no exception! Seven years ago, we established Sodexo Business Services (SBS), our shared service center. Here, you will have the opportunity to join various Financial teams such as Record to Report, Order to Cash, Purchase to Pay, Financial Analysis, and Master Data, supporting European markets and ensuring business growth.




Join Us!

We are looking for talented professionals to be part of our team, bringing unique skills and perspectives to help us grow even further.




Data Analyst

The Data Analyst is responsible for the technical execution of reporting and insight that enhances transparency & business performance with particular emphasis on digitization (Power BI), process efficiency & solution




⚙What will you do?

Enhancing our digital reporting suite & ensuring smooth execution of business-critical activities.
Demonstrate strong technical capability across reporting tools,
Accountable for scoping, designing, and delivering reporting developments.
Explore new ways to leverage reporting technologies, with a focus on building advanced proficiency in tools such as Power BI, Power Automate, and Excel.
Ensure that reporting output and architecture is coherent and consistent with our governance framework and standards.
Evaluate reporting processes and recommend improvements to enhance efficiency and reduce non‑value‑adding analysis.
Engage proactively with stakeholders to deliver reporting and insights, building effective business partnerships and communicating data in a clear, relatable narrative.
Translate broad or unclear stakeholder requirements into structured reporting plans and deliverables.
Be able to shift between data modelling, content creation, commercial insight & business partnering.




🤙What You Need to Succeed:

Bachelor’s degree in relevant field
Fluency in English required, ability to speak other European language is a plus
Focus on produt excellence, integrity and costumer focus
Experiency in Power BI, Power Automate, and Excel.
Continuous improvement mindset
Communication skills
Problem solving
Be flexible and versatile




What we have to offer you?

Hybrid working model;
Flexible working hours;
Health & Life Insurance;
Meal allowance paid in Meal Card;
Additional Days off: extra vacation day, employee’s birthday, volunteering day;
Opportunity to grow professionally inside the Company;
Possibility to participate in multicultural projects;
Several internal activities aiming to promote our team´s wellbeing.




📩 Apply now! Send your CV to Recruitment.SBS.Portugal@sodexo.com with the job title in the subject line. Explore all our career opportunities here: Sodexo Careers.

Learn more about Sodexo: YouTube - Sodexo.




Diversity & Inclusion Statement

Sodexo is committed to creating a diverse and inclusive work environment and is proud to be an equal opportunity employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment based on merit.




🔒 Data Privacy:

We respect your privacy. Your personal data will be used exclusively for recruitment purposes, processed under GDPR regulations, and treated confidentially. For further details, contact privacy.sbs@sodexo.com.

Title: Junior Data Analyst
Description:
Junior Data Analyst

Location: Cork (Onsite)




We are looking for a motivated and analytical Junior Data Analyst to join our client's growing team in Cork. This is an exciting opportunity for someone early in their data career who enjoys working with Excel, analysing business information and helping to improve reporting processes.

Reporting to the senior leadership team, you will play a key role in transforming business data into meaningful insights.




Key Responsibilities

Data Analysis & Reporting

Collect, analyse and interpret business data to support operational and strategic decision-making.
Develop and maintain reports using Microsoft Excel.
Manipulate and analyse large datasets using advanced Excel functions including Pivot Tables, XLOOKUPs and Power Query.
Work with data extracted from SQL databases.
Identify trends, patterns and opportunities for process improvement.
Support the future implementation and development of Power BI dashboards and reporting.




Technology Coordination

Act as the primary onsite contact for day-to-day technology coordination.
Liaise with the outsourced IT provider to ensure support requests are managed effectively.
Coordinate basic hardware requests and user equipment.
Assist with user onboarding and technology-related administration.
Support continuous improvement across business systems.




Essential Skills

Strong Microsoft Excel skills, including Pivot Tables, XLOOKUPs, formulas and data manipulation.
Excellent analytical and problem-solving abilities.
High attention to detail.
Strong communication and organisational skills.
Ability to work independently and manage multiple priorities.




Desirable Skills

Exposure to SQL or SQL databases.
Experience with Power BI or a strong desire to learn.
Knowledge of Microsoft 365.
Experience working with business reporting or operational data.
Experience coordinating with third-party technology providers.

Title: Junior Data Analyst
Description:
About us – Wewo Telecommunication Italy S.r.l.




Wewo Telecommunication Italy S.r.l. is part of the international Vivo group, a global leader in the production of smartphones and smart devices.

In Italy, we collaborate with major retail and distribution partners, delivering innovative technologies within a dynamic and international environment.




The Role




We are looking for a motivated and analytical Junior Data Analyst to support business performance monitoring and data-driven decision-making across the organization.

The role will be responsible for collecting, analyzing, and interpreting data from multiple sources to provide actionable insights and support business stakeholders. The Junior Data Analyst will work closely with Retail, Sales, Marketing, GTM, and Finance teams to monitor key performance indicators, sales trends, stock levels, promotional activities, and overall business performance.

This position has a direct impact on reporting accuracy, business planning, operational efficiency, and the identification of opportunities to improve retail performance and commercial results in Italy.




Main Responsibilities




Collect, clean, and analyze data from multiple sources.
Prepare recurring and ad-hoc reports for business stakeholders.
Monitor retail business performance indicators and support KPI tracking.
Analyze sell-in and sell-out performance across retail channels.
Track sales performance by product, model, retailer, region, and promoter.
Monitor stock levels, inventory aging, product availability, and replenishment trends.
Support retail visibility and merchandising performance analysis.
Develop dashboards and data visualizations.
Identify trends, anomalies, and opportunities through data analysis.
Support forecasting and business planning activities.
Collaborate with Retail, Sales, Marketing, GTM, and Finance teams.
Monitor promotional campaign performance and support ROI analysis.
Ensure data accuracy and consistency across reports and systems.
Assist in process automation and reporting optimization initiatives.




Requirements




Bachelor's Degree in Statistics, Mathematics, Economics, Engineering, Computer Science, Business Analytics, or related fields.
1-2 years of experience in Data Analysis, Business Analysis, Reporting, or similar roles.

Title: Finance & Data Analyst
Description:
Somos un grupo de empresas en plena transformación financiera y buscamos a alguien que quiera crecer dentro de un proyecto real, con impacto desde el primer día.

Si tienes ganas de aprender, te orientas al dato y quieres dar el salto a cliente final, queremos conocerte.




¿Qué harás?

Dar soporte en la planificación financiera, presupuestos y forecasting del grupo.
Construir y mantener modelos financieros y cuadros de mando para apoyar la toma de decisiones.
Colaborar en el diseño y automatización del reporting financiero, incorporando herramientas de datos.
Analizar rentabilidad de proyectos internos.
Trabajar de forma transversal con distintas áreas del negocio.
Apoyar en el cumplimiento normativo, fiscal y coordinación de auditorías.




¿Qué buscamos?

Formación en Finanzas, ADE, Economía o similar, valorable.
Al menos 2 años de experiencia en entornos financieros. Se valorará positivamente experiencia en auditoría, aunque no es un requisito.
Manejo de herramientas de análisis y datos: Excel avanzado, Power BI o similares.
Más que el recorrido, buscamos actitud: alguien con ganas de aprender, proactividad y capacidad de entender el negocio más allá del número.




¿Qué ofrecemos?

Incorporación a un proyecto con recorrido real, en plena expansión, donde tendrás visibilidad y aprendizaje constante.
Acompañamiento y desarrollo dentro de un equipo financiero en pleno crecimiento.
Salario en el rango de 25.000€ – 30.000€ en función del perfil.
Oportunidades de formación continua.




Somos una empresa comprometida con la igualdad de oportunidades y la diversidad en el equipo.

Title: Data Analyst - Team Intelligence
Description:
At Voyado, hundreds of retailers rely on dashboards, exports and shared analytics experiences to understand their business and make decisions. Behind every metric, dashboard and export sits a series of product decisions: what should be measured, how it should be defined, and how it should work consistently across customers, markets and products.

We’re now looking for Data Analysts to join Team Intelligence in Stockholm and help shape the analytics products our customers will rely on for years to come.

About The Role

As a Data Analyst in Team Intelligence, you will work with analytics products that scale across Voyado’s product portfolio. You will help harmonise how data, KPIs and analytics surfaces work across products such as dashboards, BI Export and DeltaShare.

Your focus will be on building analytics experiences that are accurate, consistent and useful for many customers — not creating one-off reports for individual stakeholders.

You’ll work closely with Product Managers, Analytics Engineers and domain teams to understand customer needs, investigate data discrepancies, define shared metrics and contribute to the broader product roadmap for analytics across Voyado.

Who you are

You have a product mindset and see dashboards, metrics and analytics experiences as products that need ownership and continuous improvement. You’re comfortable working closely with Product Managers, engineers and other stakeholders, and you enjoy creating clarity where definitions, data or expectations differ.

You’re curious, analytical and enjoy understanding how data works beneath the surface. When numbers don’t add up, you want to find out why.

Most importantly, you care about building analytics that people can trust. We'd love to see that you also have:


Experience working as a Data Analyst, BI Analyst or in a similar analytics-focused role.
Strong SQL skills.
Experience with BI tools such as Sigma, Looker, Tableau, Power BI or similar.
Experience owning dashboards, reports or data products over time.
Ability to translate business and customer questions into clear data requirements.
Strong communication skills and experience working with multiple stakeholders.


High Impact. Great People. Real Growth.

You’ll join a company with strong momentum, modern technology, and a collaborative culture where ideas and initiatives are encouraged.

At Voyado, we care about creating an environment where people can grow, collaborate, and enjoy the journey together. We move quickly, challenge ideas, and work closely across teams to deliver meaningful impact.

Some of our benefits:


30 days of vacation
Wellness allowance
Personal development opportunities
Summits and team activities


Four days from the office and one day from home

About Voyado

Voyado is the Agentic Customer Experience Suite for retail.

More than 500 retail brands across 100 markets use Voyado to unite CRM, loyalty, product discovery, e-commerce personalization, and retail media with retail-trained AI that acts on real-time shopper intent.

By combining customer data with product intelligence, Voyado helps retailers deliver more relevant experiences across every channel — online and in-store. The result is more traffic, higher conversions, stronger customer relationships, and measurable business impact.

We’re growing across Europe and are looking for people who want to help shape the future of retail technology.

Ready to make an impact?

Fantastic! 🎉

A quick note about our recruitment timeline: As many of us will be taking some well-deserved summer vacation, this process will move a bit slower than usual.

We’d still love to hear from you as soon as possible! We’ll be holding initial screening conversations before July 13, after which we’ll take a short summer break. First-round interviews will begin from July 27 onwards.

Thank you for your understanding, and we hope you’ll apply! 🌞

Additional information

As part of our recruitment process, we conduct a background check on the final candidate.

Making it personal with AI

We believe you’ll position yourself best for success in the interview process by being yourself, with support from AI where it makes sense. Later in the process, you may even be expected to use AI. At the end of the day, what matters is meeting you — the person behind the application.

There is no such thing as a perfect candidate; we’re all human, so please bring your authentic self. Don’t let AI over-polish your CV or answers to the point where we can’t hear your real voice, because that’s what we care about most.

In today’s hyper-competitive market, we help over 500 retail brands across 100 markets attract, convert, and retain loyal customers.

With AI-driven CRM, loyalty, e-commerce personalization, and retail media, Voyado turns every interaction - online or in-store - into lasting growth.

By combining customer data with product intelligence, we empower retailers to deliver the right message, product, or offer at exactly the right time. The result? Stronger customer relationships and measurable business impact.

Title: Data Analyst – Risk, Controls & Reporting
Description:
About Company:

Synechron is a global technology consulting firm that helps leading organizations accelerate digital transformation through innovation, expertise, and agility. With more than 16,500 professionals across around 60 offices in over 20 countries, we combine deep industry knowledge with advanced capabilities in AI, cloud, cybersecurity, and data engineering. Our regional teams, supported by strategic delivery centers, provide scalable, cost-efficient solutions tailored to local markets. Through our award-winning Synechron FinLabs accelerators and strategic partnerships with AWS, Microsoft, Databricks, Salesforce, and ServiceNow, we enable clients to innovate fast and lead with confidence. For more information on the company, please visit our website or LinkedIn community.




Diversity, Equity, and Inclusion

Diversity & Inclusion are fundamental to our culture, and Synechron is proud to be an equal opportunity workplace and an affirmative-action employer. Our Diversity, Equity, and Inclusion (DEI) initiative ‘Same Difference’ is committed to fostering an inclusive culture – promoting equality, diversity and an environment that is respectful to all. We strongly believe that a diverse workforce helps build stronger, successful businesses as a global company. We encourage applicants from across diverse backgrounds, race, ethnicities, religion, age, marital status, gender, sexual orientations, or disabilities to apply. We empower our global workforce by offering flexible workplace arrangements, mentoring, internal mobility, learning and development programs, and more.

All employment decisions at Synechron are based on business needs, job requirements and individual qualifications, without regard to the applicant’s gender, gender identity, sexual orientation, race, ethnicity, disabled or veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by law.




Job Description:




We’re looking for a Technology Risk Data Analyst to join our team and play a key role in enhancing Technology, Cyber, and Data Risk reporting and metrics. This is a great opportunity for someone who enjoys working with large datasets, challenging existing processes, and driving smarter decision-making through analytics.




What you’ll be doing




Improve Technology, Cyber, and Data Risk metrics and reporting using insights from multiple data sources, research, and stakeholder feedback
Identify new reporting requirements and help implement enhanced risk and control MI
Respond to management information requests and analyze available data to generate meaningful insights
Challenge and improve existing risk and control metrics through a structured, data-driven approach
Work closely with data engineers to assess data relevance and improve reporting quality
Support change and transformation initiatives to strengthen risk and control management across the organization
Collaborate with global teams to solve complex business and risk challenges.




What we’re looking for




3+ years of experience in data analytics
Strong experience analyzing large and complex datasets
Ability to turn data into clear, actionable insights for senior management
Good hands-on knowledge of SQL, Python, and Excel
Experience with data visualization/reporting tools such as Qlik or Power BI
Understanding of Technology Risk and Control Management concepts, including:Key Risk Indicators (KRIs)
Key Control Indicators (KCIs)
Key Performance Indicators (KPIs)
Strong problem-solving and stakeholder management skills
Ability to work in a fast-paced, evolving environment
Proven experience collaborating with global teams




Why join us?




Work on high-impact initiatives across technology, cyber, and data risk
Be part of a team focused on transformation, insight, and innovation
Gain exposure to senior stakeholders and strategic reporting
Use your technical and analytical skills to influence real business decisions




If you’re excited about combining analytics, risk, and business impact, we’d love to hear from you.

Title: Data Governance Analyst - Barcelona O Madrid
Description:
En InnoIT Consulting estamos colaborando con una importante compañía internacional del sector asegurador en la búsqueda de un/a Senior Data Governance & Compliance Analyst.




📍 Ubicación: Barcelona o Madrid

🏠 Modalidad: Remoto

🗣️ Inglés: Nivel B2 o superior




¿Qué harás?

• Impulsar iniciativas de Gobierno del Dato y Calidad del Dato.

• Gestionar metadatos, catálogos de datos y diccionarios corporativos.

• Colaborar con equipos de negocio, arquitectura, ingeniería de datos, seguridad y compliance.

• Participar en proyectos de privacidad, protección de datos y cumplimiento normativo (GDPR).

• Contribuir a la evolución del modelo corporativo de datos.

• Trabajar con herramientas como Informatica IDMC y Microsoft Purview.




Requisitos:

• Más de 4 años de experiencia en Data Governance, Data Management, Data Quality o áreas relacionadas.

• Experiencia con Data Catalog, Business Glossary y Metadata Management.

• Conocimientos de Data Stewardship, Data Ownership y Compliance.

• Conocimientos de SQL y modelado de datos.

• Capacidad para interactuar con perfiles técnicos y de negocio.

• Nivel de inglés B2 o superior.




Valorable:

• Certificación CDMP.

• Experiencia en sector financiero o asegurador.

• Power BI.

• Experiencia con Microsoft Purview.




¿Por qué unirte a InnoIT?

💡 Plan de carrera con 1.000 € al año para formación

🏥 Seguro médico privado

🗣️ Clases de idiomas online

💳 Retribución flexible (Cobee)

🤓 Meetups y comunidades tecnológicas internas




¡Únete a nuestro equipo!

Desarrolla tu talento IT. Aprende, crea y disfruta mientras creces profesionalmente con nosotros.

Si quieres conocernos mejor, visita nuestra página web.

Compartir conocimiento está en el corazón de nuestra cultura

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
eir business talent is looking to hire a Data Analyst for a 3-month contract position based in Dublin operating on a hybrid working model (2 days onsite per week)
 
The Data Analyst supports data migration initiatives by defining, validating and governing source-to-target data mappings between legacy systems and target platforms. The role ensures data quality, transformation logic and mapping readiness for ETL development, testing and deployment.

Key Responsibilities

Define and maintain source-to-target data mappings for all in-scope data objects
Analyse legacy system data structures and identify data quality issues, gaps, and inconsistencies
Gather and document target system data requirements in collaboration with stakeholders
Develop and maintain mapping workbooks across defined data domains
Define mapping logic including:direct field mappings,data transformations and business rules,lookup/reference value mappings,default values and calculated fields
Identify and resolve mapping issues such as missing fields, conflicting definitions, and unclear data ownership
Define data object dependencies and sequencing for migration (e.g., master data before transactional data)
Facilitate workshops with SMEs, vendors, and technical teams to validate mapping decisions
Maintain version control and change management for mapping documentation
Ensure all mappings are testable, auditable, and formally signed off
Provide input to ETL design, migration build, and testing planning
 Requirements

Strong data analysis and data profiling skills
Experience with data mapping, data migration, or ETL projects
Understanding of relational databases and data structures
Ability to define and document transformation logic and business rules
Proficiency in Excel (advanced mapping workbooks, lookup tables, data reconciliation)
Familiarity with data quality assessment and cleansing techniques
Exposure to ETL tools (e.g., Informatica, Talend, SSIS, or similar) – desirable
Business & Analytical Skills

Strong analytical and problem-solving skills
Ability to interpret complex legacy data systems
Attention to detail with strong data accuracy focus
Ability to translate business requirements into technical mapping rules
Strong documentation and data governance awareness
Communication & Stakeholder Skills

Experience working with SMEs, IT teams, and vendors
Strong workshop facilitation and requirement gathering skills
Ability to manage ambiguity and conflicting data definitions
Strong written documentation and reporting skills
 
Candidates should hold appropriate RTWs for Ireland i.e. Stamp1G Spousal, Stamp 4, Irish/British/EU passport. 
For more information please apply here or contact harleen.tora@eirbusiness.ie

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Would you like to join an international atmosphere and be involved in projects across Europe and with impact worldwide having the opportunity to consolidate your career? This is your opportunity!




🚀 We are looking for a Data Analyst to join our team!




The Data Analyst will be responsible for designing, developing, and maintaining data models and Power BI dashboards based on Apptio Cost & Financial Data Lake (CFDL) to support Aftersales performance, cost transparency, and financial steering. The role acts as a bridge between Aftersales business stakeholders, Finance, and IT/Data teams, ensuring high‑quality, actionable insights.




🔑 Key Responsibilities




- Develop and maintain Power BI reports and dashboards using data sourced from Apptio CFDL.

- Build and optimize data models (facts, dimensions, measures) aligned with Aftersales KPIs.

- Ensure data accuracy, consistency, and reconciliation with financial and operational sources.

- Perform ad‑hoc analyses to support decision‑making in Aftersales and Finance. Aftersales & Financial Steering

- Translate Aftersales business needs into analytical requirements and KPIs.

- Support cost transparency, profitability, and performance tracking across Aftersales activities.

- Collaborate with Finance to align reporting with financial controlling and cost allocation logic.

- Identify trends, risks, and improvement opportunities through data insights. Apptio / CFDL Ownership

- Act as subject matter expert for Apptio CFDL data structures and logic.

- Coordinate with IT / Data Platform teams on data ingestion, transformations, and enhancements. - Ensure correct usage of Apptio data for reporting and analysis purposes. Stakeholder Management

- Work closely with Aftersales stakeholders to understand reporting needs and priorities.

- Present insights in a clear, business‑oriented way to non‑technical audiences.

- Support users with Power BI adoption, interpretation of reports, and best practices.




🛠 Technical Skills




- Strong experience with Power BI (data modeling, DAX, performance optimization).

- Solid understanding of Apptio / TBM concepts, ideally with exposure to CFDL.

- Experience working with financial and cost data.

- Good knowledge of SQL and data analysis concepts.

- Understanding of data quality, controls, and reconciliation processes.




🌍 Business & Soft Skills




- Strong analytical and problem‑solving mindset.

- Ability to translate complex data into clear business insights.

- Experience working with cross‑functional teams (Aftersales, Finance, IT).

- Structured, detail‑oriented, and proactive way of working.

- Strong communication skills in English (Spanish/German is a plus). Nice to Have

- Experience in Aftersales, Automotive, or Shared Services environments.

- Knowledge of SAP (FI/CO, Aftersales‑related data).

- Experience with cost allocation models and profitability analysis.

- Previous work in international or multi‑country setups. Key Interfaces

- Aftersales Business Teams - Finance / Controlling - IT / Data Platform / Apptio Teams

- Power BI & Reporting Communities Success Indicators

- High adoption and satisfaction of Aftersales Power BI reports.

- Reliable, reconciled, and trusted data from Apptio CFDL.

- Clear contribution to Aftersales cost transparency and performance steering.

- Reduced manual reporting and ad‑hoc data requests.




⭐ Within our benefits as employee, we provide:




60% Mobile work
30 working days of holidays
Annual Training Plan

Title: Data Analyst Trainee
Description:
Sobre o Publicis Groupe,




O Publicis Groupe, o segundo maior grupo de comunicação do mundo, é uma plataforma de soluções que está presente em mais de 100 países e conta com mais de 114.000 colaboradores. Com uma filosofia de trabalho única, conhecida como The Power of One, coloca os clientes no centro, promovendo o crescimento dos seus negócios através de agências criativas como a Publicis, Leo Burnett, Publicis Health, Wysiwyg, Nurun e Digitas, agências de media como a Zenith, Starcom, Spark e Performics, e áreas de conhecimento transversal.




O Publicis Groupe une Criatividade, Tecnologia e Dados, oferecendo uma proposta de valor que liga efetivamente marcas e consumidores.




Estamos à procura de um/a Data & Analytics Trainee para integrar a nossa equipa e apoiar na transformação de dados em insights úteis para o negócio. Nesta função, terás a oportunidade de trabalhar com diferentes fontes de dados, contribuir para a criação de dashboards e relatório. Irás colaborar de perto com várias áreas da empresa, desenvolvendo competências analíticas e uma visão orientada a dados num ambiente dinâmico e de aprendizagem contínua.




Responsabilidades:




Recolha e Limpeza de dados: Auxiliar na extração e tratamento de dados de diferentes fontes. Contacto com provedores de dados para manutenção das fontes de dados.

Reporting: Apoiar na criação e manutenção de dashboards (Power BI, Tableau ou Excel) e outros tipos de relatório.

Análise: Identificar tendências e padrões que ajudem a otimizar processos internos.

Colaboração: Trabalhar em conjunto com as equipas de negócio para perceber as suas necessidades de informação.




Habilitações:




Excel: Domínio sólido de Excel (vlookups, tabelas dinâmicas, etc.).

Pensamento Crítico: Capacidade de questionar os dados e procurar a história por trás dos números.

Línguas: Bom domínio da língua inglesa (escrita e falada).

Valorizamos: Conhecimentos básicos de SQL ou Python e familiaridade com ferramentas de visualização de dados.




Porquê juntar-se a nós?




🚀 Oportunidades de Crescimento: Pode progredir na sua carreira não só através da experiência de trabalhar com grandes clientes, mas também através do acesso a programas de formação locais e globais especializados de acordo com a sua função, abrangendo tanto competências técnicas como soft skills.
📚 Formação online gratuita: Pode aceder a cursos ilimitados dos catálogos LinkedIn Learning e Udemy através da nossa plataforma de inteligência artificial, Marcel.
🥇 Certificações de parceiros: Terá a oportunidade de obter certificações de gigantes da indústria como Meta, Google ou Amazon.
🌍 Trabalhe de qualquer lugar: Teletrabalho até 6 semanas a partir de mais de 100 países com o nosso programa WorkYourWorld.
✨ Equilíbrio entre vida pessoal e profissional: Modelo de trabalho híbrido com 1 dia de home office. Horário de início de trabalho flexível.




No Publicis Groupe, estamos empenhados em criar uma força de trabalho culturalmente diversificada e, por isso, encorajamos vivamente as candidaturas de grupos sub-representados. Estamos empenhados na igualdade de oportunidades e aceitamos candidaturas de todos os indivíduos, independentemente da sua origem. Não discriminamos com base na raça, cor, religião, identidade ou expressão de género, orientação sexual, nacionalidade, idade, deficiência, estado civil ou estatuto de qualquer indivíduo em qualquer grupo ou classe protegida por lei.




Se tiver necessidades de adaptações razoáveis decorrentes de uma deficiência ou condição médica para participar plenamente no processo de recrutamento, fale com o recrutador que o contactar.




Esta oportunidade não oferece apoio à relocalização.

Title: Data analyst
Description:
Dans le cadre de sa politique diversité et inclusion l’AGPM s'engage à étudier toutes les candidatures, à reconnaitre et à promouvoir tous les talents quels que soient leurs croyances, handicap, origine ethnique, sexe, orientation sexuelle, âge ou tout autre critère qui pourrait faire l'objet d'une discrimination.




Au sein de la Direction de l’Organisation des Systèmes d’Information (DOSI), le Pôle Data de la Division Architecture et Data a pour mission de garantir la qualité et la pertinence des données, essentielles pour éclairer les décisions stratégiques de l'entreprise.

Ce Pôle Data est composé de dix collaborateurs répartis au sein de trois services : Data Engineering, Data Usages et Data Gouvernance.

Le Data Analyst du service Data Usages agit en tant qu’assistance à maitrise d’ouvrage Data en traduisant les besoins métiers en spécifications fonctionnelles de données, puis en convertissant ces données complexes en tableaux de bord et recommandations d’aide à la décision pour les Directions Métiers.




A ce titre, les missions seront les suivantes :

· Animer des ateliers avec les utilisateurs finaux pour comprendre leurs problématiques

· Traduire les besoins business en spécifications fonctionnelles

· Définir les dictionnaires de données

· S’assurer de la fiabilité, de la cohérence et de la conformité des données sources

· Faire l'interface avec les data engineer pour le déploiement des flux de données

· Suivre le planning et les livrables du projet

· Extraire et explorer les données pour répondre à des questions ponctuelles

· Concevoir des tableaux de bord d'aide à la décision

· Réaliser les tests fonctionnels

· Rédiger la documentation utilisateur et accompagner les collaborateurs aux nouveaux outils.




Titulaire d’un diplôme de niveau Bac +5 en informatique, vous justifiez d’une expérience professionnelle d’au moins sept ans sur des fonctions similaires. Vous disposez de solides compétences techniques, notamment en gestion de projet AMOA, en requêtes SQL, en gestion de bases de données relationnelles et en SGBD. Vous êtes capable de créer des rapports et de visualisations des données avec Power BI ou Business Objects. La connaissance du langage Python est également requise. Une bonne compréhension du domaine de l’assurance et la capacité à challenger un besoin métier sont fortement appréciées.




Autonome, vous faites preuve d’un fort sens du service et du respect des engagements. Doté d’un bon esprit de synthèse, vous savez prendre du recul et vous remettre en question. Vous appréciez le travail en équipe et contribuez activement à une dynamique collective. Curieux(se), vous avez une forte appétence pour les nouvelles technologies liées à la data.




Ce poste offre l’opportunité d’intervenir sur des problématiques techniques riches et variées dans un contexte de transformation structurant, notamment dans le cadre des synergies entre différentes entités. Vous évoluerez dans un système d’information modernisé, bénéficiant d’expertises solides et de pratiques innovantes favorisant la performance et la montée en compétences continue.




Le poste peut être basé à Aix-en-Provence ou Toulon.




Ce poste est soumis à l’obtention d’une habilitation de sécurité délivrée par les autorités françaises.

Dans le cadre de sa politique diversité et inclusion l’AGPM s'engage à étudier toutes les candidatures, à reconnaitre et à promouvoir tous les talents quels que soient leurs croyances, handicap, origine ethnique, sexe, orientation sexuelle, âge ou tout autre critère qui pourrait faire l'objet d'une discrimination.


Au sein de la Direction de l’Organisation des Systèmes d’Information (DOSI), le Pôle Data de la Division Architecture et Data a pour mission de garantir la qualité et la pertinence des données, essentielles pour éclairer les décisions stratégiques de l'entreprise.

Ce Pôle Data est composé de dix collaborateurs répartis au sein de trois services : Data Engineering, Data Usages et Data Gouvernance.

Le Data Analyst du service Data Usages agit en tant qu’assistance à maitrise d’ouvrage Data en traduisant les besoins métiers en spécifications fonctionnelles de données, puis en convertissant ces données complexes en tableaux de bord et recommandations d’aide à la décision pour les Directions Métiers.


A ce titre, les missions seront les suivantes :

· Animer des ateliers avec les utilisateurs finaux pour comprendre leurs problématiques

· Traduire les besoins business en spécifications fonctionnelles

· Définir les dictionnaires de données

· S’assurer de la fiabilité, de la cohérence et de la conformité des données sources

· Faire l'interface avec les data engineer pour le déploiement des flux de données

· Suivre le planning et les livrables du projet

· Extraire et explorer les données pour répondre à des questions ponctuelles

· Concevoir des tableaux de bord d'aide à la décision

· Réaliser les tests fonctionnels

· Rédiger la documentation utilisateur et accompagner les collaborateurs aux nouveaux outils.


Titulaire d’un diplôme de niveau Bac +5 en informatique, vous justifiez d’une expérience professionnelle d’au moins sept ans sur des fonctions similaires. Vous disposez de solides compétences techniques, notamment en gestion de projet AMOA, en requêtes SQL, en gestion de bases de données relationnelles et en SGBD. Vous êtes capable de créer des rapports et de visualisations des données avec Power BI ou Business Objects. La connaissance du langage Python est également requise. Une bonne compréhension du domaine de l’assurance et la capacité à challenger un besoin métier sont fortement appréciées.


Autonome, vous faites preuve d’un fort sens du service et du respect des engagements. Doté d’un bon esprit de synthèse, vous savez prendre du recul et vous remettre en question. Vous appréciez le travail en équipe et contribuez activement à une dynamique collective. Curieux(se), vous avez une forte appétence pour les nouvelles technologies liées à la data.


Ce poste offre l’opportunité d’intervenir sur des problématiques techniques riches et variées dans un contexte de transformation structurant, notamment dans le cadre des synergies entre différentes entités. Vous évoluerez dans un système d’information modernisé, bénéficiant d’expertises solides et de pratiques innovantes favorisant la performance et la montée en compétences continue.


Le poste peut être basé à Aix-en-Provence ou Toulon.


Ce poste est soumis à l’obtention d’une habilitation de sécurité délivrée par les autorités françaises.

Title: ALTERNANCE - Assistant Data Analyst F/H
Description:
Poste

From the house of dream to the house of talents

« Quoi que vous fassiez - pour le travail ou le plaisir - faites-le avec passion ! Vivez avec passion... »* Christian Dior

Christian Dior fut le couturier du rêve. Dès la fondation de sa Maison en 1947, marquée par la révolution du New Look, il métamorphose ses songes en de merveilleuses créations. Son esprit visionnaire n’a eu de cesse de magnifier les femmes du monde entier, inspirant aujourd’hui et plus que jamais ses successeurs. « Le tissu est le seul véhicule de nos rêves (…). La mode, en somme, est issue d’un rêve, et le rêve, c’est une évasion », écrivait-il dans son ouvrage Je suis couturier. Au fil de ses collections, Monsieur Dior est devenu le démiurge du bonheur de ses muses.

Aujourd’hui, riche de cet héritage d’exception et d’une communauté de plus de 7 500 talents, notre Maison incarne plus que jamais la Maison des Talents pensé par Christian Dior. Dans plus de 40 pays, nous faisons rayonner notre savoir-faire, notre audace et notre excellence à travers la passion et la créativité de nos collaborateurs. Rejoignez-nous et créez votre avenir dans notre univers généreux, disruptif et ambitieux où nous grandissons dans l’excellence, le courage et l’optimisme.


Le Petit Dictionnaire de la Mode


Missions

DETAIL DE LA MISSION

Contexte

Au sein de l’Analytics Factory (siège Monde), vous assisterez l’équipe dans la production et l’analyse des données clients afin d’aider aux prises de décisions. Vous serez amené à être en contact avec nos interlocuteurs en Zone ainsi que les divisions Produits et équipes Marketing Client, dans le but de les soutenir dans leur suivi client.

Descriptif

En fonction de votre profil, nous pourrons définir une mission principale : réalisation d’un score d’appétence produits, retravailler la segmentation clients actuelle, affiner la définition des clients inactifs pour mettre en place des traitements différenciés, …

En Périphérie De Cette Mission Principale, Vous Participerez à La Production Et à L’analyse Quotidiennes Des Données Clients


Réalisation d’études et d’analyses clients (analyse du comportement d’achat, profils clients, parcours clients, segmentations)
Analyses des performances des produits et des collections (ventes, tendances, recrutement etc.)
Gestion opérationnelle des campagnes marketing : recommandation de ciblage, comptages et créations
Mise en place de tableaux de bord afin de suivre la performance des campagnes (newsletters, mailing…)
Analyse des impacts des différentes actions clientèles monde (événements clients, …)


Profil

De formation Bac+5, type école d’ingénieur ou université spécialisée en statistiques


Dataiku / SQL indispensable
Rigueur, fiabilité et autonomie
Esprit d’équipe
Anglais indispensable


Ce que vous apprendrez au cours de cette alternance :

Vous pourrez développer vos compétences analytiques (lecture de résultats, analyse et communication pertinente des enseignements).

Vous aurez également l’occasion de mettre en application vos compétences statistiques à l’aide notamment de Dataiku, dont vous pourrez de plus améliorer votre maîtrise.

Vous participerez à la vie courante d’un grand groupe de luxe, en découvrirez l’univers et les problématiques clients.

A titre personnel, vous développerez vos compétences relationnelles au contact des différentes équipes en Zone, Marketing Client et des divisions Produit, ainsi que votre rigueur et votre autonomie.

Encourageant l’inclusion et la diversité sous toutes ses formes, Christian Dior Couture s'est engagé depuis 2013 dans une démarche en faveur de l'emploi des personnes en situation de handicap, et de la valorisation de toutes les singularités.

Title: Data Analyst (LX or Porto)
Description:
Company Description







Inetum is a global leader in IT services, dedicated to delivering innovative solutions to our clients. We are committed to fostering a dynamic and inclusive work environment that values diversity, where creativity and collaboration can thrive. We are present in 19 countries and have more than 28,000 employees worldwide

.If you are looking for a dynamic, innovative, and technology-driven company, Inetum is the place for you! Come be part of Inetum!







Job Description

Identify business problems and propose data-driven solutions to support decision-making and growth
Retrieve, clean, prepare, and analyze complex datasets from multiple sources
Design and implement analyses using statistical methods and data models
Develop reports, dashboards, and visualizations following best practices (Tableau, PowerBI)
Build automated tools and solutions to deliver recurring insights efficiently
Contribute to data feed industrialization, ETL pipelines, and data warehousing processes
Translate business requirements into technical specifications for the development team
Communicate complex analytical results clearly to both technical and non-technical stakeholders
Collaborate closely with traders, sales teams, business sponsors, and technical teams







Qualifications

Academic background in Engineering, Economics, Computer Science, Mathematics, Statistics, or a related field
Professional experience with data visualization tools such as Tableau and/or PowerBI
Professional experience in data analysis using programming languages, preferably Python (Pandas)
Strong ability to analyze, synthesize, and simplify complex information
Knowledge of descriptive statistics and hypothesis testing is a plus
Familiarity with Big Data technologies such as PySpark, Presto, and Airflow is a plus
Knowledge of Power Query and DAX is aplus
Fluent in English

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
We’re looking for the special, unique and amazing YOU!

@ KCS IT, we look for the ones that stands out, for those that always wants to be better and fight for it, and for those who has the same values that we do: dedication, energy, integrity, transparency, flexibility, trust, honesty, hard work, proactivity, team work.

At KCS we stand for equality and value diversity. We create a safe, diverse environment where opportunities are equal for everyone! We do not discriminate based on age, ethnicity, sexual orientation, gender, disability or any factor other than merit. All applications with skills for the position are welcome!




We are looking for an Amazing: Data Analyst

Seniority: +4 years

Location : Lisbon/Porto




Skills Needed?




Degree in Computer Science, Statistics, Mathematics, Economics, or related fields.
Proven experience in data analysis and Power BI.
Proficiency in SQL and other data manipulation tools.
Ability to create interactive dashboards and reports in Power BI.
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills.
Good communication skills to present insights clearly and effectively.
Ability to work independently and in a team.
The candidate has to be in Portugal.







Why should you become part of our family?




You can develop a career that fits you: your career development is personalized, taking in consideration your needs and goals from a short to long term
Interesting Challenges Ahead: you can work for several clients from different sectors of activity
Free training programs: Our training and certification programs in languages, tech, behavior and business will help you to reach your full potential faster
International projects in Benelux: you can gain international experience in Benelux and balance a new way of living with work
Type of projects (depending on the project you might find one of this types of projects):
In hybrid Systems: Is important to balance work with socialization, that´s why a hybrid system works for us and for you
Full Remote projects: If you want to work while you enjoy the comfort of your home
Full Onsite projects: if you prefere the company of your colleagues!
Take care of your well-being: Enjoy our free nutrition, psicologist, general medicine appointments and our yoga and personal training days… all remote!




Who are we?

Founded in 2008 and based in Lisbon, KCS IT is a consulting company in the field of Information Technology and Services, focused on creating value for our clients through three main areas: Consulting, Outsourcing, Inovation and Training. Our commitment to talent development is unmistakable in the recent opening of the Porto, Leiria and Azores hubs, which aims to develop technology for the national and international market. Since 2018 we have been elected one of the 10 major companies in the “Index of Excellence”, an initiative that aims to reward organizations that invest most in the development and satisfaction of their employees.We believe transparency, trust and integrity are the pillars of any business and relationship and that is what we promise our clients and employees who join our company KCS IT.

Title: Business Data Analyst (h/f)
Description:
Nous recherchons un Business Data Analyst pour rejoindre notre équipe grand compte.

Lieu : Paris

Démarrage : ASAP

Durée : >12 mois

Télétravail : 3 jours / semaine

Contexte de la mission

Au sein du département Data Platform, des agents Data Analytics sont en cours de construction pour aider les utilisateurs commerciaux à transformer les données en insights exploitables. Ce rôle est idéal pour un professionnel qui saura aligner les besoins commerciaux avec les aspects techniques de notre plateforme, Snowflake.

Responsabilités :


Travailler avec le Data Product Owner pour transformer les besoins en composants d'agents.
Évaluer le modèle sémantique et déterminer les métriques et cas d'utilisation.
Concevoir, construire et tester des compétences d'agents.
Définir des fonctions pour récupérer des données et répondre à des questions commerciales.
Créer et gérer une bibliothèque de requêtes vérifiées.
Construire des filtres nommés et rédiger des instructions personnalisées.
Coordonner avec les utilisateurs commerciaux et les parties prenantes techniques.
Capturer et mapper les questions des parties prenantes aux bonnes métriques.
Évaluer la précision des agents et identifier les erreurs.
Maintenir une documentation claire et précise.


Must Haves :


3+ ans d'expérience en tant qu'analyste de données ou BI.
Solides compétences en SQL et expérience dans Snowflake.
Capacité à traduire des questions commerciales en logiques de données.
Expérience dans la gestion d'une couche sémantique.
Compétences en collaboration et communication.
Expérience dans le secteur du commerce de détail.


Nice to Haves :


Connaissance des outils d'analytique ou de BI.
Compréhension des processus métier de la distribution.
Expérience en gestion de projet agile.


Autres détails :

Ce poste est basé à Paris avec la possibilité de télétravail 3 jours par semaine, et est orienté vers une collaboration à long terme dans un secteur en pleine évolution.

Title: Estágio | Data Analyst
Description:
QUEM SOMOS?




Uma das maiores seguradoras do mundo, temos como pilar a sustentabilidade e o cuidado com as pessoas. Possuímos um amplo portfólio de produtos e diversas unidades em que atuamos: Investimentos, Consórcios, Capitalização, Previdência e Vida Resgatável, Saúde, Seguros e Assistência.

Com presença em mais de 100 países nos cinco continentes, somamos mais de 34 mil colaboradores em todo o mundo, mais de 300 em Portugal.

Trabalhamos para construir o futuro e estamos comprometidos com a inovação.

Estamo-nos a transformar para acompanhar os nossos clientes também no mundo digital.

A Fundación MAPFRE, instituição sem fins lucrativos, é a nossa acionista maioritária, o que fortalece a nossa independência e estabilidade. Entre os nossos compromissos está a valorização e apoio à diversidade e inclusão, construindo ambientes mais acolhedores. A presença de mulheres em cargo de gestão e o equilíbrio racial e de género na constituição das equipas é a nossa prioridade.




Como estagiário(a) de Data Science farás parte da transformação data‑driven da Mapfre Portugal, contribuindo para a gestão dos dados como um ativo estratégico ao longo de todo o seu ciclo de vida. Integrarás uma equipa multidisciplinar, trabalhando de perto com engenharia de dados e desenvolvimento de projetos. O teu papel será apoiar a análise, exploração e modelação de dados, aprendendo a traduzir problemas de negócio em soluções analíticas com impacto real.

Valorizamos a tua curiosidade, vontade de aprender e capacidade de colaborar. Durante o estágio terás acompanhamento contínuo, contacto com casos de uso reais e oportunidade de desenvolver competências técnicas e de negócio.




👉O que Oferecemos:




Estamos a ampliar a nossa equipa. Procuramos jovens curiosos e com vontade de começar uma carreira numa atividade em que nunca paramos de aprender.

A Mapfre é feita de pessoas, por isso retribuímos da melhor maneira possível o comprometimento dos nossos Estagiários, que contam com:

Estágio de Duração 1 ano;
Remuneração fixa;
Carga de trabalho: 35h/semanais;
12 dias de férias ano;
Horário flexível;
Cursos e formações presenciais e Elearning na nossa plataforma digital, Universidade Corporativa MAPFRE. Lembrando que a MAPFRE é uma empresa global, o crescimento vai além das barreiras territoriais!
Integração numa equipa experiente e dinâmica;

Formação superior em Data Science/Analytics, Engenharia, Matemática, Estatística ou áreas similares (finalistas ou recém‑graduados).

Experiência académica ou projetos pessoais em Python ou R (valorizado).

Interesse em ambientes Cloud (GCP, Azure ou AWS).
Conhecimentos de ferramentas de visualização como Power BI (valorizado).
Interesse pela indústria seguradora.
Boa capacidade de comunicação e vontade de aprender a explicar conceitos complexos de forma simples.

Apoiar o desenvolvimento de modelos e soluções analíticas, desde o entendimento do problema até à avaliação dos resultados.

QUEM SOMOS?


Uma das maiores seguradoras do mundo, temos como pilar a sustentabilidade e o cuidado com as pessoas. Possuímos um amplo portfólio de produtos e diversas unidades em que atuamos: Investimentos, Consórcios, Capitalização, Previdência e Vida Resgatável, Saúde, Seguros e Assistência.

Com presença em mais de 100 países nos cinco continentes, somamos mais de 34 mil colaboradores em todo o mundo, mais de 300 em Portugal.

Trabalhamos para construir o futuro e estamos comprometidos com a inovação.

A presença de mulheres em cargo de gestão e o equilíbrio racial e de género na constituição das equipas é a nossa prioridade.

Title: Data Analyst Junior
Description:
Joining Unlimitail means becoming part of the Retail Media revolution — the marketing lever that is transforming advertising by leveraging data and the digital and physical environments of retailers.




With the ambition to become a leader in Europe and Latin America, Unlimitail helps brands deploy powerful Retail Media strategies and supports retailers in developing their own offers through cutting-edge proprietary technology.




At Unlimitail, you’ll be joining a fast-growing company at the crossroads of data, technological innovation, and digital marketing — reinventing the way brands and retailers connect with consumers.




Role Overview :




Within the Analytics team, your mission will be to participate in the development of Unlimitail’s analytics capabilities. Your environment will be millions of transactional data coming from major retailers on the market to analyze and improve the performance of online and offline marketing campaigns.




As we work with both retailers (providing media inventory) and brands (buying media inventory), you will have the opportunity to gain a deep understanding of the retail media ecosystem.




Your Mission :




You will work with different teams (Ad Operations, Marketing, Sales) in different countries on a wide variety of topics:

Perform ecommerce campaigns analysis to provide detailed insights
Conduct end-to-end ad hoc analysis to help retailers best monetize their inventory
Participate in the design and maintenance of databases, using SQL to store and manage data efficiently
Work on automating data analysis and data visualization capabilities
Work on Unlimitail’s customer lifetime value calculation capability
Participate in the development of new, cutting-edge capabilities to create the best retail media products




What we’re looking for :




Master's degree in Data Analysis, business analytics, Computer Science, Statistics, Mathematics, Engineering, or a related field.
First experience in data analysis
Data Analysis: Proven ability to analyze large datasets, identify trends, and extract actionable insights through the use of strong SQL skills for querying and data extraction.
Programming Languages: Python and SQL mandatory, any other is a plus
Data Visualization: previous experience in creating interactive and insightful visualizations, using tools like Looker is mandatory
Knowledge of GCP environment is a plus
Problem-solving: Adept in approaching analytical problems and proposing effective solutions.
Good communication skills in English. Another language (Spanish, Portuguese) is a plus.
Strong team player with the ability to collaborate effectively




Salary range: €40k - €45k (commensurate with profile)




What’s in it for you :




Work in a fast-paced, international, and innovative environment, at the intersection of data, retail, and media – one of the fastest-growing spaces in digital
A strategic role in a high-growth team with autonomy and impact
A dynamic, supportive environment with smart people and bold ambitions
Office located in the heart of Paris (Sentier district)
A flexible remote work policy of 2 days/week
90% reimbursement of public transportation
10€/day Swile card for each working day, we'll cover 60% of the cost




Ready to make a big impact? Apply now and be part of our innovative journey!

Unlimitail is committed to fostering a diverse workforce and strongly encourages applications from underrepresented groups. We are committed to equality of opportunity and welcome applications from individuals, regardless of their background.

If you have any reasonable adjustment needs arising from a disability or medical condition to fully participate in the recruitment process, please discuss this with the recruiter who contacts you.

Title: EXPLORE - Data Analyst (H/F) - CDI
Place: Carquefou, Pays de la Loire, France
Description:
Data Analyst (H/F)

CDI

Rejoignez le Groupe INTESCIA, leader de la Business Intelligence en Europe !

Qui sommes-nous ?

EXPLORE, filiale d’INTESCIA GROUP, est un acteur leader dans le développement de solutions de data intelligence BtoB. Nous produisons des données à forte valeur ajoutée pour accompagner le développement commercial des entreprises, faciliter leurs analyses de marchés et optimiser leur connaissance clients. Nous comptons aujourd’hui plus de 1 500 clients et près de 35 000 utilisateurs dans les univers de l’Immobilier, la Construction, l’Énergie, l’Environnement et des Services à l’entreprise.

Nos atouts ?


Un groupe en pleine expansion, reconnu pour son expertise et ses solutions à forte valeur ajoutée.
Une culture d’innovation, où la créativité et l’amélioration continue sont au cœur de notre ADN.
Des équipes passionnées et engagées, évoluant dans un environnement stimulant et collaboratif.



Votre mission :

Au cœur de la donnée, vous contribuez à transformer l’information en insights stratégiques pour Explore et ses clients. Vous participez activement à la création de valeur à travers des analyses pertinentes, des outils décisionnels performants et des données fiables.

Vos activités :


Réaliser des études ad hoc (dimensionnement de marché, potentiel commercial, analyses sectorielles…) pour Explore et ses clients
Produire des baromètres, indicateurs marché et extractions de données qualifiées
Interroger, structurer et exploiter les bases de données via SQL
Concevoir, développer et optimiser des tableaux de bord (Power BI)
Automatiser les traitements et flux de données (Python, FME)
Créer et suivre des indicateurs de performance pertinents
Rédiger et maintenir la documentation méthodologique
Contrôler et garantir la qualité des données
Identifier des opportunités d’enrichissement en croisant différentes sources de données



Le profil que nous recherchons :

Vous êtes à la fois analytique, curieux(se) et orienté(e) solutions. Vous aimez comprendre les enjeux métiers et transformer des données complexes en outils utiles et impactants.

Vos compétences :


Maîtrise des bases de données et des systèmes de gestion (MS SQL Server)
Solides compétences en data visualisation (Power BI)
Bonnes connaissances en programmation (Python apprécié)
Capacité à analyser, structurer et synthétiser l’information
Esprit d’initiative et force de proposition
Rigueur, organisation et sens du détail
Bonne compréhension des enjeux clients et appétence pour la data



Expérience :

Une première expérience en analyse de données, business intelligence ou gestion de bases de données est appréciée.

Ce que nous vous offrons :


Situé à Carquefou, dans le cadre paisible et verdoyant du quartier de la Fleuriaye, à quelques pas de l’Erdre (Port-Jean), vous profiterez d’un cadre idéal pour allier productivité et bien-être.
Type de contrat : CDI
Rémunération fixe
Horaires flexibles : forfait jours - afin de respecter votre équilibre entre vie professionnelle et personnelle.
Organisation hybride : Télétravail possible plusieurs jours par semaine.
Home office possible uniquement pour les candidats résidant à plus de 100 km de nos locaux.
Carte tickets restaurant, épargne d’entreprise, prime vacances, et des bornes de recharge pour véhicules électriques à disposition.
Frigo connecté, rempli quotidiennement pour vos pauses, et un cadre de travail aménagé en flex-office.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Decskill was founded in 2014 as an IT Consulting Company and their main mission is to delivery value through the knowledge. We enable companies to meet the chalenges of digital world by providing our clients with business models that ensure technological capacity, flexibility and agility. We are more than 500 consultants with offices in Lisbon, Porto and Madrid.




DECSKILL operates in 3 main areas:

• DECSKILL TALENT: through which we provide our clients with an extension to their IT teams;

• DECSKILL BOOST: through which we provide our client with software development models to increase capacity and optimize Time-to-Market, where we create and manage teams that deliver according to their needs, at the desired speed;

• DECSKILL CONNECT: through which we provide our client with consulting services, as well as the implementation and management of information technology infrastructures.




Our practice results in the creation of value for our customers, either by delivering qualified and value-added services, or through highly qualified and motivated professionals, as well as technology solutions that allow us to operate and transform the business of our customers.




We are looking for a DATA ANALYST.




Responsabilities:

As a Data Analyst, you will play a vital role in our team. Working directly with one or more of the bank's business areas, you will provide valuable insights to drive business growth, understading their needs, proposing and implementing data-driven solutions (analysis, visualization and automation). You will exercise a lot of problem identification and problem solving skills.
You will have the opportunity to take ownership of business essential analytical projects that involve retrieving, preparing and analysing complex datasets using data/statistical models and best practices in data visualization for the reporting creation, with the goal to produce insights and automated tools that will guide strategic solutions
You will move between technical and non-technical areas and should be able to communicate well with different types of professionals. It is very important to be able to clearly and concisely understand stekeholders’ needs and translate these to our technical team, as well as present the results of complex analysis to our stakeholders.




Requierments:

Academic background in Engineering, Economics, Computing, Mathematics, Statistics or related fields
Professional experience in any data visualization tool such as Tableau or Power BI
Professional experience in data analysis with programming, preferably Python (Pandas)
Professional experience in any data visualization tool such as Tableau or Power BI
Professional experience in data analysis with programming, preferably Python (Pandas)
Ability to analysze, synthetize and simplify.
Knowledge of descriptive statistics and hypothesis testing is a plus.
Knowledge in Big Data technology such as Pyspark, Presto and Airflow is a plus
Knowledge of Power Query and DAX is a plus




If you’re interested in this job please send your CV in English to carolina.mustra@decskill.com with reference “CM/Data Analyst".




Decskill is committed to equality and non-discrimination with all our talents. We recruit and promote talent, based on diversity and inclusion, regardless of age, gender, ethnicity, race, nationality or any other form of discrimination incompatible with the dignity of the human being.

Title: Data & BI Analyst
Description:
Company Description

Inetum is a European leader in digital services. Inetum’s team of 28,000 consultants and specialists strive every day to make a digital impact for businesses, public sector entities and society. Inetum’s solutions aim at contributing to its clients’ performance and innovation as well as the common good. Present in 19 countries with a dense network of sites, Inetum partners with major software publishers to meet the challenges of digital transformation with proximity and flexibility. Driven by its ambition for growth and scale, Inetum generated sales of 2.5 billion euros in 2023.

Job Description


Creation of dashboards and reports to support decision-making.
Implementation of data visualization strategies.
Data Governance: asset cataloging, application of technical and functional rules.
Data Quality: execution of profiles and alerts for stakeholders.
Connections with Microsoft Fabric, Dynamics, and other sources.
Support to business teams in Purview, Data Governance, and Data Quality.
Onboarding and training


Qualifications


Between 2 and 5 years of experience in the role.
Experience in Data Governance and EDP projects.
Tools and platforms:
Microsoft Purview (implementation of data quality rules)
Microsoft Fabric
Power BI
Azure
Databricks
AWS
Languages and tools:
SQL
Advanced Excel
Dashboard tools
Desirable knowledge:
SAS Enterprise Guide
DOMO
DAMAn framework
Experience in CI/CD and agile methodologies.


Additional Information

Work model: Hybrid.

Title: Data Analyst- Power BI
Description:
B2B Freelance Opportunity

Onsite, Brussels

Duration: 6 months

Starts : ASAP

NOTE: French OR Dutch mandatory

EU Citizens and Belgium Work Permit Candidates are Eligible




Service description

As our Power BI Expert you will be the go-to person to design, automate, optimise and maintain the Power BI reporting landscape that supports these initiatives — making sure stakeholders, from executives to operational teams, get the right insights at the right time, in a way they can trust and act upon.




KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

1.KPI reporting & automation

Build and automate end-to-end KPI reporting for Group Controlling stakeholder
Translate business questions into clear, decision-ready dashboards aligned with our performance framework

Title: Data Analyst
HTML:
Who we are

Neotalent Conclusion is one of the leading Iberian specialists providing services in Information Technology and Engineering services. As part of the Netherlands-based organization Conclusion, which has an ecosystem of over 25 companies and 3,600 employees, we are focused on increasing the technological capacity of our clients and the digital transformation of their application ecosystems.

We are talent specialists, attracting the best people and combining them with the needs of the most ambitious projects, at the right time. Our success comes from our investments in innovation, our focus on results, and, above all, the guarantee that our people will have happy and rewarding careers.

With over 25 years of solid experience, Neotalent Conclusion has established its presence in key locations, including Lisbon, Porto, and Madrid. The company currently has more than 950 professionals in the different areas in which it operates.


Neotalent, the responsive people.


Find out more about us at www.neotalentconclusion.com.


Role Overview

We are looking for a Data Analyst to integrate our teams and support data-driven decision-making across the organisation. In this role, you will focus on descriptive and diagnostic analytics, transforming complex data into meaningful insights that help stakeholders understand where the business has been.


What you will do

  • Gather, analyse, and translate business requirements into effective data reporting and analytical solutions;
  • Design and support data solutions such as data warehouses, data marts, dashboards, and reporting systems;
  • Develop and maintain data models, reports, and interactive visualisations;
  • Collaborate closely with IT and data teams to ensure accurate data integration, quality, and governance;
  • Support stakeholders in interpreting analytical results and turning insights into actionable business decisions.


What you are like

  • Proficient in SQL for data querying, analysis, and performance optimisation;
  • Experienced with data visualisation tools such as Tableau, Power BI, or similar technologies;
  • Proficient in statistical analysis and data manipulation using tools such as Python (e.g. pandas) or R;
  • Comfortable working with complex data sets, identifying patterns, trends, and root causes;
  • Skilled in data cleansing, preparation, and validation techniques;
  • English is fluent.


Why join us

  • Work on challenging projects for big clients
  • Learn more about emerging technologies
  • Possibility of working remotely
  • Design your career plan
  • Access to continuous training and certifications
  • Evolve through our internal mobility programme
  • … and much more!

Title: Data analyst - cartographe F/H
Description:
Direction des services judiciaires

Site ODG : 35, rue de la Gare, 75019 Paris




Description du poste

Vos activités principales

Le titulaire du poste a pour mission de contribuer à l’expansion du pôle de l’évaluation et de la prospective de la direction des services judiciaires en apportant ses compétences techniques de data analyse, data visualisation et cartographie.




Il s’agit d’une opportunité unique de rejoindre une équipe à fort impact sur des sujets d’actualité. En lien avec les services de la direction et en application des directives données par le directeur des services judiciaires, il (ou elle) est chargé de :




Conduire des travaux ou études quantitatives en lien avec le cadre global d’évaluation des politiques en matière de ressources humaines, d’organisation judiciaire et budgétaires ;
Exploiter des données administratives à des fins statistiques et initier des collectes de données complémentaires via des enquêtes ;
Accompagner la mise en place d’outils d’appui au pilotage (par exemple dashboard ou reporting) ;
Permettre la visualisation des implantations territoriales des services judiciaires (cartographie) ;
Animer des groupes de travail autour de la cartographie au sein de la direction des services judiciaires et en lien avec le secrétariat général.




Moyens mis à votre disposition (matériel, logiciels spécifiques, véhicules…)

Un bureau, un ordinateur portable, un double-écran, utilisation d’application propres au ministère de la justice.




Les activités du service

Chargé de définir le cadre global d‘évaluation des politiques en matière de ressources humaines, d’organisation judiciaire et budgétaires, le pôle de l’évaluation et de la prospective a une activité transversale et intervient en soutien du cabinet et des sous-directions de la direction dont il bénéficie également des ressources.




Les missions du pôle sont les suivantes :

Réalisation d’évaluations thématiques, collecte de données, restitution d’analyses en lien avec les évaluations réalisées ;
Suivi des recommandations des institutionnels ;
Elaboration d’outils statistiques, d’enquêtes et de reporting pour les besoins de la direction et des juridictions ;
Détermination du programme d’études et de recherche de la direction
Cartographie de structures de justice




Composition et effectifs du service

Le pôle de l’évaluation et de la prospective est composé d’un chef de pôle, un adjoint au chef de pôle adjoint statisticien, un chargé d'étude et un data cartographe (poste à pourvoir




Votre hiérarchie

Le poste de Chargé d’études qualifié – data analyst est placé sous l’autorité du chef de pôle de l’évaluation et de la prospective dont le pôle est rattaché au cabinet du directeur des services judiciaires.




Votre profil

Vos principales compétences à mettre en œuvre

Connaissances techniques




Savoir-faire




Econométrie/statistiques pour l’évaluation




Gestion de base de données




Maîtrise des outils de cartographie




Concevoir un rapport d’analyse




Maîtrise des outils de reporting (tableau/powerBI...)




Connaissances d’un SIG requis




Savoir-être




Outils informatiques




Savoir travailler en équipe




Maîtrise : QGIS, programmation statistique (R, Python…) PythPythonPython…), Excel




Grandes qualités relationnelles




Excel




Capacités d'écoute et de conseil




Souhaité : Sphinx




Savoir gérer l'urgence




Capacité à s'organiser pour répondre dans les meilleurs délais




Force de proposition




Expériences professionnelles antérieures souhaitées

Expérience d’au moins 2 ans - première expérience souhaitée dans le domaine de la cartographie.

Offres de formation associées à votre prise de poste




Informations complémentaires

Organisation du travail : possibilité de télétravail selon l’organisation et les nécessités de service




Restauration : oui




Accessibilité en transport / Parking :

RER (RER E – arrêt Rosa Parks / RER B – arrêt la Plaine Stade de France)

Tram (T3b – arrêt Rosa parks)

Métro (ligne 7 – arrêt Corentin Cariou / ligne 12 – arrêt Front populaire)

Bus 239 ou navette privée « Icade » (arrêt Parc du Millénaire)

Parking sécurisé : voiture, moto, scooter et vélo




Personnes à contacter Lettre de motivation et CV impératifs




M. Amarale JANEIRO

Chef du pôle de l’évaluation et de la prospective




M. Nathan DE VOS

Adjoint au chef du pôle




Mme Héléna YAZDANPANAH

Chargée d’études prospectives

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Company Description

Inetum is a European leader in digital services. Inetum’s team of 28,000 consultants and specialists strive every day to make a digital impact for businesses, public sector entities and society. Inetum’s solutions aim at contributing to its clients’ performance and innovation as well as the common good. Present in 19 countries with a dense network of sites, Inetum partners with major software publishers to meet the challenges of digital transformation with proximity and flexibility. Driven by its ambition for growth and scale, Inetum generated sales of 2.5 billion euros in 2023.

Job Description

Job Description:

The Data Analyst is responsible for collecting, cleaning, analyzing, and interpreting data to provide actionable insights for business decisions. The role involves creating and managing data pipelines using Airflow for data extraction, processing, and loading, ensuring their maintenance, monitoring, and stability. The analyst will work closely with end-users and other teams to provide accessible and reliable data

Main Tasks


Responsible for creating data pipelines in Airflow for data extracting, processing and loading
Responsible for code and query optimization
Responsible for generating insights and identify trends, anomalies or opportunities to improve users experience and anticipate their daily tasks
Responsible for delivering data in useful and appealing ways to users
Responsible for understanding business change and requirement needs, assess the impact and the cost.


Qualifications

Technical Skills:


Python
Experience in creating APIs in Python
PySpark
SQL, Oracle Data Base
Experience with Data Warehouses
Apache Airflow - Experience in pipeline development
Git
Agile methodologies


Language Skills


English – Practice


Soft Skills


Good communication skills
Strong analytical abilities, able to have the right synthesis and all possible details at same timee required soft skills
Adaptability skills
Team spirit
Client Focus
