Title: Data Analyst (Product)
Description:
About Revolut

People deserve more from their money. More visibility, more control, and more freedom. Since 2015, Revolut has been on a mission to deliver just that. Our powerhouse of products — including spending, saving, investing, exchanging, travelling, and more — help our 75+ million customers get more from their money every day.

As we continue our lightning-fast growth,‌ 2 things are essential to our success: our people and our culture. In recognition of our outstanding employee experience, we've been certified as a Great Place to Work™. So far, we have 13,000+ people working around the world, from our offices and remotely, to help us achieve our mission. And we're looking for more brilliant people. People who love building great products, redefining success, and turning the complexity of a chaotic world into the simplicity of a beautiful solution.

About The Role

Our Data Science team solves complex problems with smart, practical solutions. Data Analysts and Scientists ‌work directly with product teams to uncover insights, guide decisions, and improve how customers experience Revolut. They do this by delivering smart, scalable solutions that move the business forward.

We’re looking for a talented Data Analyst to dig into our complex databases, look for root causes of a problem, design your own solutions, and write your own code to implement them. We aim for you to never stop learning, picking up new skills and delivering value along the way.

Up to shape what's next in finance? Let's get in touch.

What You’ll Be Doing


Understanding our business and its processes through our data
Applying this understanding and knowledge of data to help product and service teams
Designing key metrics to measure different aspects of the business
Creating and maintaining new aggregated views and tables to simplify data querying
Maintaining and creating new dashboards to track metrics and visualise insights
Promoting data literacy across the company, organising and holding workshops


What You'll Need


Previous experience in an analytical role, creating impactful solutions
A background/education in a quantitative discipline
5+ years of experience with SQL
Great skills with Python or other programming languages
Evidence of solid mathematical and statistics knowledge


Building a global financial super app isn’t enough. Our Revoluters are a priority, and that’s why in 2021 we launched our inaugural D&I Framework, designed to help us thrive and grow everyday. We're not just doing this because it's the right thing to do. We’re doing it because we know that seeking out diverse talent and creating an inclusive workplace is the way to create exceptional, innovative products and services for our customers. That’s why we encourage applications from people with diverse backgrounds and experiences to join this multicultural, hard-working team.

Important notice for candidates:

Job scams are on the rise. Please keep these guidelines in mind when applying for any open roles.


Only apply through official Revolut channels. We don’t use any third-party services or platforms for our recruitment.
Always double-check the emails you receive. Make sure all communications are being done through official Revolut emails, with an @revolut.com domain.


We won't ask for payment or personal financial information during the hiring process. If anyone does ask you for this, it’s a scam. Report it immediately.

By submitting this application, I confirm that all the information given by me in this application for employment and any additional documents attached hereto are true to the best of my knowledge and that I have not wilfully suppressed any material fact. I confirm I have disclosed if applicable any previous employment with Revolut. I accept that if any of the information given by me in this application is in any way false or incorrect, my application may be rejected, any offer of employment may be withdrawn or my employment with Revolut may be terminated summarily or I may be dismissed. By submitting this application, I agree that my personal data will be processed in accordance with Revolut's Candidate Privacy Notice

Title: Senior Data Analyst — Hybrid, Barcelona
Description:
Who we are:
At Rover, pets and their people are at the heart of everything we do. We connect pet parents with trusted pet care across the U.S., Canada, Europe, and Australia.

Headquartered in Seattle and Barcelona, we’re a values driven, fast-growing tech company focused on building safe and personalized experiences tailored to the needs of each unique pet. We’re investing in AI as a business accelerator and provide every team member access to AI tools in service of creating better experiences for our community.

We are proud to be recognized as a great place to work, having been named among the 100 Best Companies to Work For in Seattle Business Magazine and Washington’s Best Workplaces in the Puget Sound Business Journal. At Rover we’re committed to creating an accessible, inclusive, and welcoming community, which starts with our employees.

Want to make an impact? Join our pack and come work (and play!) with us.

Own the data behind customer lifetime value and repeat bookings at Rover — modeling how customers behave over time and turning a large event-based warehouse into reporting our leaders actually trust. A senior, hands-on analytics role on our Barcelona team.

About The Role
Rover's Barcelona analytics team has one north star: grow customer lifetime value and drive more repeat bookings across both sides of our marketplace of pet parents and sitters. As a senior individual contributor, you'll own the models, metrics, and reporting that explain how customers retain, churn, and come back — and you'll dig into why.

You'll report to the Analytics Manager and partner closely with data engineering and the teams responsible for retention and growth.

This is a hybrid role: we're in our Poblenou office two days a week (Mondays and Thursdays), so you'll need to be based in Barcelona or willing to relocate.

What You'll Do


Model customer lifetime value and retention. Build the cohort, retention-curve, and LTV models that show how customer value develops over time — and make them something the business can actually steer by
Own reporting leaders trust. Build and maintain the dashboards, data models, and reporting layers leadership leans on day-to-day, and proactively fix what's noisy, inconsistent, or missing. You'll be the go-to when a number looks off.
Root-cause the metrics. When retention or repeat-booking behavior shifts, find out why, tie it to demand- and supply-side marketplace dynamics, and bring a clear narrative to the people who need to act.
Write SQL against large event-based datasets, build transformation models (dbt or similar), and review and improve code from other analysts.
Partner with data engineering to QA business logic and keep our data clean, well-modeled, and measurable from day one.




What We're Looking For (must-haves)


3+ years using data to drive business impact — finding insights, telling the story, and partnering with cross-functional teams to act on them
Strong SQL, including confidence with complex queries on large datasets and reviewing others' code
Experience modeling customer behavior — cohorts, retention, lifetime value, or segmentation
Solid grounding in statistical analysis with Python or R
A clear communicator who makes complex analysis land with technical and non-technical audiences
Experience with a data visualization tool (e.g., Mode, Looker, Tableau)




Nice to have


Experience in a two-sided marketplace, subscription, or consumer business — and a feel for LTV/CAC dynamics
Familiarity with transformation models (dbt or similar) and a point of view on what makes a good data model
Experience with event or product analytics (e.g., Amplitude)
Familiarity with any of our stack: Amazon Redshift, dbt, Mode, Statsig, Amplitude, Iterable, or Hightouch
Experience mentoring analysts or raising the technical bar without formal management
Experience putting agentic AI tools (e.g., Claude) to work in your analytics workflow — to speed up SQL, analysis, or documentation




Benefits Of Working At Rover


Long-term incentive plan with a company performance-based cash payout
Pension plan
Private medical insurance
25 days PTO
Meal allowance and flexible compensation plan (transport and nursery)
Gym membership
€450 to cover the costs associated with the adoption of a pet
Annual €150 wellness reimbursement
Flexible work hours, sometimes you'll need to be in at certain times, but on the whole, we're pretty flexible when it comes to managing workload and time
Grab snacks, fresh fruit, in our kitchen to keep yourself going
Regular team activities, events, game nights, and more
Dog-friendly office




Compensation


In the greater Barcelona area the first-year salary range is €47,124 - €60,319
The cash compensation offered for this role will be dependent on the candidate's experience, qualifications, skills, and abilities as demonstrated in the interview and hiring process




Rover is an equal-opportunity employer committed to promoting a diverse, inclusive, and inventive environment with the best employees. Who we are:
At Rover, pets and their people are at the heart of everything we do. We connect pet parents with trusted pet care across the U.S., Canada, Europe, and Australia.

Headquartered in Seattle and Barcelona, we’re a values driven, fast-growing tech company focused on building safe and personalized experiences tailored to the needs of each unique pet. We’re investing in AI as a business accelerator and provide every team member access to AI tools in service of creating better experiences for our community.

We are proud to be recognized as a great place to work, having been named among the 100 Best Companies to Work For in Seattle Business Magazine and Washington’s Best Workplaces in the Puget Sound Business Journal. At Rover we’re committed to creating an accessible, inclusive, and welcoming community, which starts with our employees.

Want to make an impact? Join our pack and come work (and play!) with us.

Own the data behind customer lifetime value and repeat bookings at Rover — modeling how customers behave over time and turning a large event-based warehouse into reporting our leaders actually trust. A senior, hands-on analytics role on our Barcelona team.

About The Role
Rover's Barcelona analytics team has one north star: grow customer lifetime value and drive more repeat bookings across both sides of our marketplace of pet parents and sitters. As a senior individual contributor, you'll own the models, metrics, and reporting that explain how customers retain, churn, and come back — and you'll dig into why.

You'll report to the Analytics Manager and partner closely with data engineering and the teams responsible for retention and growth.

This is a hybrid role: we're in our Poblenou office two days a week (Mondays and Thursdays), so you'll need to be based in Barcelona or willing to relocate.

What You'll Do

  • Experience putting agentic AI tools (e.g., Claude) to work in your analytics workflow — to speed up SQL, analysis, or documentation

  • Pension plan
  • Private medical insurance
  • 25 days PTO

  • €450 to cover the costs associated with the adoption of a pet

  • Flexible work hours, sometimes you'll need to be in at certain times, but on the whole, we're pretty flexible when it comes to managing workload and time

Title: Data Analyst (H/F)
Description:
De Dietrich est le leader mondial pour la conception et la fourniture de systèmes, d'équipements de procédé et de solutions destinés aux industries pharmaceutique, agroalimentaire, de la chimie verte et de la chimie.




À propos du poste

Dans le cadre du programme de transformation de nos processus et outils, amenant au déploiement d’un nouvel ERP au sein d’un environnement industriel international, nous recrutons un(e) Data Analyst en CDD de 12 mois pour sécuriser et fiabiliser l’ensemble des travaux d’analyse, de préparation et de migration des données. Véritable interface entre les équipes métiers, la DSI et les partenaires projet, vous jouerez un rôle clé dans la réussite opérationnelle du programme.




Pourquoi nous rejoindre ?

Vous rejoignez un projet structurant pour l’entreprise, au cœur de la transformation de nos processus et de notre système d’information.

Ce CDD vous permettra d’avoir un impact direct sur la fiabilité des données, la performance opérationnelle et la réussite du déploiement ERP, dans un environnement où les enjeux métiers sont concrets et la qualité des données est un facteur clé de succès.




Profil recherché

Formation et expérience

Issu(e) d’une formation supérieure en data, informatique, systèmes d’information ou équivalent
Expérience réussie en analyse de données, qualité des données ou migration de données, idéalement dans un contexte ERP et/ou industriel

Compétences et connaissances

Maîtrise des méthodes d’analyse, de nettoyage, transformation et contrôle des données
Bonne compréhension des enjeux de migration de données dans un projet ERP
Aisance avec les requêtes SQL, l'analyse de fichiers de données et les outils de traitement (Talend) et/ou de visualisation
Capacité à formaliser des règles de gestion, des plans de contrôle et des procédures opérationnelles
Bonne connaissance d’un environnement industriel, supply chain, finance ou production appréciée
Compréhension des référentiels articles / clients / fournisseurs

Savoir-être

Rigueur, sens du détail et esprit d'analyse
Capacité à investiguer et analyser des anomalies
Bon relationnel et capacité à travailler en transverse avec des interlocuteurs métiers et techniques




Vous souhaitez contribuer à un projet stratégique et mettre vos compétences data au service d’une entreprise industrielle en transformation ? Postulez dès maintenant et rejoignez-nous !

Title: Data Analyst
HTML:
A Job? Or a Lifetime Experience? Start Yours Here!


A nossa missão é ser uma parte significativa da carreira das nossas pessoas. À medida que crescemos, cresce também a determinação em oferecer a melhor experiência aos nossos colaboradores e clientes e é exatamente isso que nos move. Somos uma empresa portuguesa de consultoria tecnológica com escritórios em Lisboa, Porto e Óbidos e representações no Brasil e Tunísia. Contamos com mais de 11 anos de expertise no mercado, e hoje somos um universo de cerca de 400 pessoas a trabalhar em contexto presencial, remoto ou híbrido em projetos em mais de 20 países.

Acreditamos que grandes pessoas fazem empresas de sucesso e por isso mesmo defendemos a valorização, reconhecimento e crescimento dos nossos profissionais.

Não queremos ser mais uma linha do teu CV, queremos que connosco vivas uma Lifetime Experience. Apostamos em formações e certificações que confirmam a tua real progressão, na promoção de um saudável work-life balance e na oferta de benefícios com impacto na tua vida pessoal e carreira.



O que procuramos

  • Licenciatura em Economia, Estatística, Matemática, Gestão ou similar;
  • Mais de 3 anos de experiência profissional em funções como Data Analyst;
  • Conhecimentos sólidos de SQL Server;
  • Conhecimentos sólidos de PowerBI (dashboards, DAX, modelagem de dados);
  • Conhecimentos sólidos de SSIS
  • Desenvolver, executar e automatizar processos ETL
  • Bons conhecimentos de inglês (B2 ou superior).



Ser Affinity

#Affinidade – Criares relações de proximidade e empatia com colegas, clientes e candidatos (e não apenas com tecnologias, linguagens e plataformas). Espírito de equipa e boa disposição;

· #Ambição – Quereres fazer mais e melhor. Vontade de exceder expectativas e evoluir pessoal e profissionalmente;

· #Ação – Energia para fazer acontecer. Mostrares proatividade e capacidade de iniciativa;

· #Aprendizagem – Vontade de evoluir individual e coletivamente e tornares-te um expert no mercado tecnológico;

· #Assertividade – Comunicação transparente e honesta, sempre com feedback construtivo.



A tua #LifetimeExperience

  • Participar em projetos nacionais e internacionais numa empresa baseada em relacionamentos pessoais, na simplicidade e na eficiência, com uma ação disruptiva no mercado tecnológico;
  • Aceder a um plano de carreira e formação personalizado e diretamente proporcional ao teu desempenho e interesse;
  • Fazer parte de um ambiente acolhedor, de confiança, respeito e informalidade;
  • Integrar as nossas Affinity Communities (de desporto, tecnologia, hobbies, etc.) e participar ativamente em projetos de responsabilidade social e ambiental;
  • Tirar partido de um conjunto de benefícios, parcerias, descontos eventos e dinâmicas internas.


Para além da tua satisfação profissional queremos proporcionar-te oportunidades de lazer e convívio memoráveis e dignos de uma experiência Affinity.

Join the Experience!


Envia-nos a tua candidatura e aproveita para nos conhecer e ir acompanhando em:

https://www.instagram.com/affinityportugal/

https://www.linkedin.com/company/2701281/admin/

  • https://twitter.com/WithAffinity

Title: Data & Business Operations Intern
Description:
What is Shotgun?

Shotgun is a leading platform dedicated to empowering live music event organizers and touring artists, driving the future of the live music industry. Our mission is to build the infrastructure that enables live music to thrive.

We provide powerful tools to sell tickets, build strong brands, and connect with fans in meaningful ways. From 2020 to 2025, Shotgun achieved rapid growth with GMV increasing 25× and revenue growing 40× — leading the company to profitability. Today, we support more than 10,000 clients and engage over 8,000,000 users across Europe, the US, and LATAM.

In the past seven years, Shotgun has raised €10M from Newfund, Venrex, One RagTime, Creator Collective, and BPI Culture Fund. We are a lean and dynamic team of 90 people, with offices in Paris, Miami, São Paulo, Lisbon, and Marseille. Our culture is built on passion, commitment, ownership, and humility.

What we're looking for

We're looking for individuals who want more than just a job. People willing to get out of their comfort zone to reach outstanding goals. Doers who never stop learning, are curious about everything and love solving problems. We want people who are talented, optimistic, and easy-going: who set the bar high but don't take themselves too seriously.

The Role

As Shotgun grows, so does our ambition. We're branching out into new territories, inaugurating fresh ventures, and, in the process, assembling new teams. Our pathway to success lies in our analytical rigor and exhaustive exploration of every opportunity.

As a Data & Business Operations Intern, you'll be embedded within the Business Operations & Strategy team. You will play a crucial role in scaling the operational teams. Your mission is to provide the processes, tools, datas and organizational frameworks that allow teams to scale efficiently and remain resilient in a growth context. You will also support business teams with robust business intelligence and data analytics insights to enable their potential.

What you will do

Operational processes & tooling


Identify potential improvements of the processes we use to solve common issues and propose creative and smart solutions.
Hack our tools stack to automate manual tasks to save time for (you) and other team members to work on impactful projects.
Handcraft no-code/low-code tools for organizers (that could become part of the Product one day) in order to test & gather feedback
Document and train other team members on the new tools and processes you came up with getting the infinite satisfaction of having others use your creation in their daily work.
Create and maintain tools to help the operational teams be more efficient (automation, macro etc.)
Maintain tools and processes so that everyone can keep on working seemlessly
Build and deploy internal AI-powered tools, write efficient prompts and contexts


Business intelligence & data analytics


Help us step back and understand our organisers performance (revenue they generate vs cost: marketing, support time etc.)
Run ad-hoc in depth data analysis to identify root cause of problems and qualify how bad they are (or not)
Assist Operational stakeholders (Support, Account Management, Marketing, Field ops…) with their BI needs and requests


The job is for you if


You are looking for a 6 months internship in Paris
You were born to solve problems
You like getting your hands dirty trying to help your team get better at what they do
You are about to graduate from an Engineering, Business School or University with a strong quantitative major
You have outstanding analytical skills
You are proactive, organized and you can work autonomously
You use AI on a daily basis
You master SQL/BI tools or are willing to master it. Experience with no code automation tools (Zapier, Make, n8n, etc.) is a plus.
You are fluent in French ; strong English is necessary; Portuguese is a plus.
You're eligible to work in France
Start date : September/October 2026 - Paris
👪 Note to women and underrepresented groups: We value diversity because it’s essential for creativity and healthy growth. If you don’t meet 100% of the requirements but are excited by this role, please apply 🙏


Comps & Perks


💰 Compensation: 1500€/month
✈️ Team Offsites & Events: We invest in team moments because they matter
🕺 Shotgun Event Credits: Enjoy live events with quarterly credits
🏋️‍♀️ Egym Wellpass: Preferential rates for gyms and sports activities
🌱 Sustainable Mobility Bonus: For eco-friendly commuting like biking
🍔 Swile Lunch Card: Meals and more
💊 Alan Health Insurance: Full coverage for your health needs
👶 Childcare Support

Title: Junior Data Engineer
Description:
Smadex is a cutting-edge ad-tech company: we process more than 4,5 million ad requests per second, replying under 100ms (for reference, the Google search engine handles ~76,000 QPS). Every day we generate several TBs of data that need to be processed, analyzed and aggregated, so our real-time components get smarter every day. We need you to help us keep our platform in motion! If you are ready to boost your professional career in the tech world, this is your opportunity.

The team

You will join our core engineering team, a collaborative group of experienced engineers responsible for enabling complex data to be available in the real-time bidding platform for the Machine Learning models to use. We foster a fast-paced, supportive environment where knowledge sharing is key. We value autonomy and will provide the platform for you to grow your career while solving complex engineering challenges.

What are we looking for?

We're looking for an enthusiastic Junior Data Engineer who is passionate about data pipelines and excited by extreme performance challenges. Your success will be measured by your ability to help build robust data pipelines, deliver high-quality features, and collaborate closely with the team while eagerly learning and applying your knowledge effectively.

What will you do at Smadex?

You will dive into a dynamic environment, tackling short-cycle tasks such as implementing complex data ingestion services, following a "you build it, you run it" philosophy. You will be expected to participate in code reviews, contribute to technical and architectural decisions, and collaborate in a fast-paced environment where every opinion counts.

Key Responsibilities


Feature Ownership: Design, develop, and implement Data features for the Machine Learning models to use..
Quality Assurance: Write clean, efficient, testable code and thoroughly test work locally and in a sophisticated staging environment.
Operational Excellence: Monitor deployments and help ensure the stability, reliability and performance of production systems.
Agile Collaboration: Collaborate with the team in an agile environment, participating in planning and reporting on progress.


Requirements


A Bachelor's degree in Computer Science or a related technical field.
1-2 years of experience in Software or Data engineering
SQL Knowledge
Python or Java knowledge.
Interest in Big Data, Machine Learning and Data Engineering.
Excellent communication skills in English (written and spoken)


What Will Make You Stand Out (Bonus Points)


Scala knowledge
Experience with Key-Value stores and in-memory databases (e.g. Redis, Memcached).
Familiarity with cloud environments, particularly AWS.
Experience with Airflow workflows
Experience in Big Data
Experience with Spark


Our Tech Stack


Languages: Python, Scala and bash.
Frameworks: Spark
Databases: MySQL, Redis
Cloud & DevOps: AWS, Jenkins, Docker, Airflow
Tools: Git, Jira, Notion


What We Offer


Join a highly motivated and talented team in a fun and friendly work environment.
Opportunity to work in a complex environment and gain experience from it.
Directly add value to the business with the projects that you are assigned.
Great compensation package.
Top location at the heart of Barcelona with a rooftop terrace, Barbeque, and a fully stocked fridge.
Great work-life balance: work from home (2 days per week) and flexible hours.
Professional development with a LinkedIn Learning subscription and other training opportunities.
Meal vouchers - Ticket Restaurant monthly allowance.
Monthly gym allowance: Choose between DiR and Wellhub
Monthly TGIF (Thank God It’s Friday) events and regular team-building activities.
Ping-pong table and arcade machine.
And more!


If you want to know more about us visit our website Smadex.com, and for a sneak peek of the cool stuff we build check this video out! And, if you are ready to take your career to the next level and tackle engineering challenges at a global scale, we encourage you to apply!

Please note that we do NOT provide VISA sponsorship. Candidates without a legal permit to work in Spain won't be considered.

  • Feature Ownership: Design, develop, and implement Data features for the Machine Learning models to use..
  • Quality Assurance: Write clean, efficient, testable code and thoroughly test work locally and in a sophisticated staging environment.
  • Operational Excellence: Monitor deployments and help ensure the stability, reliability and performance of production systems.
  • Agile Collaboration: Collaborate with the team in an agile environment, participating in planning and reporting on progress.

Requirements

  • A Bachelor's degree in Computer Science or a related technical field.
  • 1-2 years of experience in Software or Data engineering
  • SQL Knowledge
  • Python or Java knowledge.
  • Interest in Big Data, Machine Learning and Data Engineering.
  • Excellent communication skills in English (written and spoken)

What Will Make You Stand Out (Bonus Points)

  • Scala knowledge
  • Experience with Key-Value stores and in-memory databases (e.g. Redis, Memcached).
  • Familiarity with cloud environments, particularly AWS.
  • Experience with Airflow workflows
  • Experience in Big Data
  • Experience with Spark

Our Tech Stack

  • Languages: Python, Scala and bash.
  • Frameworks: Spark
  • Databases: MySQL, Redis
  • Cloud & DevOps: AWS, Jenkins, Docker, Airflow
  • Tools: Git, Jira, Notion

What We Offer

  • Join a highly motivated and talented team in a fun and friendly work environment.
  • Opportunity to work in a complex environment and gain experience from it.
  • Directly add value to the business with the projects that you are assigned.
  • Great compensation package.
  • Top location at the heart of Barcelona with a rooftop terrace, Barbeque, and a fully stocked fridge.
  • Great work-life balance: work from home (2 days per week) and flexible hours.
  • Professional development with a LinkedIn Learning subscription and other training opportunities.
  • Meal vouchers - Ticket Restaurant monthly allowance.
  • Monthly gym allowance: Choose between DiR and Wellhub
  • Monthly TGIF (Thank God It’s Friday) events and regular team-building activities.
  • Ping-pong table and arcade machine.
  • And more!

If you want to know more about us visit our website Smadex.com, and for a sneak peek of the cool stuff we build check this video out! And, if you are ready to take your career to the next level and tackle engineering challenges at a global scale, we encourage you to apply!

Please note that we do NOT provide VISA sponsorship. Candidates without a legal permit to work in Spain won't be considered.

Title: Senior Data Analyst (m/f/d)
Description:
Senior Data Analyst (m/f/d)

Working model: hybrid in Munich

Salary: Depending on experience, approx. €70,000 – €100,000 p.a.




About our client

Our client is an established, growth-oriented technology company with a digital business model. Data plays a central role in improving products, internal processes and strategic decision-making.

The data team works closely with various business functions as well as technical teams and develops analytical solutions that are actively used across the company. The focus is not only on reporting, but also on building scalable data models, analytical frameworks and data-driven decision foundations.

We are looking for an analytically strong person who combines technical depth, business understanding and clear communication.




Your profile

You have several years of experience as a Data Analyst, Product Analyst, Business Analyst, or in a comparable analytics role with strong business exposure
You have delivered analyses or analytics products that influenced concrete business decisions or created measurable business impact
You have very strong SQL skills and experience working with databases, data warehouses, or modern analytics stacks
You can structure complex business questions, identify relevant data sources, and turn your findings into clear recommendations for stakeholders
You communicate very strongly in English and can explain projects both clearly to business stakeholders and in technical depth when needed
You have experience working with Product, Operations, Sales, Marketing, Customer Support, or similar business teams
You think entrepreneurially, work pragmatically, and see data as a means to solve real business problems
You bring strong ownership, enjoy learning new tools and technologies, and have a clear maker mindset
You ideally have experience in Tech, E-Commerce, Marketplace, Travel, SaaS, Product Analytics, or a similarly data-driven environment
Experience with Python, dbt, Airflow, AWS, Looker, or comparable tools is a plus, but what matters most is that you use technical skills to create business impact
Experience with AI tools such as Claude, ChatGPT, Copilot, or similar solutions in an analytics context is a strong plus
You have a thoughtful perspective on how AI is changing the work of Data Analysts and how modern tools can be integrated meaningfully into analytics workflows




Your responsibilities

You analyze complex business questions and translate them into clear analytical concepts, decision frameworks, and actionable recommendations
You work closely with stakeholders across Business, Operations, Product, Sales, Customer Support, and Data Engineering to understand which decisions your analyses should support
You identify performance drivers, optimization opportunities, and relevant patterns in data to improve processes, products, and operational workflows
You develop analytics solutions, frameworks, and data products that go beyond dashboards and are actively used in day-to-day business decision-making
You define and challenge KPIs, create clear metric definitions, and ensure that business teams work with consistent and reliable decision-making foundations
You own analyses end-to-end: from problem definition, data exploration, SQL analysis, and modeling to presenting insights and recommendations to stakeholders
You communicate your findings clearly and in a structured way: starting with the business context and impact, then going into technical depth when needed
You collaborate with Data Engineers to build reliable data sources, clean data models, and scalable analytics structures
You use modern analytics and AI tools to make analysis more efficient, test new approaches, and create better decision-making support
You take ownership of your projects, prioritize pragmatically using an 80/20 mindset, and ensure your work creates measurable business impact




Interested? Feel free to reach out to me:

Constantin Clodius

Mobile: +49 170 3660753

E-Mail: c.clodius@zabelglobal.com

Show more

Title: Data & Analytics Junior Manager (f/m)
Description:
BAT is evolving at pace into a global multi-category business. Our purpose is to create A Better Tomorrow™ by Building a Smokeless World.

To achieve our ambition, we are looking for colleagues who are ready to join us on this journey! Tomorrow can’t wait, let’s shape it together!

British American Tobacco Poland has an exciting opportunity for a Data & Analytics Junior Manager in Warsaw

To support this journey, British American Tobacco is looking for an experienced professional to join our Central Europe Data, Analytics & AI Team. This role goes far beyond traditional reporting - you will act as a trusted analytics partner to the business, helping translate complex challenges into actionable insights and scalable data and AI solutions.

We are looking for a business-oriented data professional who combines strong analytical skills with excellent communication and stakeholder management capabilities. If you thrive in fast-paced environments and are passionate about leveraging modern data and AI technologies to solve complex business challenges, this opportunity is for you.

Your key responsibilities will include


Acting as a trusted Data, Analytics & AI partner for business participants, translating business priorities and challenges into clear, impactful analytical solutions
Identifying, shaping and supporting analytics and AI use cases that deliver measurable business impact, while promoting awareness of modern data and AI capabilities across the organization
Gathering, structuring and validating business requirements, ensuring strong alignment between business needs and technical delivery
Designing and delivering analytical solutions and insights that enable data-driven decision-making at both operational and management levels
Coordinating cross-functional efforts, tracking progress, managing dependencies and ensuring deliverables meet business objectives and compliance standards
Working closely with data engineering and platform teams to ensure data quality, availability and reliability across analytics solutions
Supporting the adoption of modern analytics and AI through standards, enablement sessions and advisory support, fostering a culture of innovation
Managing multiple initiatives in parallel, ensuring clear prioritization, transparent communication and alignment with broader strategic objectives


What are we looking for?


A data professional committed to practical results with solid experience in data analytics, ideally in a complex or international environment
Strong hands-on expertise in technology - SQL and Power BI are essential; experience with modern data platforms such as Microsoft Fabric, Microsoft Azure cloud services and AI tools is a strong advantage
Good understanding of data warehousing, analytics and AI concepts, with the ability to contribute confidently to architecture discussions
Proven ability to structure ambiguous business problems and translate them into clear analytical questions and requirements
Strong communication skills, with the ability to explain complex topics in a clear, concise and business-relevant way
Confidence and credibility when working with both technical and non-technical partners, including senior business leaders
A proactive, pragmatic attitude with a willingness to take end-to-end ownership — from problem definition to insight delivery
Full professional proficiency in English; German is an advantage


What we offer you?


We offer a market leading annual performance bonus (subject to eligibility)
Our range of benefits varies by country and includes diverse health plans, initiatives for work-life balance, transportation support, and a flexible holiday plan with additional incentives
Your journey with us isn't limited by boundaries; it's propelled by your aspirations. Join us at BAT and become a part of an environment that thrives on internal advancement, where your career progression isn't just a statement – it's a reality we're eager to build together. Seize the opportunity and own your development; your next chapter starts here.
You'll have access to online learning platforms and personalized growth programs to nurture your leadership skills
We prioritise continuous improvement within a transformative environment, preparing for ongoing changes


WHY JOIN BAT?

We’re one of the few companies named as a Global Top Employer by the Top Employers Institute – certified in offering excellent employee conditions.

Collaboration, inclusion and partnership underpin everything we do here at BAT. We are looking forward to enabling every individual to thrive, regardless of gender, sexual orientation, marital or civil partnership status, gender reassignment, race, religion or belief, colour, nationality, ethnic or national origin, disability, age, skills, experience, education, socio-economic and professional background, veteran status, perspectives and thinking styles. We know that embracing talent from all backgrounds is what makes us stronger and best prepared to meet our business goals.

We see the career breaks as opportunities not obstacles. Through The Global Returners program, we support professionals looking to restart their careers after an extended absence from the workforce (e.g. time out caring for family, parental leave, national service, sabbatical and/or starting an own venture).

Come bring your difference and see what is possible for you at BAT. Learn more about our culture and our award winning employee experience here.

If you require any reasonable adjustments or accommodations to help you perform at your best during the recruitment process, you are encouraged to notify us. We are fully committed to support you by making appropriate arrangements for you to demonstrate your full potential.

British American Tobacco Polska Trading sp. z o.o., in compliance with Article 24(6) of the Act of 14 June 2024 on the Protection of Whistleblowers (the “Act”), has established an internal reporting procedure (the “Procedure”). The Procedure is available on the British American Tobacco Poland website under “Our Procedures and Policies.”

Under the Act, protection applies to individuals who submit a report in good faith. Submitting a report while knowingly aware that no violation of law has occurred may constitute an offense under Article 57 of the Act, punishable by up to two years of imprisonment.

Orario flessibile

diploma di scuola secondaria di secondo grado

ottima precisione e attenzione al dettaglio

autonomia operativa nella gestione dei processi di inserimento dati e capacità di rispettare scadenze e priorità

Title: Executive Assistant
Description:
At The Coca-Cola Company, we create moments of connection and refreshment for people around the world. Behind our iconic brands is a global organization powered by curiosity, agility, and a growth mindset—where talented people come together to move the business forward in meaningful ways.

We are seeking an Executive Assistant, who thrives in fast paced, dynamic environments and brings a strong blend of executive partnership, digital fluency, and operational excellence. This role provides critical support to senior leaders, enabling focus, clarity, and impact across a complex, global organization. As a trusted partner, you will anticipate needs, orchestrate priorities, and leverage modern tools and ways of working to simplify complexity and enhance leadership effectiveness.

This role working cross functionally and across geographies to support collaboration, and execution. It is an opportunity to contribute to Coca-Cola's purpose by ensuring leaders can operate at their best—every day.

You will work as part of an Enterprise support community.

What You’ll Do For Us


Partner closely with senior leaders to manage complex calendars, proactively prioritizing meetings, resolving conflicts, and ensuring time is aligned to business critical priorities
Anticipate executive needs by staying ahead of schedules, deadlines, and deliverables, providing thoughtful recommendations and solutions in moments of ambiguity
Coordinate end-to-end planning for leadership routines, including leadership team meetings, offsites, business reviews, and strategic planning sessions across multiple geographies
Prepare, draft, and manage high impact correspondence and communications with internal and external stakeholders, ensuring clarity, professionalism, and follow-through
Track action items, decisions, and commitments, proactively managing follow-ups with leaders and partners to ensure timely execution
Orchestrate domestic and international travel, including approvals, bookings, visas, and expense reporting, ensuring compliance with company travel and expense policies

We are seeking an Executive Assistant, who thrives in fast paced, dynamic environments and brings a strong blend of executive partnership, digital fluency, and operational excellence. This role provides critical support to senior leaders, enabling focus, clarity, and impact across a complex, global organization. As a trusted partner, you will anticipate needs, orchestrate priorities, and leverage modern tools and ways of working to simplify complexity and enhance leadership effectiveness.

This role working cross functionally and across geographies to support collaboration, and execution. It is an opportunity to contribute to Coca-Cola's purpose by ensuring leaders can operate at their best—every day.

You will work as part of an Enterprise support community.

What You’ll Do For Us

Title: Ingénieur Data BI F/H
HTML:
L'entreprise

Pour vos pauses gourmandes, vous hésitez entre une crêpe Whaou!, un pancake Le Ster ou encore une madeleine Armor Délices ? Sachez que toutes ces pâtisseries sont fabriquées en Bretagne par le groupe Goûters Magiques.

Avec près de 700 collaborateurs répartis sur 5 sites et un chiffre d’affaires de 200M€, Goûters Magiques est un acteur majeur sur le marché de la pâtisserie, avec ses marques Whaou!, Le Ster et Armor Délices. Labellisé Engagé RSE depuis 2018, le groupe s’est donné pour mission de proposer des produits qui ont du Goût et du Sens et qui mettent une touche de magie dans le quotidien de tous ! Goûters Magiques s’engage pour préserver l’environnement en travaillant avec des partenaires locaux qui partagent les valeurs du groupe afin de concilier plaisir et nutrition.

Description Du Poste

Au sein de la DSI, vous jouerez un rôle central dans la construction et l'évolution de l'écosystème Data du groupe. Vous contribuerez à structurer au sein de l’équipe ESM les flux de données, développer les outils décisionnels et participer à la définition de l'architecture Data qui accompagnera la croissance de l'entreprise. Véritable interface entre les métiers et les équipes techniques, vous transformerez les données en leviers de performance et d'aide à la décision.

Vos Principales Missions Seront Les Suivantes

Développer et valoriser les usages Data

  • Recueillir et analyser les besoins des métiers avec le PO ;
  • Concevoir et mettre en œuvre les solutions décisionnelles ;
  • Développer les tableaux de bord, indicateurs et analyses nécessaires au pilotage de l'entreprise ;
  • Accompagner les utilisateurs dans l'exploitation et la compréhension des données ;

Industrialiser les flux et les interfaces

  • Concevoir, développer et maintenir les interfaces entre les applications du SI ;
  • Participer activement à l'intégration du nouvel ERP Infor M3 ;
  • Garantir la fiabilité, la performance et la traçabilité des échanges de données ;
  • Mettre en œuvre les dispositifs de supervision et d'alerte ;

Participer à la construction de l'architecture Data du groupe

  • Concevoir et faire évoluer les modèles de données de référence ;
  • Participer à la définition de l'architecture Data et BI du système d'information ;
  • Garantir la cohérence, la qualité et la pérennité des données ;
  • Contribuer aux choix techniques liés aux échanges de données et à leur stockage ;

Contribuer à la gouvernance de la donnée avec notre Data Manager

  • Participer à l'amélioration continue de la qualité des données ;
  • Sensibiliser les métiers aux bonnes pratiques Data ;
  • Favoriser le développement d'une véritable culture de la donnée au sein du groupe...

Profil recherché

De formation supérieure Bac+5 en informatique, Data, Business Intelligence ou domaine équivalent, vous disposez d'une expérience réussie de 5 ans minimum dans un environnement Data, BI ou intégration applicative.

Vous Maîtrisez Plusieurs Des Domaines Suivants

  • Modélisation de données
  • SQL et bases de données relationnelles
  • Data Warehouse et reporting décisionnel
  • ETL et flux d'intégration, de préférence Blueway,
  • BIRST, Power BI ou outils décisionnels équivalents
  • Interfaces applicatives et API

Au-delà de vos compétences techniques, vous appréciez comprendre les enjeux métiers et traduire les besoins en solutions concrètes.

Rigoureux, curieux et force de proposition, vous aimez construire, faire évoluer et améliorer les solutions existantes.

Votre capacité à communiquer avec des interlocuteurs variés constituera un atout majeur pour réussir dans ce poste.

Pourquoi nous rejoindre ?

  • Participer à un programme de transformation majeur autour d'Infor M3 ;
  • Contribuer à la construction de l'architecture Data d'un groupe industriel en croissance ;
  • Rejoindre une DSI à taille humaine où les décisions se prennent rapidement et où chaque contribution a un impact concret ;
  • Évoluer dans un environnement mêlant industrie, innovation, projets et proximité avec les métiers ;
  • Intégrer un groupe familial solide, engagé et profondément ancré en Bretagne !

Ce Que Nous Proposons

  • Un poste à fort impact et à forte visibilité.
  • Des projets variés couvrant l'ensemble de la chaîne de valeur de l'entreprise.
  • Une équipe Solutions Métiers experte, autonome et collaborative.
  • Un environnement où la donnée devient un véritable levier stratégique.

N’hésitez plus et rejoignez-nous en adressant votre CV ! Chez Goûters Magiques, la diversité et l’inclusion sont notre richesse, ainsi toute candidature est la bienvenue !

Title: Gestor De Pacientes - Aveiro (M/F/D)
HTML:
✅ Sobre Nós

A Clínica Médis é uma rede de Clínicas Dentárias de referência composta por 14 clínicas estrategicamente localizadas em Lisboa, Setúbal,Portoe Aveiro.

Com uma equipa de mais de 160 Médicos Dentistas altamente qualificados, a Clínica Médis proporciona todas as áreas de atividade da medicina dentária em todas as suas unidades.

O compromisso com aqualidadeé evidenciado pelo seu impressionante NPS Global de 90, refletindo a satisfação dos pacientes.

Com mais de ****** clientes que já escolheram os seus serviços, a Clínica Médis continua a ser uma escolha de confiança para cuidados dentários de excelência.

Queres fazer parte da nossa equipa?

Se tens excelente capacidade de comunicação, forte orientação para a satisfação do cliente, és uma pessoa positiva e orientada para o sucesso, envia-nos a tua candidatura à função de Gestor/a de Pacientes.

Função:Gestor de Pacientes (M/F/D);

Local / Região:Aveiro;

Horário:Full-Time;

✅ O Que Esperamos De Ti

Acompanhamento personalizado de pacientes e potenciais pacientes;

Gestão das consultas de primeira visita;

Apresentação de planos de tratamento;

Acompanhamento dos contratos de financiamento;

Gestão de qualquer insatisfação por parte do paciente.

✅ Perfil Pretendido

Experiência em atendimento ao público;

Experiência em funções com uma componente comercial;

Experiência em medicina dentária;

Conhecimentos informáticos na ótica do utilizador;

Conhecimentos de inglês.

✅ Oferecemos

Remuneração ajustada à experiência;

Possibilidade de progressão de carreira dentro do grupo;

Prémios associados ao desempenho e resultados;

Seguro de saúde, de acordo com a política interna da empresa e em função da antiguidade;

Formação interna contínua e acompanhamento;

Outros benefícios associados à função;

Acesso a parcerias com benefícios e descontos em serviços de saúde nas Clínicas Médis.

✅ Horário

Horário detrabalhorotativo, de Segunda a Sexta entre as 8h-21h e Sábados entre as 8h30h-18h30.

Se consideras ter o perfil que procuramos, envia-nos o teu CV atualizado para ******, colocando no assunto "Gestor de Pacientes - Lisboa".

Title: Data Management Officer (DMO) - H/F
Description:
Description Du Poste

Data Management Officer (DMO) SQL // H/F

🎯 Votre rôle

Vous rejoignez le Crédit Agricole d’Île-de-France dans un contexte clé : la création du Data Management Office (DMO).

Ici, tout est à construire.

L’objectif est clair : unifier, structurer et fiabiliser la donnée pour en faire un socle commun utilisé par l’ensemble des métiers de la Caisse Régionale.

Votre rôle est central. Vous n’êtes pas là que pour produire de la donnée au sens technique, mais pour définir la manière dont elle doit exister, être organisée et utilisée.

Vous intervenez au croisement de la gouvernance, des métiers et de la data, dans un environnement bancaire exigeant, notamment au regard des enjeux réglementaires (BCBS 239).

Vous contribuez directement à faire de la donnée un actif stratégique, fiable et piloté.

🧩 Vos missions

Votre quotidien s’articule autour de plusieurs dimensions complémentaires :

🔹 Gouvernance de la donnée

Vous définissez et structurez les règles de gestion de la donnée. Vous posez un cadre clair sur les méthodes de calcul, les référentiels et les usages afin de garantir une cohérence globale à l’échelle de l’organisation.

🔹 Qualité & fiabilité des données

Vous analysez les données existantes, identifiez les écarts et mettez en place des règles de contrôle. Vous pilotez des indicateurs de qualité et inscrivez la donnée dans une logique d’amélioration continue.

🔹 Structuration & modélisation

À partir de données brutes, vous êtes capable de :


recréer des tables pertinentes
redéfinir des logiques de calcul
organiser et ordonnancer les flux
construire des référentiels métiers fiables


Vous travaillez dans des environnements SQL, avec une logique de structuration et non de développement pur.

La compétence en développement SQL est impérative pour votre réussite en poste

🔹 Pilotage de projets data

Vous intervenez comme un véritable chef de projet sur les sujets data :


compréhension des besoins métiers
cadrage des usages
priorisation des sujets
coordination avec les équipes techniques


Vous assurez la bonne mise en œuvre des évolutions dans une logique de time to market.

🔹 Interaction métiers & accompagnement

Vous êtes l’interlocuteur privilégié des métiers sur les sujets data. Vous les accompagnez dans la compréhension, l’utilisation et l’appropriation des données mises à disposition.

🔹 Conformité & réglementation

Vous intégrez les contraintes réglementaires dans vos travaux, notamment :


BCBS 239
exigences de traçabilité
qualité et auditabilité des données


Profil recherché

🧠 Votre profil

Vous disposez d’une formation supérieure en data, informatique ou équivalent, et d’une expérience significative dans des environnements data structurants.

Vous maîtrisez les enjeux de gouvernance de la donnée, qualité et référentiels.

Vous êtes un développeur SQL senior et vous souhaitez donner un coup d’accélérateur à votre carrière.

Vous comprenez les enjeux réglementaires liés à la data, notamment en environnement bancaire.

Vous savez faire le lien entre des enjeux métiers concrets et des problématiques de structuration de la donnée.

🧬 Votre posture

Ce poste s’adresse à une personne qui aime structurer, clarifier et construire.

Vous êtes rigoureux, capable de poser un cadre là où il n’existe pas encore, tout en avançant dans un environnement en construction.

Vous avez une vraie capacité à prendre du recul, à analyser des situations complexes et à proposer des solutions concrètes.

Vous êtes à l’aise dans la collaboration transverse et savez embarquer des interlocuteurs variés.

Vous aimez les environnements exigeants, où l’impact est réel et visible.

🌱 Pourquoi rejoindre ce projet

Vous participez à l'expansion d’un Data Management Office, avec un rôle structurant et visible.

Vous intervenez sur des sujets à fort enjeu stratégique pour la banque.

Vous contribuez directement à transformer la manière dont la donnée est utilisée, pilotée et valorisée.

C’est un terrain de jeu rare pour quelqu’un qui veut construire, structurer et avoir un impact durable.

Vous rejoignez le Crédit Agricole d’Île-de-France dans un contexte clé : la création du Data Management Office (DMO).

Ici, tout est à construire.

L’objectif est clair : unifier, structurer et fiabiliser la donnée pour en faire un socle commun utilisé par l’ensemble des métiers de la Caisse Régionale.

Votre rôle est central. Vous n’êtes pas là que pour produire de la donnée au sens technique, mais pour définir la manière dont elle doit exister, être organisée et utilisée.

Vous intervenez au croisement de la gouvernance, des métiers et de la data, dans un environnement bancaire exigeant, notamment au regard des enjeux réglementaires (BCBS 239).

Vous contribuez directement à faire de la donnée un actif stratégique, fiable et piloté.

🧩 Vos missions

Votre quotidien s’articule autour de plusieurs dimensions complémentaires :

🔹 Gouvernance de la donnée

Vous définissez et structurez les règles de gestion de la donnée. Vous posez un cadre clair sur les méthodes de calcul, les référentiels et les usages afin de garantir une cohérence globale à l’échelle de l’organisation.

🔹 Qualité & fiabilité des données

Vous analysez les données existantes, identifiez les écarts et mettez en place des règles de contrôle. Vous pilotez des indicateurs de qualité et inscrivez la donnée dans une logique d’amélioration continue.

🔹 Structuration & modélisation

À partir de données brutes, vous êtes capable de :

  • recréer des tables pertinentes
  • redéfinir des logiques de calcul
  • organiser et ordonnancer les flux
  • construire des référentiels métiers fiables

Vous travaillez dans des environnements SQL, avec une logique de structuration et non de développement pur.

La compétence en développement SQL est impérative pour votre réussite en poste

🔹 Pilotage de projets data

Vous intervenez comme un véritable chef de projet sur les sujets data :

  • compréhension des besoins métiers
  • cadrage des usages
  • priorisation des sujets
  • coordination avec les équipes techniques

Vous assurez la bonne mise en œuvre des évolutions dans une logique de time to market.

🔹 Interaction métiers & accompagnement

Vous êtes l’interlocuteur privilégié des métiers sur les sujets data. Vous les accompagnez dans la compréhension, l’utilisation et l’appropriation des données mises à disposition.

🔹 Conformité & réglementation

Vous intégrez les contraintes réglementaires dans vos travaux, notamment :

  • BCBS 239
  • exigences de traçabilité
  • qualité et auditabilité des données

Profil recherché

🧠 Votre profil

Vous disposez d’une formation supérieure en data, informatique ou équivalent, et d’une expérience significative dans des environnements data structurants.

Vous maîtrisez les enjeux de gouvernance de la donnée, qualité et référentiels.

Vous êtes un développeur SQL senior et vous souhaitez donner un coup d’accélérateur à votre carrière.

Vous comprenez les enjeux réglementaires liés à la data, notamment en environnement bancaire.

Vous savez faire le lien entre des enjeux métiers concrets et des problématiques de structuration de la donnée.

🧬 Votre posture

Ce poste s’adresse à une personne qui aime structurer, clarifier et construire.

Vous êtes rigoureux, capable de poser un cadre là où il n’existe pas encore, tout en avançant dans un environnement en construction.

Vous avez une vraie capacité à prendre du recul, à analyser des situations complexes et à proposer des solutions concrètes.

Vous êtes à l’aise dans la collaboration transverse et savez embarquer des interlocuteurs variés.

Vous aimez les environnements exigeants, où l’impact est réel et visible.

🌱 Pourquoi rejoindre ce projet

Vous participez à l'expansion d’un Data Management Office, avec un rôle structurant et visible.

Vous intervenez sur des sujets à fort enjeu stratégique pour la banque.

Vous contribuez directement à transformer la manière dont la donnée est utilisée, pilotée et valorisée.

C’est un terrain de jeu rare pour quelqu’un qui veut construire, structurer et avoir un impact durable.

Title: Plusieurs missions permanentes à pourvoir à Tours
Description:
Lors du week end de notre grand collecte d'automne, pendant quelques heures ou plus, vous pouvez accueillir les clients à l'entrée d'un supermarché, distribuer des flyers, donner des explications, recevoir les dons.

Vous pouvez aussi participer en devenant responsable d'une équipe de magasin, chargé du planning des bénévoles (liste des bénévoles de l'an précédent fournie), de la mise en place dans le magasin, et du relais avec la Banque Alimentaire, ce qui demande un peu de temps avant la collecte

Title: Atención al Cliente
Description:
Administrativa de Atención al Cliente y Planificación de Rutas

¿Te gusta el trato con las personas y disfrutas organizando tareas para que todo funcione de forma eficiente? Buscamos una persona organizada, resolutiva y con excelentes habilidades de comunicación para incorporarse a nuestro equipo de administración.

## ¿Cuáles serán tus funciones?


Atención telefónica y gestión de incidencias de clientes.
Planificación y programación de rutas de técnicos instaladores y de mantenimiento.
Coordinación diaria de avisos, reparaciones e instalaciones.
Organización y seguimiento de la agenda del servicio técnico.
Actualización y mantenimiento de las bases de datos de la empresa.
Apoyo administrativo a los diferentes departamentos.
Organización de calendarios y coordinación de entrevistas para los distintos procesos de selección de la empresa.


## ¿Qué buscamos?


Experiencia previa en puestos administrativos, atención al cliente (se valora mínimo 1 año de experiencia).
Excelente trato telefónico y orientación al cliente.
Capacidad para gestionar varias tareas de forma simultánea y priorizar el trabajo.
Persona organizada, resolutiva y con iniciativa.
Buen manejo de herramientas informáticas y programas de gestión.
Habilidades de comunicación y trabajo en equipo.


## ¿Qué ofrecemos?


Incorporación a una empresa consolidada y en constante crecimiento.
Estabilidad laboral.
Formación inicial y apoyo continuo.
Buen ambiente de trabajo y colaboración entre departamentos.
Posibilidades de desarrollo profesional dentro de la empresa.
L-V 8:30-14:00 / 16:00-19:00

Title: Addetto/a risorse umane junior per distribuzione

Per la nostra sede di Pavia, ricerchiamo un/una Addetto/a risorse umane da inserire nel team HR di una distribuzione organizzata con 12 punti vendita in Lombardia. Ti occuperai di attività concrete a contatto con operai, magazzinieri e personale di vendita.

diploma di scuola superiore
anche senza esperienza pregressa nel settore
buona conoscenza di Excel e strumenti informatici
precisione nella gestione dati e scadenze
ottime capacità relazionali e riservatezza

Offerta e benefit:


contratto a tempo determinato 12 mesi finalizzato a indeterminato
retribuzione lorda mensile indicativa: 1.450-1.750 € (CCNL Trasporti e Logistica)
Buoni pasto
Formazione continua
Orario flessibile
affiancamento diretto nel primo periodo


Orario full-time 40 ore su 5 giorni, dal lunedì al venerdì. Inserimento previsto entro 2 settimane.

Title: Data Governance Consultant (Data Steward)
Description:
Data Steward / Data Governance Consultant




We are seeking an experienced Data Steward / Data Domain Lead to drive data governance and ownership across a critical business domain. This is a governance-focused role, ideal for someone with expertise in data standards, lineage, metadata, and stakeholder management rather than data engineering or analytics.




Key Responsibilities

Define and manage the scope of a business data domain, including critical data elements, ownership, and stewardship.
Establish data definitions, standards, governance policies, and quality rules.
Identify and document business processes, applications, and data flows within the domain.
Analyse and remediate gaps in business, technical, application, and data lineage.
Lead workshops and governance meetings to align stakeholders and drive strategic decisions.
Gather business requirements across multiple departments to understand data needs.
Develop subject matter expertise in IT applications, infrastructure, and enterprise data.




Essential Skills

Proven experience as a Data Steward, Data Domain Lead, Data Governance Consultant, or similar governance-focused role.
Strong knowledge of data governance frameworks, data quality, metadata management, and data ownership.
Experience defining business data standards, critical data elements, and data definitions.
Strong understanding of business, application, technical, and data lineage.
Excellent stakeholder management and workshop facilitation skills.
Ability to translate business requirements into governance standards and policies.
Show more

Title: IT Specialist
Description:
Build something monumental for Healthcare!

At Tandem Health we’re reimagining healthcare by putting clinicians first. Our platform - designed by clinicians, for clinicians - is built on deep insight into real-world pain points, with intuitive medical notes and workflows that truly support patient care.

We’re a fast-scaling health-tech company backed by top investors and expanding globally. We move fast, stay curious, and believe building something that matters starts with an extraordinary team. If you're passionate about impact and innovation, we'd love to meet you!

About The Role

You’re the kind of person who notices when things are inefficient, insecure, or manually repetitive and then fixes them before anyone has to ask.

At Tandem, we’re scaling quickly across teams and offices, and internal IT is becoming increasingly important to how we operate. We’re looking for a IT Specialist who enjoys owning the day-to-day while also improving the bigger picture over time. Someone who likes helping people, but who also naturally thinks about automation, scalability, and security.

In this role, you’ll be responsible for making sure our internal systems and employee IT experience work smoothly as we grow. That means everything from onboarding new hires and managing devices to improving identity management, automating workflows, and driving internal IT projects forward.

You’ll be part of our security team, while working closely with our IT Lead and stakeholders across the company. We’re looking for someone pragmatic and operational who can balance speed, security, and usability in a fast-moving environment.

This role is ideal for someone who enjoys taking ownership, building structure where needed, and operating with a high degree of autonomy.

What You'll Do


Own and improve the day-to-day internal IT operations across the company
Support onboarding/offboarding, hardware management, access management, and employee support
Manage and improve endpoint management across both macOS and Windows environments
Help build scalable identity and access management processes, including SSO and device management
Drive automation of repetitive workflows and operational tasks
Configure, maintain, and improve internal systems and tooling
Work closely with stakeholders across the company to ensure a smooth and secure IT experience
Participate in internal IT and security-related projects
Help ensure systems and applications remain secure, updated, and compliant
Identify operational bottlenecks and proactively improve how we work internally


What You Bring


Experience working in IT, ideally in a fast-moving tech company or startup environment
Experience working with MDM / endpoint management platforms such as Kandji, Intune, Jamf, or similar
Strong understanding of both macOS and Windows environments
Experience managing users and access at scale
Familiarity with identity providers and SSO solutions such as Okta, Entra ID, or Google Workspace
A strong ownership mentality, you naturally take initiative and drive things forward
A proactive mindset around automation and operational improvements
Experience implementing or maintaining internal IT systems and tooling
Good understanding of IT security fundamentals and secure device/application management
Strong communication skills and ability to collaborate across different teams and stakeholders
A service-minded and low-prestige approach — you enjoy helping people and being close to the business


Bonus Points


Experience with tools such as Kandji, Okta, CrowdStrike, 1Password, or Notion
Experience leading smaller technical projects end-to-end
Experience working in high-growth or scale-up environments
Some scripting or automation experience in Bash, PowerShell, Python, or similar


Location

We believe the best ideas happen when we’re together. This role is based in our vibrant HQ in the Epicenter building in the heart of Stockholm where you will work primarily from our office to collaborate, connect, and build our culture.

How To Apply

We adopt a continuous selection process, so please make sure to apply with your CV in English.

Our Interview Process Consists Of 5 Stages


Screening interview with Talent Acquisition
Interview with IT Lead
Interview with Head of Security
Join us in the Stockholm office for a lunch and a case interview to experience our culture firsthand, collaborate with our team, and see how you work in action.
Follow-up interview with our CTO & Co-Founder


Benefits


Competitive salary & company stock options
30 days/year of paid vacation
Pension
5,000 SEK wellness allowance (friskvårdsbidrag)
Parental top up
Social and team-building activities (off-sites, after works, winter/summer parties)
An opportunity to make a real positive impact in the world of healthcare
Work with some of the best minds in AI, healthcare, and engineering.


We review our benefits packages on a regular basis and might modify our benefits from time to time.

Culture at Tandem

At Tandem, we move fast, think big, and take ownership. We're a high-performing, diverse team with a shared drive to change the future of healthcare - and we’re just getting started.

Our culture is built on action, ambition, and learning. You'll be trusted to take the lead, challenge yourself, and make an impact from day one. We believe real growth happens when you're stretched, supported, and surrounded by smart, passionate teammates who want to win together.

Even though we’re spread across countries, we come together often in Sweden for team meetings, social events, and offsites - blending global reach with real human connection.

We hire for talent, potential, and attitude - valuing different backgrounds and fresh perspectives. Great ideas come from everywhere, and we’re building a team that reflects the world we want to change.

Tandem handles sensitive patient data and will conduct a background check before hiring any candidate.

Title: Junior Data Analyst
Description:
Description

We are seeking a motivated and detail-oriented Junior Data Analyst to join our dynamic team. This entry-level position offers an excellent opportunity for individuals who are passionate about data and eager to develop their analytical skills in a professional environment. As a Junior Data Analyst, you will collaborate with various departments to collect, process, and analyze data to support business decision-making. You will work under the guidance of senior analysts and managers to transform raw data into meaningful insights that drive strategic initiatives. The ideal candidate should have a strong foundation in data analysis techniques, be proficient with data tools, and possess a keen eye for accuracy and problem-solving. In this role, you will gain hands-on experience with data visualization, reporting, and database management, contributing directly to the company’s growth and operational efficiency. This position is perfect for someone looking to kick-start their career in data analytics and grow their expertise in a supportive and innovative workplace. Join us and be part of a team that values curiosity, collaboration, and continuous learning, all while making a tangible impact through data-driven insights.

Responsibilities


Collect, clean, and validate data from various sources to ensure accuracy and completeness.
Assist in the development and maintenance of dashboards and reports for internal stakeholders.
Conduct basic statistical analysis to identify trends, patterns, and insights.
Collaborate with cross-functional teams to understand data requirements and provide analytical support.
Prepare visualizations and presentations to communicate findings effectively to non-technical audiences.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Responsibilities

Kforce has a client in need of a Data Analyst in Boston, MA. Responsibilities:


Manage and maintain appeal tracking data, ensuring accuracy, consistency, and reliability across systems
Support development and optimization of database queries for reporting and analysis
Execute targeted audience segmentation to support acquisition, retention, and upgrade initiatives
Coordinate use of constituent data across multiple outreach channels to ensure alignment in campaign delivery


Requirements


Advanced Excel skills, including formulas and pivot tables
High attention to detail with strong process accuracy
Analytical thinker with ability to interpret data, identify trends, and support decisions


The pay range is the lowest to highest compensation we reasonably in good faith believe we would pay at posting for this role. We may ultimately pay more or less than this range. This range may be modified in the future.

We offer comprehensive benefits including medical/dental/vision insurance, HSA, FSA, 401(k), and life, disability & ADD insurance to eligible employees. Salaried personnel receive paid time off. Title: Data Analyst Internship
Description:
About ConnectPrep: Our goal is to redefine "tutoring" - to use data, insight, and our extensive teaching experience find solutions for our students. ConnectPrep is about connecting - connecting students, parents, educators, and the community. We have bold ideas and an eye towards the future.

The Opportunity: We are offering a Data Analyst Internship for qualified candidates. This is a cross-disciplinary role, both instructional and technical. You'll engage with our team of educators to validate and iterate the models that drive our analytics. Our Data Analysts develop an understanding of our algorithms, the technologies and platforms that support our products, and our proprietary educational content. This knowledge will then be synthesized into actionable tests to build modules that will assist in identifying the academic needs of each individual student. You will engage with team members that think outside of the box, with the goal of improving education through the creation of a data infrastructure and insightful analytics.

Requirements

What we are looking for:


Core skills
You enjoy creative problem solving and working with messy, real-world data.
You have strong math/quantitative skills and excellent attention to detail.
You are curious about how data can improve learning and student outcomes.
Academic & technical background
You are studying (or have studied) Applied Mathematics, Statistics, Computer Science, Data Science, or a related field.
You have some coding experience and a strong interest in technology and AI.
You have experience with at least one of the following:
SQL, Python data tools (Pandas, NumPy, or similar)
Building simple data pipelines or analysis scripts in Python
Excel & analysis skills
You are comfortable doing serious work in Excel, including several of the following:
Advanced formulas and functions
Tables and formatting
Conditional formatting
Advanced charting and visualizations
Pivot tables and pivot reporting
Using Excel productively for analysis, not just basic spreadsheets
Data tables, simulations, and Solver
(Bonus) VBA and macros
AI, data, and analytics work you will support
Work with AI tools and workflows to support data-driven decision-making
Perform data mining and cleaning from multiple sources (web, internal trackers, marketing platforms)
Support projects involving AI and chatbot development for ConnectPrep's programs and website
Conduct SEO and keyword research to identify growth opportunities and track performance
Analyze website analytics (traffic, engagement, conversions) and prepare simple dashboards or summary reports
Assist with data analysis for marketing campaigns, content performance, and business operations
Help test, document, and iterate on new data and AI workflows as they are developed
Nice to have
Prior internship or project experience in data analysis, business intelligence, or marketing analytics
Familiarity with web analytics and marketing tools (for example, Google Analytics, GA4, Search Console, Looker Studio, or similar platforms)
Experience visualizing data in tools such as Tableau, Power BI, or Data Studio
Experience working with SEO tools or keyword platforms
Experience collaborating in remote or hybrid teams (Slack, Zoom, project management boards)


Benefits

Why join ConnectPrep?


Get in on the ground floor of an exciting new company, with the potential to help shape our direction
Be involved with a team that has bold ideas, with the goal of reinventing education across the globe
Gain valuable experience, meet technology innovators, and learn about the business side of our company


Sound Interesting?

Please submit your resume and letter of interest at careers@connectprep.com.

All applicants must be a U.S. Citizen or National of the U.S., an alien lawfully admitted for permanent residence, or an alien authorized to work in the U.S. for this employer. ConnectPrep is an equal opportunity employer EEO/M/F/D/V.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
About The Role

Curely is looking for a Medical Receptionist to be the first point of contact for our patients. In this role, you’ll ensure a smooth check-in process, manage appointment scheduling, and provide administrative support to our medical team. If you have strong communication skills, a patient-friendly attitude, and a passion for healthcare, we’d love to hear from you!

What You'll Do


Greet and check in patients with professionalism and warmth.
Schedule, confirm, and manage patient appointments efficiently.
Handle phone calls, emails, and patient inquiries.
Maintain patient records and ensure all information is up to date.
Verify insurance details and process billing transactions.
Coordinate with doctors, nurses, and other staff for seamless patient care.
Uphold confidentiality and comply with HIPAA regulations.
Assist with other front desk and clerical duties as needed.


What We’re Looking For


High school diploma or equivalent (Associate’s degree preferred).
1-2 years of experience in a receptionist or administrative role (medical setting preferred).
Strong verbal and written communication skills.
Proficiency in scheduling software and Microsoft Office Suite.
Ability to multitask and work efficiently in a fast-paced environment.
Friendly, patient-focused attitude with excellent customer service skills.
Knowledge of medical terminology and insurance processes is a plus.


Bonus Skills & Experience


Bilingual in English and Spanish (or other languages) is a plus.
Experience using electronic health record (EHR) systems.
Previous work in a hospital or clinic setting.


Why Join Curely?


Competitive Salary – Fair and rewarding compensation.
Health & Dental Insurance – Comprehensive coverage for you and your family.
Paid Time Off & Holidays – Enjoy a work-life balance.
Career Growth Opportunities – Training and promotion possibilities.
Supportive Team Culture – Work in a collaborative and respectful environment.
Modern & Innovative Workplace – Utilize cutting-edge healthcare technology.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
We are looking to hire a data analyst to join our data team. You will take responsibility for managing our master data set, developing reports, and troubleshooting data issues. To do well in this role you need a very fine eye for detail, experience as a data analyst, and a deep understanding of the popular data analysis tools and databases. Data Analyst Responsibilities: Managing master data, including creation, updates, and deletion. Managing users and user roles. Provide quality assurance of imported data, working with quality assurance analysts if necessary. Commissioning and decommissioning of data sets. Processing confidential data and information according to guidelines. Helping develop reports and analyses. Managing and designing the reporting environment, including data sources, security, and metadata. Supporting the data warehouse in identifying and revising reporting requirements. Supporting initiatives for data integrity and normalization. Assessing tests and implementing new or upgraded software and assisting with strategic decisions on new systems. Generating reports from single or multiple systems. Troubleshooting the reporting database environment and reports. Evaluating changes and updates to source production systems. Training end-users on new reports and dashboards. Providing technical expertise in data storage structures, data mining, and data cleansing. Data Analyst Requirements: Bachelor s degree from an accredited university or college in computer science. Work experience as a data analyst or in a related field. Ability to work with stakeholders to assess potential risks. Ability to analyze existing tools and databases and provide software solution recommendations. Ability to translate business requirements into nontechnical, lay terms. High-level experience in methodologies and processes for managing large-scale databases. Demonstrated experience in handling large data sets and relational databases. Understanding of addressing and metadata standards. High-level written and verbal communication skills.
